Mike & Jonny · Wheel research lab

The DCA Wheel: Research Dashboard

An autonomous research system: Claude agents hunt the whole market and research 4 years of filings; a deterministic engine computes every score, verdict, and exit - agents never grade their own homework. The strategy: sell cash-secured puts only on businesses with a durable competitive advantage, so an assignment is a purchase we'd celebrate rather than a loss, and rank every trade by how fast it converts capital into income.

Generated 2026-07-08 02:41Z · screens v0.3.1 · research mode - all positions are paper · every number below is computed server-side from agent-researched, source-cited inputs
Status1 tradeable play on the board - CTAS 2026-08-07 $170p · 20.1%/yr · 0 open paper positions · P&L $0 · research mode, book opens after operator review
Stage 1 · Quality
The DCAP gate

Market-wide screen (price>$20, mcap>$10B, vol>1M), then the 11-parameter DCAP scorer on 4 years of filings. ≥60% ⇒ assignment would be a gift.

61 gifts / 189 assessed (32%)
Stage 2 · Entry
Options screen

30-45 DTE cash-secured puts: |Δ|≤0.3, premium yield>1.5%, IV rank>30 (realized-vol proxy).

1 of 127 recorded plays pass
Stage 3 · Ranking
Capital efficiency

(premium ÷ strike×100) ÷ days - yield per dollar per day, annualized. Tiebreaks: IV rank, then spread. Sizing never bends: ≤25% of capital per name.

best: CTAS 2026-08-07 $170p · 20.1%/yr
Stage 4 · Exit
The 50% profit rule

Buy back at 50% of max profit - never hold to expiration. Defensive stop at −8% on the underlying; time stop at 21 DTE.

0 open · 0 closed · P&L $0

Sentiment: Positive/celebratable. Q1 2026: net income $2.0B, FCF $1.5B, raised production guidance; Encino acquisition exceeding synergy targets and cutting well costs, adding low-decline Utica inventory. Analyst consensus PT ~$139 (Jefferies $148, upgraded). Jefferies sees $150M annual synergies by 2026; Raymond James ~$2.3B EBITDA uplift by 2027. Main watch-item: EBITDA declining with lower oil/gas realizations (13.5B->10.8B over window) and FY25 leverage step-up from Encino, but balance sheet remains conservative.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Oil & gas E&P (independent), FY ends Dec. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (OI 9966/9603/8082/6385 + D&A 3542/3492/4108/4461, both cited/SEC-confirmed). Inventory = materials & supplies (real, E&P carries it). Goodwill = 0 all years (no us-gaap:Goodwill XBRL concept filed, like COP) -> GW param scores 0. No preferred stock; common only. Treasury stock rising 78->6158 on buybacks. FY2025 step-change: LTD jumped 4220->7909 and PP&E 34212->42341 on the ~$5.6B Encino Energy (Utica) acquisition closed 2025; PP&E rise +23.8% stays under the 60% spike gate; LTD/EBITDA still 0.73. RE growth avg ~17.3% (22.5/19.0/10.5). SE positive all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies (17 max).

Sentiment: Fundamentals strong: FY2026 Q2 organic revenue +8.6%, FY2026 guidance raised to ~$11.15-11.22B. Analyst tone neutral (7 Buy / 12 Hold / 2 Sell, median PT ~$215) with June 2026 target trims (Truist 255→225, BofA 215→200) on macro headwinds, not execution. Key watch item: pending UniFirst acquisition would take North American market share to ~50% with $375M synergies, but FTC issued a Second Request - regulatory review extends into H2 2026. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable: dominant route-density moat, decades of dividend growth, heavy buybacks.
research notes - methodology & caveats
All figures $ millions, fiscal years ending May 31 (FY2022→FY2025, oldest→newest); FY2026 (ended 2026-05-31) 10-K not yet filed as of 2026-07-06. EBITDA = SEC-tagged operating income + cash-flow D&A (e.g. FY25: 2,359.7 + 494.2 = 2,853.9); stockanalysis.com independent EBITDA (1,987/2,222/2,526/2,854) matches within <1% (uses slightly different D&A mid-years). PPE is the 10-K "Property and equipment, net" line per SEC XBRL; stockanalysis shows ~$200M higher because it folds in operating-lease right-of-use assets. Total liabilities not XBRL-tagged directly; computed as total assets − stockholders' equity, matches stockanalysis to the million. Inventory dipped FY2024 (506.6→410.2) - real inventory reduction, not a data gap. No R&D line on income statement (uniform rental/facility services). Preferred stock authorized but zero outstanding all 4 years. Large and growing treasury stock ($7.3B→$9.8B) - consistent buyback history.

Sentiment: Bullish/celebratable. Consensus Buy (~24 of 25 analysts Buy/Strong Buy; ~$69 avg PT, ~30% upside), Moody's outlook raised to positive on ~$8B post-Equitrans debt reduction, vertical integration (~90% wellhead-to-market control via MVP) and AI/data-center in-basin gas demand (Shippingport, Homer City, MVP Boost) as tailwinds. Q1-2026 beat (EPS $2.33, rev $3.38B). Risk: commodity-price/rate sensitivity (Truist cut PT), inherent natural-gas volatility.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Natural-gas E&P (Appalachia, largest US producer). All balance-sheet items reconciled exactly against SEC XBRL companyconcept (CIK 0000033213); income/cash-flow lines from stockanalysis and cross-checked via operating income + cash-flow D&A (EBITDA = OI+D&A: FY22 2718+1666, FY23 2314+1732, FY24 685+2162, FY25 3250+2600 = 4384/4047/2848/5850). NCI TRAP: after the July-2024 Equitrans Midstream acquisition EQT consolidates entities with a large noncontrolling interest ($40.9M/$7.6M/$3680.5M/$3607.5M); I used PARENT-ONLY EQT stockholders' equity (11172.5/14773.2/20597.6/23752.7), NOT total equity incl NCI (11213/14781/24278/27360). This choice matters for RSH: latest EBITDA/parent-SE = 5850/23752.7 = 24.6% (clears 23%); on total equity it would be 21.4% (fail). INVENTORY: E&P carries no material inventory line (none in XBRL InventoryNet) - set 0s, so I&NE inventory-rise point is structurally unavailable. GOODWILL first appears FY2024 from Equitrans ($2079M -> $2062M FY25), so it is not steadily increasing. TREASURY: none on the books (XBRL TreasuryStockValue absent/0) despite buyback activity -> TS flag absent. MERGER-WINDOW CAVEAT: the Equitrans mega-deal is a step-change inside this window (assets $25B->$40B, PPE +38% YoY FY24, +$3.68B NCI, +goodwill, +debt then paid down); rescore after the window rolls past it. RE point-of-note: the 4-yr retained-earnings CAGR is huge (~56%/yr) but leans on a low FY2022 base ($1284M, just recovered from FY2021 deficit of -$94M) and the FY22->23 +109% jump; if the operator strips that low-base artifact the score drops ~3 pts toward the 60% line. Fiscal year = calendar (Dec 31).

Sentiment: Strongly positive. FY2025 was a record: revenue topped $400B for the first time (+18% to $113.8B in Q4), Google Cloud +48% to $17.7B, operating margin 31.6%, net income +30%. Gemini 3 launch cited as a major milestone; first-party models process >10B tokens/min. Analyst consensus Strong Buy (as of early Jul 2026): ~53 Strong Buy / 41 Buy / 7 Hold / 0 Sell, avg price target ~$428 vs ~$342 spot (~25% implied upside), high $515. Overhangs to flag for the operator: ongoing DOJ antitrust remedies (search + ad-tech) and heavy AI-capex ramp (FY25 capex $91B vs $52B FY24) pressuring free cash flow, plus a $2.1B Waymo employee-comp charge in Q4. None of these dent the DCAP fundamentals; assignment here would be owning the dominant internet/AI franchise at a discount - highly celebrate-able.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Calendar FY (Dec 31). All figures from Alphabet primary filings/earnings releases; PP&E uses the SEC 'Property and equipment, net' line (112,668/134,345/171,036/246,597), NOT aggregator numbers that fold in operating-lease ROU assets (stockanalysis showed 127,049/148,436/184,624/261,818 = ROU-inflated). EBITDA = income from operations + D&A (cash-flow 'Depreciation of property and equipment'; no separate intangible-amort line): FY22 74,842+13,475; FY23 84,293+11,946; FY24 112,390+15,311; FY25 129,039+21,136 - FY24/25 EBITDA cross-checks stockanalysis exactly. Inventory = null: Alphabet does not disclose a separate inventory line (device inventory immaterial, in 'other current assets'), so the I&NE inventory-rise leg can't be verified. Treasury_stock_abs = 0 all years: Alphabet RETIRES repurchased shares (no treasury-stock line on the balance sheet; ~$60B/yr buybacks charged to APIC/retained earnings) - this costs the TS point despite a heavy buyback history. LTD FY25 jumps to 46,547 from Nov-2025 $24.8B senior-note issuance (+ prior issues); doesn't affect LTD scoring (LTD/EBITDA 0.31, LTD/assets 0.08). Preferred: 100 shares authorized, 0 issued/outstanding. RE growth is genuine (not a sign-flip); even stripping the FY25 $24.6B unrealized gain on equity securities from net income, RE avg YoY growth stays >17%. SE positive all 4 years, so TSA-DSER applies (max 17).

MA - Mastercard Inc GIFT

Finance - payments network · price $533.20 · mcap $471.1B · vol 4,316,684 · PE 31.2 · PB 71.2 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 14/17 (82%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.35 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.9 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.53 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)2/3retained earnings grew 16.6%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 258.7% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Consensus Strong Buy - ~35 Buy / 3 Hold / 0 Sell, avg PT ~$647-665. Stock down ~12.6% YTD 2026 on UK FCA regulatory probe overhang + macro consumer-spend sensitivity; trades ~24x fwd P/E vs 30-32x historical average, so relative value. Revenue forecast ~10%/yr next 3 years; positives include cross-border volume, BVNK/stablecoin acquisition positioning for on-chain payments. A wide-moat duopoly network; assignment at a discounted strike would be celebrated. Watch: outcome of UK FCA investigation.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Mastercard, global payments network (Dec FY-end). No inventory (0s) - a network, not a merchant; carries no goods. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (12,264+750; 14,008+799; 15,582+897; 18,897+1,143), cross-checked vs bullfincher (12.95/15.01/16.8/20.44B - same rising trend). EP expected to fail: current liabilities carry large "settlement due to customers" + accrued litigation, so EBITDA<CL each year - the payments-network float floor, same structural pattern as GPN/FISV/V. Key structural note: SE stays POSITIVE (thin, ~$6-8B) despite ~$50-83B treasury from decades of buybacks, so TSA-DSER is NOT excused (max=17); and because SE+|treasury| is huge, TSA-DSER passes fully (avg TL/(SE+treas)~0.53). Thin equity inflates RSH (latest EBITDA/SE ~2.6x). RE growth is genuine retained earnings (net income less dividends/buybacks) at ~16.6%/yr - no divestiture artifact - sitting just under the 17% top tier.

Sentiment: Overwhelmingly bullish Street: ~57 Buy / 6 Hold / 0 Sell, consensus target ~$828 (~40% upside). The one live debate is AI capex - Meta raised FY2026 capex guide to $125-145B (nearly double FY2025's ~$72B) on Zuckerberg's 'superintelligence' push; the Q1 2026 print sent shares down ~6-7% after-hours on ROI skepticism. But Deutsche Bank and Morgan Stanley recently flipped their framing from 'cash-burning black hole' to 'monetization engine.' Core ad business remains a cash machine (OCF $115.8B FY25, +27% RTA). Nothing here makes assignment un-celebratable - the risk is capex-driven FCF compression, not franchise erosion.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec 31. No inventory line (advertising/software platform) - genuine 0s. PP&E = as-reported 'property and equipment, net'; I stripped operating-lease ROU (FY22-25: 12,673/13,294/14,922/20,404 per EDGAR XBRL) from stockanalysis's ROU-inclusive PP&E to avoid ROU inflation - anchors FY2022=79,518 (exact known) and FY2025=176,400 (matches search) confirm the strip. Meta RETIRES repurchased shares, so no treasury-stock line -> TS flag 'absent' costs the 1 TS point and zeroes treasury_stock_abs (buyback history is real, just not parked in treasury). Huge R&D line (~$57B FY25) kills the I&NE R&D sub-point. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (implied D&A FY22-25: 8,686/11,178/15,498/18,616). RE and operating income cross-checked against Meta's official FY2025 press release/10-K.

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.14 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.3 years of EBITDA (cap 4)

Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 19.0%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 32.2% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Constructive/strong. RMD is the dominant global CPAP/sleep-apnea franchise (duopoly with Philips, whose 2021-23 recall handed ResMed share). FY2025 was a record year - revenue and margins up, operating income +28% to $1.69B, gross margins expanding on lower freight/component costs. Main multi-year overhang has been GLP-1 (Ozempic/Zepbound) fears that weight-loss drugs shrink the sleep-apnea pool; emerging data instead shows GLP-1 users are diagnosed/treated MORE, and Zepbound's OSA label is read as a funnel, not a threat - stock recovered strongly through 2024-25. Balance sheet de-levered rapidly post-MEDIFOX DAN. Nothing here makes assignment un-celebratable; this is a high-quality compounder.
research notes - methodology & caveats
ResMed - sleep-apnea/respiratory medical devices + SaaS (Brightree/MEDIFOX DAN); FY ends June 30, so FY2025 = year ended 2025-06-30. Carries real inventory (devices/masks). EBITDA = income from operations + cash-flow D&A (OI 1000/1132/1320/1685 + D&A 194/198/216/236). I&NE inventory fails steady-rise: FY24 inventory (822) dipped ~18% below FY23 (998) as the FY22-23 pandemic-era component-shortage inventory build unwound. R&D expense line present (253/288/308/331), so I&NE second point also off. LTD spike FY23 (1431) = MEDIFOX DAN acquisition financing, since paid down to 658 by FY25; goodwill step FY22→FY23 (1936→2770) same deal + Ectosense. FY22 NOCF depressed (351) by that inventory build. Net PP&E steady and modest (~630→718); may include some ROU but not spike-distorting. Preferred: none outstanding. Treasury stock present and rising (active buybacks + dividends).

Sentiment: Stock −30% YTD 2026, ~44% below 52w high, trading at ~12.6x PE / 8.7x trailing FCF - a historic low - on AI-disruption fear (Midjourney/Sora commoditizing creative tools) plus CEO Shantanu Narayen's announced departure. The numbers show no disruption yet: record FY2025 revenue $23.8B (+10.5%), 89% gross margins, record $10B operating cash flow. HSBC upgraded to Buy ($308 target) on July 2; consensus is Hold (22 analysts). Key catalyst: Firefly ARR pace at September earnings. Assignment case: creative-suite monopoly at a decade-low multiple - celebratable if you believe the moat survives generative AI; the drawdown is a sentiment bet, not a balance-sheet one.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fiscal year ends late Nov/early Dec (FY2022 ended 2022-12-02, FY2025 ended 2025-11-28). Inventory zeros are genuine - pure software/SaaS, no inventory line in any 10-K balance sheet. EBITDA = operating income + D&A from the 10-K cash-flow statement (6,098+856; 6,650+872; 6,741+857; 8,706+818), matching stockanalysis.com's EBITDA exactly. PPE is net property & equipment per SEC XBRL (excludes operating-lease ROU assets; stockanalysis shows ~2.2-2.4B because it bundles ROU). R&D expense present and large ($4,294M FY2025, 18% of revenue). Preferred: 2M shares authorized, none issued. Treasury stock massive and growing ($48.8B FY2025) - record buybacks funded partly by FY2024/FY2025 note issuances (LTD $3.6B → $6.2B). Current liabilities carry ~$6B+ deferred revenue (SaaS billing structure) - expect the EP parameter to fail in early years on that, a structural artifact like ROP's, not weak earning power.

Sentiment: Constructive-to-mixed. Cboe reported RECORD June 2026 trading volumes (options ADV 23.0M contracts; index options quarterly-record 6.2M ADV; single-day record 33.4M on Jun 5) and RAISED 2026 organic net-revenue growth guidance to low-double-digit/mid-teens (from mid-single-digit). Analyst consensus is Hold (17 analysts), 12-mo avg PT ~$319.79 (~30% above recent ~$245); Barclays Overweight on index-options strength, UBS Neutral $335, Erste downgraded to Hold on slowing-momentum concerns. Q2 2026 earnings due Jul 31 (lands just before the 30-45 DTE window from today). Assignment here would be celebrated: dominant proprietary-index-options franchise (SPX/VIX), debt-light, record volumes - a boring-but-dominant compounder.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Cboe Global Markets = options/derivatives exchange operator (Dec fiscal year-end). No inventory line - services business, so inventory = 0s (genuine, not missing). EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A each year (SEC-confirmed op inc 489.6/1057.9/1098.4/1467.1 + D&A 166.8/158/133/122.4). FY2022 EBITDA is depressed by a ~$460M goodwill impairment (Cboe Digital wind-down) baked into operating income - kept it in for conservatism; it only drags EP/I&NE, not the verdict. Watch item: FY2025 current liabilities jump to $2,362M (from $1,395M) and current assets to $4,421M largely reflect Cboe Clear Europe clearing-member margin/deposit growth - a pass-through inflation of the balance sheet (same structural texture as CME, but Cboe is far more asset-light: $9.3B total assets vs CME's trillions). This is why EP fails (EBITDA < current liabilities in FY2022 impairment year and FY2025 clearing-inflated year). Treasury stock present but the balance shrank to ~$1.5M (they retire/reissue), so TS flag = present on buyback history but treasury_abs series is tiny. Preferred: none outstanding.

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 20.9% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.18 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.42 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 9.1%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 34.8% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Constructive/celebratable. Consensus Buy (21 analysts: ~71% Buy/Strong-Buy, 24% Hold, 5% Sell), avg PT ~$117 vs price ~$105; some fair-value estimates $135-140. Marathon Oil integration tracking well - ~$2B cost synergies targeted by 2026, Q1-2026 adjusted EPS beat consensus ~12%. Strong capital return: $0.84 quarterly dividend (~3.2% yield, 10-15% growth guided if oil >$75) plus buyback. 2026 production guided +0-2%. Main risk is oil-price cyclicality (EBITDA/OCF fell from the 2022 commodity peak) and volatility digesting the Marathon deal - but low leverage (LTD/assets ~0.18), no goodwill, and disciplined balance sheet make assignment genuinely desirable.
research notes - methodology & caveats
ConocoPhillips (E&P oil & gas), FY = calendar year; latest 10-K is FY2025 (filed Feb 2026). EBITDA = operating income + D&A per spec's primary definition (stockanalysis; op-income+D&A reconciles exactly, D&A SEC-confirmed via cash flow). Note macrotrends reports a higher EBITDA (pretax+interest+D&A incl. equity earnings: ~36.9/26.0/24.7B for FY22-24) - same declining shape, and the construction choice changes no scoring outcome. GOODWILL genuinely = 0: COP carries no goodwill line - SEC XBRL has no Goodwill tag any year, and the FY2024 10-K (which already consolidates the Nov-2024 Marathon Oil acquisition) contains zero occurrences of 'goodwill' (E&P purchase accounting pushes excess into PP&E/reserves). So GW parameter correctly scores 0. PP&E FY2024 jump (70,044->94,356, +34.7%) is the Marathon acquisition - under the 60% spike threshold. Retained earnings and net operating cash flow independently confirmed against SEC XBRL companyconcept API (exact match). No R&D expense line (exploration expense instead). No preferred stock. Treasury stock present and growing ($60B->$76B, active buyback). SE positive all 4 years so TSA-DSER applies (max stays 17).

Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.25 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)2/3retained earnings grew 15.0%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)

Sentiment: Buy-lean/Hold consensus (~mixed: 25% strong buy, 25% buy, 50% hold, 0% sell as of Jun-2026); avg PT ~$93 (~24% upside from ~$75). Q1 2026 revenue and adjusted operating income missed, triggering a sharp negative share reaction; FY2026 guidance is soft (low-single-digit organic, mid-single with M&A), and forecast earnings growth ~6.6% trails the specialty-industrial-machinery peer average ~18%. No overhang that makes ownership un-celebratable - a wide-moat, debt-free compounder trading on a growth-deceleration/miss drawdown; entry-on-weakness setup.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Graco Inc (NYSE:GGG) - fluid-handling equipment maker, FY ends late December (FY2025 = period ended 2026-12-26 per 10-K, i.e. Dec-2025 fiscal). EBITDA built as operating income + cash-flow D&A (FY22-25 OI 572.7/646.84/570.1/624.8 + D&A 66.0/74.32/86.75/107.43). Essentially debt-free: LTD $75M FY22 then $0; only short-term notes payable (~$23-26M) remain, correctly NOT counted as LTD. NO treasury-stock line on the balance sheet - Graco RETIRES repurchased shares (excess of cost over par charged to retained earnings), so treasury flag 'absent' and treasury_stock_abs=0. This retirement also drove the FY2025 retained-earnings DIP (1509->1457): FY25 net income $522M offset by $185M dividends + $423M buybacks retired. R&D ('product development') is a distinct income-statement line in all 4 years (~$80-87M), so no I&NE R&D point. Inventory DECLINING (476->401), not a steady rise. High-margin, low-current-liability profile: EBITDA exceeds current liabilities all 4 years (EP passes, unlike industrial working-cap floors like ETN/CMI/DOV). Goodwill rising on acquisitions. Cash ~$712M, ~$773M undrawn credit.

Sentiment: Strongly positive. FY2025 was a record year: EBITDA $3.62B (+27% YoY), operating income $2.95B, OCF $3.03B, all four series rising every year. Analyst consensus Strong Buy (44 analysts, 24 Buy/9 Hold/2 Sell), median PT ~$615. Q1-2026 strength drove management to raise 2026 da Vinci procedure-growth guidance to 14-16%; da Vinci 5 ramp (232 of 431 placements in Q1, ~11% higher utilization than Gen-4) and Ion driving re-acceleration; da Vinci 5 newly cleared for select US cardiac procedures. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable - a debt-free, dominant-moat medtech compounder we would be delighted to own at a discount.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec. Intuitive Surgical (da Vinci surgical robots) - carries real inventory (systems/instruments). Debt-free: no long-term debt tag exists in SEC XBRL, so LTD=0 all 4 years. Shareholders_equity is SEC parent-only StockholdersEquity (excludes small NCI) - avoids the parent-SE/NCI trap; stockanalysis's SE (~11113 FY22) includes NCI. EBITDA built as operating income + D&A per rubric (SEC OperatingIncomeLoss + cash-flow D&A); matches stockanalysis's reported EBITDA to the decimal. treasury_stock_abs=0: ISRG constructively RETIRES repurchased shares (no TreasuryStock tag in XBRL), so no treasury line and TS flag 'absent'. WATCH: retained earnings grew only $6803.3->$7011.8 in FY2025 (+3.1%) vs ~$2.5B net income - a large FY2025 buyback was charged against retained earnings (retirement artifact); the 4-yr AVERAGE YoY RE growth is still high (~27%) because FY22->FY23 (+35.5%) and FY23->FY24 (+43.4%) dominate. R&D expense line present (costs the I&NE no-R&D half-point). No preferred stock.

Sentiment: Very positive. Stock reportedly +154% in H1 2026 on AI tailwinds; consensus Strong Buy/Overweight from ~36 analysts, avg PT ~$311 with recent Street hikes (BofA $480, Susquehanna $475). CY2026 WFE spend est ~$140B; management guides advanced-packaging revenue +50% in 2026; Zacks FY26/FY27 revenue growth est +25%/+31%, EPS +37%/+38%. >50% plasma-etch share, core to HBM/advanced packaging/3D NAND. Key risk: customer concentration (TSMC/leading foundries) and cyclicality. Assignment on a dip would be celebratable.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Lam Research; FY ends late June (FY2025 = fiscal year ended June 29, 2025). Semiconductor WFE equipment maker (etch/deposition leader). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (OI: 5382/5175/4264/5901; D&A: 334/342/360/386), matches stockanalysis EBITDA series. Watch items: inventory dips FY23->FY24 on memory-cycle destock (4816->4218), so I&NE steady-rise likely fails; R&D expense line present (~$1.6-2.1B/yr) so no I&NE R&D point. EP is a knife-edge: EBITDA > current liabilities in FY22-FY24 but FY25 current liabilities balloon to 6568 (short-term debt / current portion) vs EBITDA 6287, so FY25 fails. Goodwill essentially flat ~$1.5-1.6B and dips slightly FY24->FY25 (1627->1600) so GW-increasing likely fails. RE YoY growth ~19%/13%/17%, avg ~16.3% (may miss the 17% top tier by a hair). SE positive all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies. Carries large treasury stock from consistent buybacks.

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.00 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)

Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 62.7%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 22.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Very positive Street tone: Strong Buy / Buy consensus (22 buys, 3 holds, 0 sells), 12-mo targets ~+36%. MPWR is an AI-data-center power darling - projected ~70% share of Nvidia Vera Rubin (VR200/R200) power delivery, record FY2025 revenue $2.8B (+26% y/y), Q1-2026 +26% y/y, debt-free, high-ROIC fabless model. Business quality is exactly the kind of assignment we'd celebrate. Cautions for an operator: nosebleed valuation (trailing P/E ~90-112x vs peer ~57x / fair ~45x) leaves the stock highly sensitive to any AI-capex slowdown or design-win loss; semiconductor-cycle volatility (FY23 destock is visible in the numbers); and heavy insider selling - ~$139M sold in the last 3 months, 220 insider sales and zero buys in 6 months. A CSP strike well below spot is the natural way to demand a discount before owning it.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Monolithic Power Systems: fabless power-management IC maker; FY ends Dec 31. Latest 10-K = FY2025 (filed early 2026); window FY2022-FY2025. Carries real inventory (fabless but holds chip inventory) - not a services zero. Debt-free: XBRL shows no long-term debt any year (LTD=0 all 4). No treasury-stock line - MPWR retires repurchased shares rather than holding treasury (TS flag absent, treasury_abs=0); this zeros the TS parameter but note the DEBT-FREE balance sheet still clears TSA-DSER easily (avg TL/SE ~0.20). No preferred outstanding. R&D line present (~$190M FY21 -> ~$382M FY25) so I&NE loses its no-R&D point; I&NE rise also broken by FY23 cyclical destock (inventory 447->384, EBITDA 564->522 both dipped). WATCH: RE 3/3 rides a one-time artifact - FY2024 net income was $1,592M (vs $438M FY22 / $427M FY23 / $621M FY25) due to a large one-time income-tax BENEFIT, spiking retained earnings +142% FY23->FY24 (947->2293). Strip that year and RE growth is ~23% (FY22->23) then ~14% (FY24->25) - still solid but the 3rd RE point leans on the tax-benefit year. RSH is a genuine near-miss: latest EBITDA/SE = 781/3532 = 22.1%, just under the 23% bar (0/1). Goodwill: flat $6.57M FY22-23, stepped to $25.94M FY24 (small acquisition), flat FY25 - newest>oldest, no dip, so GW scores. All 12 series cross-checked against SEC XBRL company facts (authoritative) and they match stockanalysis.com exactly; EBITDA = operating income + D&A confirmed from XBRL (OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization).

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.38 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.5 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.82 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)2/3retained earnings grew 14.9%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 43.7% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Bullish-to-neutral. 32-analyst consensus 13 Buy / 8 Hold / 1 Sell, median PT $270 vs ~$230 (≈17% upside). Q1 2026 beat; management guides FY2026 revenue $17.05-17.15B, adj EPS $7.20-7.28. Soft spots: Environmental Solutions segment revenue −7% / EBITDA −26% on manufacturing weakness, ~$100M 2026 revenue headwind, flat volumes, stock down slightly over the past year. Core collection/landfill franchise remains a classic boring-but-dominant moat (routes, permitted landfills, pricing power) - assignment at a sensible strike would be celebratable. Earnings 7/27/2026.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (incl. accretion) per stockanalysis.com; cross-foots exactly with SEC XBRL OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization + accretion. Inventory is small but real (landfill/fleet parts) - from SEC XBRL InventoryNet; stockanalysis.com folds it into other current assets. Long-term debt = balance-sheet 'long-term debt, net of current maturities' (XBRL LongTermDebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations). Shareholders' equity is parent-only (XBRL StockholdersEquity); total liabilities from stockanalysis.com reconciles to Assets − total equity incl. NCI. R&D line: none on income statement (XBRL ResearchAndDevelopmentExpense not reported). Preferred authorized but none outstanding. Treasury stock present every year (fluctuates with retirements: $113M FY2024, $1,000M FY2025). Note goodwill is large (~49% of assets) - serial tuck-in acquirer by design. Next earnings 2026-07-27 (matters for chain sweeps, not DCAP).

Health Technology / Animal Health · price $75.41 · mcap $31.6B · vol 8,147,169 · PE 12.4 · PB 9.7 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 13/17 (76%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years1/1EBITDA covered current liabilities in every one of the 4 years
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 17.2% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.46 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.2 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative1/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.92 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)2/3retained earnings grew 16.6%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 121.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Bearish near-term: stock ~$75, down ~36% y/y after a 2026 guidance reset (revenue $9.68-9.96B, adj EPS $6.85-7.00, 2-5% organic growth) on companion-animal competition (OA-pain franchise/Librela pressure) and soft Q1; Argus downgrade to Hold, price targets cut ~$30-40 to low $100s, trading momentum 14/86 bullish/bearish. Fundamentals in the DCAP window remain intact - EBITDA and retained earnings rose every year, OCF ~$2.9B. Drawdown-entry profile similar to TSCO: assignment at a discount on the dominant animal-health franchise is celebratable, but the competitive-moat questions (analysts explicitly questioning the companion-animal moat) are exactly what DCAP is supposed to referee - flag for operator awareness.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Zoetis (animal health; calendar FY ending Dec 31). EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A (2917+465, 3016+491, 3339+497, 3546+487); D&A cross-checked exactly vs SEC XBRL. All balance-sheet and cash-flow series match SEC 10-K XBRL company-facts exactly; PP&E uses SEC PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (stockanalysis shows ~$220-290M higher, likely lease assets folded in). R&D line present every year ($539M-$698M) - genuine pharma R&D, flag is factual. Preferred authorized but none issued. LTD jumped $5.2B→$9.0B in FY2025 while treasury stock grew $7.4B→$10.7B - debt-funded buybacks compressed equity to $3.3B (still positive all 4 years, TSA-DSER applies). Goodwill dipped in FY2024 (2,759→2,724) before recovering - likely FX, may cost the GW point.

Distribution Services · price $298.55 · mcap $58.1B · vol 1,723,312 · PE 22.8 · PB 17.0 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 11/15 (73%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 5.3% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.08 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negativeN/Aexcused: shareholders' equity was negative or incomplete in the window, so this test is off the scale (max drops to 15)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 30.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 255.1% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Consensus Buy (12 Buy / 4 Hold / 1 Sell as of May 2026); FY2026 guidance raised on OneOncology/Retina-fueled earnings jump; Q2-FY2026 revenue +3.8% YoY to $78.4B, strong cash generation. Wall Street price targets $371-440 vs ~$300 now (near 52-wk low). Overhang: ~$6B opioid settlement payments through 2038 (~$330M/yr, manageable). Trades ~23x PE. Dominant #1-3 US drug-distribution oligopoly (with MCK/CAH). Assignment would be celebrate-able quality; the distributor economics simply cap the DCAP structurally.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Cencora (fmr AmerisourceBergen); pharma sourcing/distribution, FY ends Sep 30. FY2025 = year ended 9/30/2025. Distributor working-capital structure: EBITDA (~$3-3.7B) is a tiny fraction of current liabilities (~$43-58B, mostly trade payables) so EP fails all 4 yrs; NOCF/total-assets averages only ~5.3% so RTA scores 0 - same structural floor as CAH/MCK/ADM. SE went NEGATIVE in FY2023 (-$2,678M) from opioid-litigation charges + buybacks, so TSA-DSER is excused (max 15). shareholders_equity is Cencora-attributable equity (excludes ~$0.8B FY2025 noncontrolling interest from the Retina Consultants majority stake). FY2025 LTD jump ($3.8B->$7.5B) and goodwill jump ($9.3B->$13.7B) both driven by the Jan-2025 Retina Consultants of America (RCA) acquisition; that GW step-up plus the FY23->FY24 GW dip likely breaks GW steady-rise. RE grew strongly (2978->6534, ~30%/yr avg) but partly a recovery off an opioid-litigation-depressed FY2022 base (never went negative, so genuine growth not a sign-flip). RSH (latest EBITDA/SE = 3697/1449 = ~255%) is flattered by the thin buyback-eroded equity base. No R&D line, no preferred, large ongoing treasury/buybacks.

Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negativeN/Aexcused: shareholders' equity was negative or incomplete in the window, so this test is off the scale (max drops to 15)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 22.0%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1equity is negative (heavy buybacks can do this) but 4 straight years of positive EBITDA back it - DANGER-rule pass
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Analyst tone strongly positive as of July 2026: Strong Buy consensus (11 buy / 1 hold / 0 sell), ~$935 avg price target vs ~$786 price. Strong Q3 FY2026 results; raised full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $38.80-$39.20; April 2026 Apollo Funds strategic minority-interest deal in Medical-Surgical Solutions. Watch items: GLP-1 pricing/reimbursement dynamics could compress U.S. Pharmaceutical distribution margins; some recent volatility on that theme. Assignment-celebratable: dominant pharma distributor (top-3 oligopoly), massive buyback history, accelerating retained earnings (~22%/yr avg growth). Caveat: negative shareholders' equity (buyback-driven, not distress) and razor-thin distribution margins mean EBITDA is structurally far below current liabilities - inherent to the business model.
research notes - methodology & caveats
All figures $ millions, fiscal years FY2023-FY2026 oldest→newest (FY ends March 31; FY2026 ended 2026-03-31). EBITDA = operating income (SEC EDGAR XBRL 10-K: 4381/3909/4422/6212) + cash-flow D&A (stockanalysis.com: 608/635/636/729). PP&E is the SEC 10-K PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet line (excludes operating-lease ROU assets; stockanalysis shows ~4.7B because it bundles ROU). Long-term debt = balance-sheet 'long-term debt less current portion' (LongTermDebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations tag). Shareholders' equity = total equity INCLUDING noncontrolling interests (parent-only stockholders' deficit is -1857/-1971/-2074/-2172; both negative all 4 yrs → TSA-DSER excused either way). Total liabilities derived as total assets − total equity (McKesson 10-K reports no separate TL subtotal in XBRL); FY2026 stockanalysis TL (83,157) disagrees with EDGAR-derived 84,100 - EDGAR used. Negative equity is buyback-driven: treasury stock grew to $32.0B. Retained earnings and operating income cross-checked stockanalysis vs SEC EDGAR: exact match. No R&D line (drug distributor). No preferred stock on the balance sheet.

Sentiment: Analyst tone bullish: BofA PT raised to $720, Jefferies to $770 (Buy) after the advanced-packaging event; company guides semi-equipment business +30% in calendar 2026 with WFE spend accelerating in H2. Headwinds: ~$600M 2026 revenue impact from tighter US China-export curbs (China equipment spend expected to fall), and the stock sold off with the broader semi group on SK Hynix HBM4 slowdown reports. Assignment would be celebratable: dominant materials-engineering franchise, huge buyback history, fortress balance sheet; main risk is semi-cycle beta, not business quality.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fiscal year ends last Sunday of October (FY2025 ended 2025-10-26). EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A from SEC XBRL (OI 7788/7654/7867/8289 + D&A 444/515/392/435); matches stockanalysis.com EBITDA exactly. Retained earnings cross-checked EDGAR vs stockanalysis: identical. R&D expense line present and large ($3.57B FY2025) - semicap equipment maker, R&D is the moat here but the rubric counts it against I&NE. Preferred: authorized but none issued per 10-K balance sheet. Large and growing treasury stock ($45.0B abs FY2025) - sustained buyback history. Inventory dipped FY2023-FY2024 (industry destock) then recovered FY2025. LTD rose FY2025 on new issuance ($6.46B) but LTD/EBITDA ~0.74. NOCF FY2022 depressed by working-capital build.

Sentiment: Very positive (as of 2026-07-07). Buy consensus (17 analysts, ~53% Strong Buy); price targets clustering $190-$200 with KeyBanc ($200), BofA ($200), Morgan Stanley ($190) all raising on AI-networking demand. Q1 2026 revenue $2.7B (+35% YoY), FY2026 guidance raised to ~$11.5B with ~$3.5B AI sales; new 1.6T AI switch platforms backed by Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, AMD, Broadcom. Risk: management flags 1-2 years of wafer/memory/CPU/optical supply constraints pressuring gross margins. A premier AI-infrastructure franchise -- assignment would be celebrated.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Arista Networks (ANET), FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = GAAP operating income + D&A (D&A ~$63-73M/yr, immaterial). Carries real inventory (networking hardware). No long-term debt (debt-free balance sheet). No treasury stock line (buybacks retire shares directly; ~$1.6B repurchased FY2025). Preferred stock authorized but none issued/outstanding -> absent. FY2025 current liabilities jumped to $5.38B (large income-tax and deferred-revenue balances) which likely breaks EP (EBITDA<CL in FY2025). Inventory dipped in FY2024 (1945->1835) then rose, so I&NE steady-rise likely fails. PPE spikes 98.8->203.1 in FY2025 (>60% jump) -> PPE param likely 0. Goodwill flat FY2022-24 (~$266-268.5M) then jumped to $416.1M on a FY2025 acquisition. RE growth avg ~45%/yr, RTA avg ~19%, TSA-DSER avg ~0.44 -> strong on those. Values in USD millions.

Sentiment: Record fiscal 2025: net sales ~$21.5B, net income $578M, comparable club sales +2.6% ex-gas, membership fee income at all-time high. Q1 FY2026 net sales +9.9% YoY, membership income +~10%; Texas expansion running 33% ahead of plan, 12 new clubs planned in 2026 with similar pace through 2027-28. Analyst consensus ~Hold, fair-value targets ~$109 vs price ~$88 (stock -20% from $110.92 52wk high, near 52wk low $83.21 set 2026-06-22). FY2026 guide maintained: ex-gas comps +2-3%, adj EPS $4.40-4.60. Concerns: core ex-gas comps modest (~1.5%), margin pressure from price investments, and a Russell index removal that pressured the stock. Debt-light, cash-generative dominant-in-region warehouse club - assignment near current drawdown levels would be celebratable.
research notes - methodology & caveats
BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings - membership warehouse retailer. Fiscal year ends late Jan/early Feb; labels map to filing period-ends: FY2022=Jan 28 2023, FY2023=Feb 3 2024, FY2024=Feb 1 2025, FY2025=Jan 31 2026. All 12 series pulled from SEC XBRL companyfacts (10-K), so every number ties to filings. IMPORTANT: ppe = filing 'property and equipment, net' ONLY (1337/1579/1898/2365); stockanalysis inflates PP&E to ~4341 by folding in operating-lease ROU assets - excluded here, as ROU is not PP&E. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (cross-checked: 738+204=942, 800+229=1029, 772+263=1035, 817+290=1106). No R&D line (warehouse retail). No preferred outstanding. TREASURY-STOCK RETIREMENT: BJ carried treasury stock 559/718/936 (FY22-24) then RETIRED all of it in FY2025 -> treasury=0 on the Jan 31 2026 balance sheet; this also drove retained earnings DOWN from 1702.6 to 1201.2 despite $578M FY2025 net income (retirement charged against RE/APIC). So the TS flag is honestly 'absent' and the last RE YoY is a -29% retirement artifact, not deterioration - BJ has an active, ongoing buyback. This is the ORLY/SHW/MSI pattern: an operator policy call on treasury-retirement could flip the TS point (11->12/17, ~65%->71%). Likely structural zeros: EP (retail payables >> EBITDA every year), TSA-DSER (avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~2.5, heavy operating-lease load), GW (goodwill dead flat at 1008.8, no acquisitions). RTA avg NOCF/assets ~12.4% clears the 11% tier but misses 17%.

Sentiment: Buy consensus (22 analysts, ~50% Strong Buy / 45% Buy as of Jul 2026); 12-mo target trimmed to ~$82-85 from ~$96. FY2026 guide: adj EPS $3.34-3.41 (+9-11%), revenue ~$21.7B, adj operating margin +50-75bps, organic growth guided to sub-10% (~6.5-8%). Watch items: EP/Watchman growth decelerating on competition + reimbursement cuts; J&J launched a head-to-head PFA trial vs FARAPULSE and an improved Shockwave IVL catheter. FARAPULSE AVANT GUARD trial met all endpoints and showed PFA superiority over AADs. Fundamentals strong and compounding; assignment on a quality medtech compounder would be celebrable, though growth is normalizing off a torrid pace.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Boston Scientific - medical devices, FY ends Dec. Carries inventory (physical devices). EBITDA built as operating income + total D&A from cash-flow statement (SEC-confirmed op income 1649/2343/2603/3613; reconciles exactly to stockanalysis EBITDA using cash-flow D&A 1136/1196/1269/1368). Retained earnings verified against SEC XBRL exactly (-750/819/2673/5571): a positive-inflection series that crosses from accumulated deficit to positive RE - RE-growth points may ride on a sign-flip off the negative FY2022 base (artifact, flag for operator, cf. APO). Treasury stock flat at $2,251M all 4 years (no fresh buybacks in window; historical treasury present). R&D expense line present (medtech) so I&NE loses its no-R&D point. EP likely fails all 4 years: EBITDA < current liabilities every year (current liabilities elevated by acquisition/contingent-consideration and short-term debt). Steady growth on inventory/EBITDA/PPE/NOCF/goodwill; PPE largest jump FY25 +22.5% (< 60% spike gate).

Sentiment: Buy-rated (26 analysts, avg PT ~$127 implying ~20% upside). Record FY2026: revenue $5.47B (+10%), diluted EPS $7.02 (+11%), operating margin >23%, $1.9B cash and debt-free. HOKA $2.59B (+16%), UGG $2.74B (+8%). Mgmt laid out a 2030 framework: high-single-digit revenue CAGR, low-double-digit EPS growth. Stock did drop ~15% in Oct 2025 on a soft outlook (Hoka/Ugg growth-deceleration worries) but recovered as FY2026 raises followed; FY2027 guide of $5.86-5.91B rev / $7.30-7.45 EPS flags gross-margin pressure from freight/input costs. A high-quality, cash-generative, debt-free consumer brand -- assignment here would be celebratable.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Deckers Outdoor (UGG/HOKA/Teva); FY ends March 31 -> latest 4 filed fiscal years are FY2023..FY2026 (FY2026 10-K, year ended 2026-03-31, already filed). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (SEC XBRL OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationAmortizationAndAccretionNet); cross-checks stockanalysis.com EBITDA within rounding (700.6/985.1/1248.4 vs 702.8/987.2/1251). PP&E uses SEC XBRL core PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (222/267/302/326), NOT aggregator figures (405/480/528/563) which bundle operating-lease ROU assets -- the ROU trap. long_term_debt = 0 all years: Deckers carries ZERO borrowings/notes (revolver undrawn); XBRL LongTermDebtNoncurrent untagged, noncurrent liabilities are long-term operating leases + income taxes, not debt. treasury_stock_abs = 0 and TS flag 'absent' because Deckers RETIRES repurchased shares (no treasury line on balance sheet) -- the buyback-retirement pattern [[msi-near-miss-retired-buybacks]] that zeros the TS point. Note RE dips FY25->FY26 (2307.7 -> 2246.4) despite ~$1B net income because $1.075B of FY2026 buybacks were retired, charging excess over par to retained earnings; this trims avg YoY RE growth to ~13.2%, likely missing the 13.5% tier. Total liabilities = current + noncurrent (SEC 'Liabilities' concept untagged). Goodwill flat at 13.99 all 4 years -> GW point fails. No R&D line (SG&A only). No preferred. Inventory not a steady rise (533->474->495->487).

Sentiment: Recovering. FY2025 was Hershey's cocoa annus horribilis (margins crushed, EPS down sharply), but 2026 tone has turned constructive: cocoa down from >$10,000/t to ~$5,000/t, Q1-2026 net sales $3.1B with adj EPS $2.35 beating on 10% price realization, FY2026 guidance of 4-5% sales growth and $8-9 EPS with $230M efficiency savings targeted. Analysts flag margin recovery ("stock near a sweet spot as cocoa pressure eases"; one DCF pegs it ~42% below fair value), average price target ~$217.50, though Q2/FY26 EPS estimates were trimmed on soft scanner data and salty-snack freight costs. New CEO Kirk Tanner (2025) executing pricing + efficiency playbook. Assignment would be celebratable: iconic dominant confectionery brand with pricing power, taken at a cyclical input-cost trough.
research notes - methodology & caveats
All figures $ millions, FY2022→FY2025 (fiscal year = calendar year, ends Dec 31). EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A from cash-flow statement (SEC XBRL: OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization): 2261+379, 2561+420, 2898+455, 1442+504; matches stockanalysis.com EBITDA line. FY2025 EBITDA collapse (-42% YoY) driven by record cocoa costs (>$10k/metric ton early 2025) plus commodity derivative mark-to-market losses in GAAP operating income - cyclical input-cost shock, not demand/moat erosion; cocoa has since retreated to ~$5k/t and Q1-2026 pricing realization was +10%. No R&D line item on income statement (SG&A only; consumer staples). Preferred stock authorized but none issued. Large and growing treasury stock (consistent buyback history). LTD and PP&E values are SEC-filed XBRL (comparative balance sheets); all other series confirmed in 10-K XBRL facts.

Industrials - Compressors/Flow Machinery · price $80.63 · mcap $31.6B · vol 3,961,291 · PE 54.3 · PB 3.1 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 12/17 (71%)
gift at ≥60%

Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.59 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 49.3%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 15.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Constructive/quality-industrial tone. IR is a serial-acquirer compounder run on the "IRX" operating model; FY2025 was a modest down-tick in GAAP operating income ($1.30B->$1.15B) with adjusted EBITDA guided up to ~$2.13-2.19B and record cash flow, driven by M&A while organic growth softened. Stock ~$78, off a $101 Feb-2026 high (-23% from high, mid-52wk-range). PE ~54 (rich). Nothing scandalous - assignment would be owning a well-run, wide-portfolio industrial at a discount; celebratable. Main watch items: acquisition-driven goodwill now ~46% of assets, and rich multiple leaves valuation risk.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Ingersoll Rand Inc. (industrial: air/gas compression, flow-control, precision). FY ends Dec; window FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026). EBITDA built GAAP-consistent as operating income + D&A each year (IR only publishes an ADJUSTED EBITDA in press releases ~$2.0B FY24 that adds back stock comp/restructuring/acquisition-intangible amort - NOT used here). Cross-checked RE, goodwill, OI, SE against stocktitan; all match within rounding. Inventory dips slightly FY22->FY23 (1025->1001) so likely fails the >=98% steady-rise test => I&NE loses the inventory point. EP note: EBITDA < current liabilities in all 4 years (industrial working-cap/goodwill-heavy structure) so EP likely 0/4 - the same floor seen on ETN/CMI/DOV. RTA likely 0/2: avg NOCF/total-assets ~7.5%, dragged by a goodwill-heavy asset base (GW ~46% of assets). RE grows ~49%/yr avg but off a LOW base ($951M FY22) - Gardner Denver/IR 2020 merger left a young accumulated-earnings balance, so RE 3/3 is a genuine-but-low-base artifact (operator watch, like HD/CMG). LTD jump FY23->FY24 ($2.7B->$4.8B) funded acquisitions; still LTD/EBITDA ~3.1x and LTD/assets ~22%. No preferred outstanding; active buybacks ($1.02B repurchased FY2025) => treasury present. No separate R&D expense line on the income statement (engineering embedded in COGS/SG&A) => absent.

Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.78 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 9.9%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 40.2% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Consensus Buy/Moderate Buy; analyst PTs ~$238-280 (HSBC $280 high, Leerink $265). 2025 saw a re-rating as the market recognized the Kenvue-less, oncology-led growth profile (P/E recovering toward historical averages). Overhang: talc litigation - Dec 2025 record $1.5B Baltimore jury verdict, 67,000+ pending claims, third bankruptcy attempt rejected March 2025 so back in the tort system (managed via appeals/scale). Positives: 64 consecutive years of dividend increases (2026 raise to $1.34/qtr); 2026 guidance for +50bps operating-margin improvement via Project Catalyst; planned separation of the Orthopaedics business. Diversified franchise larger than the talc risk. Assignment celebrable given durable pharma/medtech moat and dividend-aristocrat status.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends late Dec (FY2025 = Dec 28, 2025). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (stockanalysis reconciles exactly: FY25 25,287+7,503=32,790); deliberately NOT macrotrends bottom-up EBITDA (26.1B FY24), which captures non-operating volatility (talc litigation charges, the Kenvue separation gain). STRUCTURAL CONTEXT: Kenvue consumer-health split-off completed Aug 2023, so FY2022 includes full consumer segment; FY2023 is transitional and its ~$20B+ split-off gain inflates the FY22->FY23 retained-earnings jump (25.5B, 19.9%); FY2024-25 are "new J&J" (oncology/medtech-led). RE avg growth ~9.9% is thus propped by that one-time gain artifact - strip it and RE growth is weak (FY23->24 only 1.3%, FY24->25 8.5%). EP fails structurally: current liabilities 46-55B exceed EBITDA 27-33B every year (large-cap pharma working capital / short-term debt). Goodwill rising on FY24 Shockwave (~$13B) + FY25 Intra-Cellular acquisitions; LTD rose FY24-25 to fund those deals but LTD/EBITDA stays low (~1.2). Treasury stock jumped FY22->FY23 (41.7B->75.7B) as J&J retired shares received in the Kenvue exchange offer. R&D line present (kills the I&NE second point). No preferred outstanding.

Sentiment: Consensus Moderate/Moderate-Buy (12 buy / 5 hold / 0 sell, ~23 analysts); 12-mo targets ~$536-576, ~14% upside. FY2025 revenue $7.72B (+8.9% y/y), earnings $2.46B (+19.5%); strong debt-issuance recovery driving MIS as the global easing cycle lifts bond issuance; MA (analytics) provides recurring subscription ballast. Wide-moat duopoly (with S&P Global) in credit ratings - assignment would be highly celebratable. Only restraint is valuation (premium multiple).
research notes - methodology & caveats
Moody's Corporation - ratings agency (MIS) + analytics/data (MA). FY ends Dec; latest 4 are FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026). No inventory line - services business (ratings/subscriptions), so inventory=[0,0,0,0]. EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A (OI 1883/2137/2875/3351 confirmed on EDGAR; D&A 331/373/431/480 from cash-flow statement; matches stockanalysis reported EBITDA). Retained earnings confirmed exactly against SEC EDGAR XBRL (13618/14659/16071/17853). Treasury stock is genuine buyback history (not a negative-equity excusal - SE positive all 4 yrs), growing $11.5B→$15.0B. No R&D expense line and no preferred outstanding. Watch item: EP likely fails FY2022 (EBITDA 2214 < current liabilities 2375) - subscription/deferred-revenue-heavy current liabilities are structural for MA; RE grows genuinely (~9.5%/yr avg) but sits below the 13.5%/17% tiers due to large buyback dividends out of equity.

Sentiment: Strongly positive - Strong Buy consensus (34 Buy / 4 Hold / 0 Sell across ~49 analysts). Increasingly framed as a primary agentic-AI infrastructure winner (the governance/orchestration control layer for enterprise AI agents), not an AI casualty. Now Assist AI ACV target raised to $1.5B for 2026; FY26 subscription guide raised to ~$15.74-15.78B (20.5-21% cc growth); RPO $27.7B / cRPO $12.64B backlog. Near-term watch items: Armis acquisition pressures FY26 op margin outlook (lowered to 31.5%) and FCF margin (35%), plus a ~75bp subscription headwind from delayed Middle East on-prem deals. Q2 2026 earnings 2026-07-22. Assignment here would be celebrated - dominant, cash-generative, debt-light compounder.
research notes - methodology & caveats
ServiceNow (NOW), enterprise SaaS/workflow platform; FY ends Dec 31. No inventory line - pure software/services, so inventory = 0s (genuine, not missing). EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A and cross-checks year by year: FY22 355+791=1146, FY23 762+1021=1783, FY24 1364+1114=2478, FY25 1824+1359=3183. LTD is a single $1.5B convertible-note-era fixed debt, near-flat all 4 yrs. Treasury stock began FY23 (buyback program) and grows to $3.045B abs - genuine buyback presence. Goodwill steps up sharply FY25 (824>1231>1273>3578) driven by 2025 M&A (Moveworks etc.); still monotonically increasing. Retained earnings inflecting hard positive off a $338M base. R&D expense line present (~$2.96B FY25). No preferred stock.

Sentiment: Positive/recovering. Steel demand and hot-rolled-coil prices rebounding (HRC >$1,100/ton) with Section 232 25% tariff shield protecting domestic mills; Wells Fargo, JPMorgan and Goldman upgraded views after NUE guided Q1'26 EPS up ~70% YoY. Stock +23% in 3 months; consensus PT ~$186 (viewed as roughly full valuation). FY2024-25 was the earnings trough; 2026 is an up-cycle inflection. As the dominant low-cost US steelmaker with a fortress balance sheet and long buyback history, assignment would be celebratable - the numbers just reflect where we sit in the steel cycle.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Nucor (NUE), largest & lowest-cost US steelmaker, Dec fiscal-year-end. Series FY2022-FY2025 from the FY2025 10-K (filed 2026-02-25, gives FY2025+FY2024, in $M) and the FY2023 10-K (filed 2024-02-27, gives FY2023+FY2022, in $thousands - converted to $M). EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A (depreciation+amortization) and independently cross-checked against stockanalysis.com's EBITDA series (identical: 11466/7399/4198/4072). shareholders_equity = TOTAL equity incl. noncontrolling interests (Nucor-only SE would be 18,415/20,941/20,294/20,936); total equity used for consistency with the reported treasury/RE components; positive every year. Treasury_stock_abs = |common stock held in treasury| line (8,498/9,988/12,144/12,779). R&D: income statement carries NO R&D expense line; the only "research and development expenditures" reference is a deferred-tax-asset component (Sec.174 capitalization) - immaterial, steel is not R&D-driven - so flag=absent. No preferred outstanding. Heavy inventory + PP&E (real steel mills, not ROU). KEY CONTEXT: FY2022 was a peak-cycle blowout (EBITDA $11.5B) and FY2024-FY2025 a cyclical trough (EBITDA ~$4B), so cyclicality mechanically dents the rising-series params (I&NE EBITDA falls; EP fails in FY2024 where EBITDA 4198 < curr-liab 4977). Balance-sheet quality is fortress: LTD/assets ~0.19, LTD/EBITDA 1.7x, treasury-adjusted TL/(SE+treasury) ~0.40, huge growing retained earnings, growing goodwill, sustained buybacks.

Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 2.96 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 17.5%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 82.7% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Strongly positive. FY2026 (ended 2026-01-31) results beat: full-year net sales $60.4B (+7%), comp sales +5%, Q4 EPS $1.58 (+28% y/y); dividend raised 13%, $2.5-2.75B buyback guided for FY27. Analyst consensus Strong Buy / Buy (roughly 19 buy, 1 hold, 1 sell among the most-cited set), price targets in the ~$166-185 range. Off-price model is a beneficiary of value-seeking/trade-down consumer behavior and tariff-driven branded inventory availability; resilient traffic and margins. Nothing that would make assignment un-celebratable - this is a dominant, low-debt, cash-generative retailer we'd be happy to own at a discount.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Off-price apparel/home retailer (T.J. Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods, Sierra, TK Maxx). Fiscal year ends late Jan/early Feb; labels use the company's fiscal convention (FY2023 ended 2023-01-28 ... FY2026 ended 2026-01-31). Latest 4 fiscal years used (FY2026 10-K filed 2026-02, XBRL-confirmed). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (D&A from cash-flow stmt: 887/964/1104/1247; OI: 4860/5797/6302/7178). PPE = XBRL PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (owned property net: 5783/6571/7346/8220) NOT stockanalysis "Net PP&E" (14869-18550), which bundles ~$9-10B of operating-lease right-of-use assets (ROU trap) - using the ROU-inflated figure would still pass PPE but overstate the asset base. Treasury stock = 0: TJX RETIRES repurchased shares (no treasury-stock line on the balance sheet, no XBRL TreasuryStock concept) -> TS param scores 0 and TSA-DSER gets no treasury add-back, despite a very active buyback ($2.5-2.75B guided for FY27). No R&D line; no preferred (XBRL PreferredStockValue = 0 all years). Total liabilities cross-checked via assets - equity identity; retained earnings cross-checked SEC XBRL vs stockanalysis (identical). Expected floors: EP fails (EBITDA < current liabilities every year - off-price payables + current lease liabilities working-capital float, same floor as ROST/COST); TSA-DSER fails (avg TL/equity ~2.9x, lease-liability heavy, no treasury cushion); GW ~flat/slightly down (97->96); TS absent. Strengths: I&NE 2/2 (inventory + EBITDA both steady rise, no R&D), RTA (avg NOCF/assets ~18%), LTD near-nil leverage, RE growth ~17.5% avg, RSH strong. Reads like a gift on the ROST off-price template.

Sentiment: Analyst tone constructive: consensus Buy (28 analysts, 0 sells), avg PT ~$256 (~13% above spot); recent PT raises from RBC ($240, Sector Perform) and Barclays ($270, Overweight). Q1-2026 showed strong EBITDA/cash-flow growth and margin expansion incl. sustainability segment; FCF nearly doubled; on track for full-year guidance. Headwinds: sluggish volumes, commodity (recycled fiber) prices, Stericycle integration still in progress. Nothing un-celebratable about assignment - dominant landfill network is a classic moat; Q2 earnings 7/28 is the near-term event.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Calendar fiscal year (Dec 31). EBITDA = operating income + D&A, both per SEC XBRL 10-K facts (2022: 3365+2038; 2023: 3575+2071; 2024: 4063+2267; 2025: 4308+2863); matches stockanalysis.com EBITDA exactly. 'Inventory' is WM's 'Parts and supplies' line (us-gaap:Supplies) - a hauler carries no merchandise inventory. Long-term debt = LongTermDebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations (noncurrent), per balance sheet. Shareholders' equity is WM parent StockholdersEquity per SEC XBRL (excludes small noncontrolling interests; total-equity variant differs by ≤$15M). Treasury stock $12.9B / 228.2M shares held - WM buys back and holds, does not retire. No R&D expense line on the income statement; no preferred shares outstanding (0 all 4 yrs, SEC XBRL). Stericycle acquisition (~$7.2B) closed Nov 2024: explains FY2024 jumps in total assets, LTD (+$6.6B), goodwill (+$4.2B), and current liabilities; goodwill dipped FY2022→FY2023 on divestitures. Q2 earnings 2026-07-28 - inside typical 30-45 DTE windows for any subsequent chain sweep.

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 8.3% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.30 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.8 years of EBITDA (cap 4)

Sentiment: Strongly positive. FY2025 revenue $17.18B (+9.4%), Q1 2026 EPS $5.66 vs $4.46 (+27%), net income +26%, margins expanding. Analyst consensus Buy (~12 Buy / 3 Hold / 1 Sell), price targets ~$396-427 (mid/high-single-digit upside); Citi upgraded to Buy ($402 Dec), Evercore ISI to Outperform ($427). NFP integration hitting milestones (bolt-on Hamilton Insurance Agency Dec 2025), credit upgrades, reinsurance tailwinds; 2026 guide = mid-single-digit+ organic growth, 70-80bp margin expansion, double-digit FCF growth. Nothing that would make assignment un-celebratable - a dominant, wide-moat broker firing on all cylinders. Only watch item: leverage/goodwill from the NFP deal still working through the balance sheet.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Aon plc, global insurance/reinsurance broker & HR consulting (Risk Capital + Human Capital segments). FY ends Dec 31; window is the 4 most recent filed years FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed 2026-02-13). NO INVENTORY - services business (0s are genuine, not missing). EBITDA built as operating income + D&A (op inc 3669/3785/3835/4344 + cash-flow D&A 264/256/686/966 = 3933/4041/4521/5310); D&A jump FY24/25 = NFP intangible amortization. TREASURY STOCK ABSENT: Aon retires repurchased shares (constructive retirement) - no treasury line on the balance sheet, so treasury_stock_abs=0 and flag 'absent' (this is a buyback-heavy company, but the rubric only rewards a held treasury line). SHAREHOLDERS' EQUITY NEGATIVE in FY2022 (-429) and FY2023 (-742) from years of retirement-method buybacks + large accumulated-OCI loss; turned positive FY2024 (+6430) / FY2025 (+9548) after the ~$13B NFP acquisition (Apr 2024) funded with stock + debt (note the LTD spike to 16,265 in FY24, goodwill 8,414->15,234). SE figures are consolidated total equity incl. NCI (reconciles to total assets - total liabilities each year); parent-only Aon SE is ~$9,352M FY2025 / $6,121M FY2024. Negative SE in 2 of 4 years => TSA-DSER excused, max drops to 15. RETAINED EARNINGS is an accumulated DEFICIT all 4 years (-2772/-3399/-2309/-245), improving but still negative (kept sign). STRUCTURAL BROKER NOTE: current_liabilities (~$23B) is dominated by fiduciary/premiums-payable balances (client float), so EBITDA<current-liabilities and NOCF/total-assets run low - same float penalty seen on insurers (see PGR/TRV pattern). Human call: this is a broker-float structural profile, likely a near-miss rather than a clean gift despite an excellent underlying franchise.

Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding

Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1equity is negative (heavy buybacks can do this) but 4 straight years of positive EBITDA back it - DANGER-rule pass
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Positive and improving. Consensus ~Strong Buy (14 buy / 1 hold / 0 sell as of mid-2026), avg PT ~$246; Citi upgraded to Buy Jan 2026 (PT 190->244). FY2026 Q1 (reported Oct 2025) beat: EPS $2.55 vs $2.22 est, revenue +22.4% YoY to ~$64B; FY2026 non-GAAP EPS guide raised to >=$10.00 at JPM Healthcare Jan 2026. Specialty (GI Alliance/ION) is the growth engine, guided >$50B revenue FY2026. Company transitioned branded-drug distribution agreements ahead of the 2026 Medicare Drug Price Negotiation implementation, defusing a regulatory overhang. Business momentum is strong and assignment would be defensible on quality -- but the DCAP structure (thin-margin distributor + negative book equity) is the constraint, not the story.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Cardinal Health, pharma/medical distributor; FY ends June 30, so window is FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed Aug 2025). Negative shareholders' equity in ALL 4 years (deep buybacks + opioid-litigation charges) -> TSA-DSER excused, applicable max 15; RSH rides the DANGER rule (all 4 EBITDA years positive: 85/1444/1953/3065). EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A from SEC XBRL (OpInc -607/752/1243/2275; D&A 692/692/710/790); FY2022 EBITDA of $85M reflects that year's operating loss (impairment + litigation). Distributor economics: current liabilities (trade payables) dwarf EBITDA every year -> EP will fail structurally; NOCF/assets modest. Inventory dipped FY2024 (destock) so I&NE inventory-rise fails; no R&D line (distributor) so I&NE gets the no-R&D point. Retained earnings swings negative->positive (-280/-642/-286/+783) = a sign-flip artifact, not clean growth. Goodwill uses the pure SEC us-gaap:Goodwill tag (5856/4613/4725/9691) -- dips in FY2023 then jumps FY2025 on the $5.3B FY2025 acquisition spree (GI Alliance ~$2.8B, ION); NOT a steady rise. Same FY2025 M&A drove long-term debt 4658->7977. shareholders_equity is parent Cardinal Health equity; total equity incl NCI is ~3/1/1/147M higher. total_liabilities computed as assets minus total equity (incl NCI), matches stockanalysis. Expect a distributor/negative-equity structural ceiling, same family as ABBV/MCK flagged in the healthcare sweep.

Sentiment: Positive/celebratable. Consensus Moderate Buy (~25-29 analysts; 9 Strong Buy), avg PT ~$372-376, stock ~$391. FY2026 guidance: net rooms +4.5-5%, record pipeline near 610k rooms, adjusted EBITDA +8-10%, RevPAR +1.5-2.5%. Multiple PT upgrades (Jefferies $415, Goldman $398, BMO $400). Modest macro/Middle-East RevPAR headwind noted but demand resilient. Wide-moat global lodging franchisor - a business we'd be delighted to own.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Asset-light hotel franchisor (fee-based); no inventory line on the balance sheet (0s reflect that, like HLT). EBITDA = operating income + D&A per stockanalysis (FY22 3462+193→~3655 rounded to reported 3862; company's ADJUSTED EBITDA is higher, e.g. FY25 ~$5.38B, but I used the consistent op-income+D&A series across all 4 yrs). Shareholders' equity turns negative FY2023-FY2025 from aggressive buybacks (treasury stock $17.0B→$27.9B) - TSA-DSER should be excused (max drops to 15). RE all positive and rising; RSH under DANGER rule qualifies since EBITDA positive all 4 yrs. FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed early 2026. Cross-checked RE (FY24 16,531 / FY25 18,414) and totals against MAR FY2025 earnings release / 10-K.

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)1/2avg debt/assets 0.69 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.7 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negativeN/Aexcused: shareholders' equity was negative or incomplete in the window, so this test is off the scale (max drops to 15)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 5.7%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)

GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Constructive. ~27 analysts: 56% buy/strong-buy, 41% hold, ~0% sell; consensus PT ~$342 near spot. Q1 global comps +3.8% beat on value leadership + high-margin menu innovation. New permanent McValue menu (Apr 2026) and CosMc's-inspired beverage rollout target budget diners vs Burger King/Wendy's; 2,600 store openings in 2026 en route to 50,000 by 2027; guided operating margin 45-47%. Watch item: forecast same-store-sales deceleration in China and Latin America on macro pressure. Dominant global QSR franchisor - assignment would be celebrated.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends December. EBITDA = operating income + full cash-flow D&A (FY22 9,371+1,871; FY23 11,647+1,978; FY24 11,712+2,097; FY25 12,393+2,199) - series internally consistent. Negative shareholders' equity in all 4 years (debt-funded buyback machine; treasury stock ~$71.6B→$79.3B swamps ~$70B retained earnings) → TSA-DSER excused, applicable max 15. RSH: SE negative but 4 straight positive EBITDA years, so DANGER rule permits the point. FY23 current liabilities spike ($6,859M) reflects a large current portion of LT debt maturing that year - watch EP. Inventory is minimal (restaurant/franchise, ~$52-61M) but nonzero. No R&D line. Preferred authorized but none outstanding.

gift at ≥60%

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 21.0% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.37 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.5 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negativeN/Aexcused: shareholders' equity was negative or incomplete in the window, so this test is off the scale (max drops to 15)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -1.5%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1equity is negative (heavy buybacks can do this) but 4 straight years of positive EBITDA back it - DANGER-rule pass
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Buy consensus (~17 analysts, PT ~$110-112 vs post-split price), long-tenured compounder with 3-5% comp guidance and 225-235 new stores planned for 2026; aging US vehicle fleet + high rates favor repair-over-replace tailwinds. Two live watch items: (1) ORLY has bid ~$10B CASH for Genuine Parts' NAPA auto-parts division - stock fell ~6.7% on the news; if it closes it adds major debt+goodwill and would warrant a full DCAP rescore (merger-year drag pattern). (2) Q2 2026 earnings 2026-07-29, inside any 30-45 DTE option window. Assignment itself remains celebratable: dominant duopoly position (with AZO), decades of double-digit EPS compounding.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY = calendar year ending Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A, both from EDGAR XBRL 10-K tags (op income FY22-25: 2954.5/3186.4/3251.2/3460.6; D&A: 357.9/409.1/461.9/511.2); EBITDA series matches stockanalysis.com exactly. Shareholders' equity NEGATIVE in all 4 years - a capital-structure artifact of ~$20B+ of buybacks with repurchased shares RETIRED (charged to APIC + retained earnings), not held as treasury stock; hence treasury_stock absent despite one of retail's longest continuous buyback programs, and retained_earnings is an accumulated deficit (narrowing since FY2023: -3131.5 → -2328.8 as earnings outpace buyback charges). TSA-DSER should be excused (max 15). PP&E is the filing's net PP&E excluding operating-lease ROU assets (stockanalysis shows ~$8.6B FY25 incl. ROU; EDGAR PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet used for consistency). Total liabilities derived as Assets − StockholdersEquity from EDGAR (Liabilities tag not exposed via companyconcept API); matches stockanalysis total-liabilities row to the million. No R&D line (auto-parts retailer; income statement carries only SG&A). Preferred authorized, none outstanding. 15-for-1 stock split June 2025 (no effect on these series).

ACN - Accenture plc GIFT

Technology Services · price $136.96 · mcap $83.8B · vol 16,393,690 · PE 11.0 · PB 2.6 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
