Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 18.2% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.02 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.4 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.77 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 5.6%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 39.3% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Contested - fundamentals vs narrative. Stock down >50% in 2026 (~$125 vs ~$259 in Jan) after a fiscal-Q3-2026 earnings miss, reduced FY2026 revenue-growth guidance, a sequential bookings decline, and analyst downgrades; the structural bear case is agentic AI automating IT-services work (shares fell on Anthropic's Feb-2026 enterprise-AI release). Counterpoints: record Q2 FY2026 new bookings of $22.1B incl. 41 clients >$100M, advanced-AI bookings ~doubling y/y ($2.2B in Q1 alone), Buy consensus with ~$249 mean target, and pristine FY2022-FY2025 financials (NOCF/assets ~18%, minimal net leverage). Assignment at a strike below ~$125 means buying the world's largest IT-consulting franchise at roughly half its January price - celebratable IF the operators accept that the AI-disruption question is genuinely open; flagging that judgment for human review per directive #4.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Aug 31 (FY2025 = year ended 2025-08-31; 10-K filed 2025-10-10). Inventory: none - pure IT-services/consulting business, no inventory line on the balance sheet; zeros are genuine. EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A ('Depreciation, amortization and other'), both taken from 10-K/XBRL: op income 9,367/8,810/9,596/10,226 + D&A 2,088/2,281/2,168/2,442; matches stockanalysis.com's EBITDA series to the million (cross-check passed). PPE is the filing's 'Property and equipment, net' line (SEC XBRL PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet); aggregators show ~$4.3B because they bundle operating-lease ROU assets - filing line used here. Shareholders' equity is the balance-sheet 'Total shareholders' equity' INCLUDING noncontrolling interests (parent-only: 22,106/25,693/28,289/31,195 - NCI ~$0.6-1.0B, doesn't change any threshold). Long-term debt was de minimis (~$43-79M) through FY2024; FY2025's $5,034M is Accenture's debut $5B senior unsecured notes (Accenture Capital, issued Oct 2024, maturities 2027-2034) - LTD/EBITDA still only ~0.40. FY2025 retained-earnings decline (23,082→21,019) alongside treasury-stock decline (10,565→7,752) reflects treasury-share retirement charged against RE plus dividends - a buyback accounting artifact, not an operating loss (FY2025 net income ~$7.9B). R&D flag: NO R&D expense line on the income statement (format is cost of services / S&M / G&A); note Accenture does disclose ~$1.2-1.5B annual R&D investment in narrative - line-item reading used, same convention as prior assessments (cf. CHD/ITW). Preferred: authorized but none issued or outstanding. Treasury stock: present ($7.8B, active buyback program).

DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 14.2% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.31 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 3.2 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.07 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 21.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 25.6% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Sharp drawdown: ~$113, down ~29% over the last 30 days despite 6% Q1-2026 revenue growth. The market is punishing the funding side of Akamai's pivot - legacy CDN delivery segment still shrinking (-7% y/y) while Cloud Infrastructure Services grows 40%, funded by billions in new convertible notes and accelerated AI-infrastructure capex (visible in the FY2025 LTD jump to $4.1B). Analyst consensus remains Buy (consensus target ~$177 vs $113 price; BofA upgraded to Buy, $175 target, citing large cloud infrastructure wins). Assignment case: profitable, cash-generative (OCF $1.5B, RSH ~26%), buying back stock, entering at a leverage/transition-fear discount - celebratable if the DCAP verdict is a gift, but the rising convertible leverage and shrinking legacy segment are the honest risks. No takeover/breakup overhang found.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fiscal year ends Dec 31. No inventory line - CDN/security/cloud services company; 0s are genuine absence. EBITDA = operating income + D&A, both from SEC XBRL (OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization; D&A largely sits in cost of revenue, so the income-statement D&A line alone understates it); matches stockanalysis.com cited EBITDA to within rounding. PPE is the 10-K net PP&E tag (PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet), EXCLUDING operating-lease right-of-use assets (stockanalysis bundles ROU assets, showing ~$3.8B FY2025 vs $2.3B per 10-K). Long-term debt = noncurrent convertible senior notes (ConvertibleLongTermNotesPayable); the FY2024 dip to $2,397M and the FY2024 current-liability spike to $2,091M are the same event - $1,149M of 2025 convertible notes reclassified to current before maturity, not an operating deterioration. FY2024/FY2025 operating cash flow nearly identical ($1,519.2M vs $1,518.8M) - verified real from SEC, not a data echo. Treasury stock first appears FY2024 ($558.5M), $434.8M FY2025; buybacks are active every year ($690-924M/yr per cash-flow statement; pre-2024 repurchases were retired/absorbed rather than held). Preferred stock authorized (700K shares) but none issued or outstanding.

Sentiment: Constructive. Buy consensus (17 analysts), avg PT ~$102-112 vs ~$100 price after an 18% 90-day recovery; management's 2026 outlook well received. Cautions: competitive pressure in consumer staples and limited organic growth (~3%/yr forecast). Boring-but-dominant staples portfolio (Arm & Hammer, OxiClean, TheraBreath, Hero) - assignment at a discount would be celebratable. Next earnings Jul 31, 2026.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026. EBITDA = operating income + D&A, both from SEC EDGAR XBRL (matches stockanalysis exactly: 816.8/1282.6/1046.2/1325.0). Retained earnings cross-checked EDGAR vs stockanalysis - exact match. EBITDA dips in FY2022 and FY2024 reflect non-cash impairments (Flawless ~$411M in 2022; vitamins/vitafusion ~$357M in 2024), not operating decay - NOCF rose every year (885→1215). JUDGMENT FLAG for operators: rnd_expense marked "absent" per the literal rule (no R&D line on the consolidated income statement - R&D sits inside SG&A) but the 10-K notes disclose real R&D spend: $110.0M/$122.4M/$139.7M/$145.6M FY22-25 (~2.3% of sales, tagged in XBRL). If the spirit-of-Buffett reading is preferred, this flag flips. Preferred: 2.5M shares authorized, none issued (no XBRL preferred-outstanding tag exists). Treasury stock present and growing ($2,665M→$3,665M abs) - active buyback history.

Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -36.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 81.2% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Mixed-to-recovering. 2025 was Chipotle's roughest year in a while: comparable sales fell ~2.5% (first meaningful traffic decline in years) amid consumer pullback, and the stock is down heavily, ~$32-34 post-50:1-split. Leadership transition: Brian Niccol left for Starbucks (2024), Scott Boatwright now CEO. But early 2026 shows a turn -- Q1 2026 comps +0.5% on +0.6% transactions (return to positive traffic). Analysts constructive: ~28/39 Buy, Strong Buy consensus, median target ~38% upside; bull case on high-protein menu, equipment upgrades, AI rewards relaunch, $1.7B buyback authorization, and 350-370 new stores in 2026 toward a 7,000-store NA target. Elevated valuation is the main pushback. Assignment celebratable on fundamentals (debt-free, ~22% ROA, huge unit-growth runway) if bought at a discount.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Chipotle Mexican Grill, FY ends Dec 31; last 4 FYs FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026). PP&E uses the TRUE 'leasehold improvements, property and equipment, net' XBRL line (1951/2170/2390/2679), NOT aggregator figures (~5254/5749/6390/7142) which fold operating-lease ROU assets into PP&E (the ROU trap, cf. BJ). LTD=0 all years: Chipotle carries no borrowings/notes; its large liabilities are operating-lease liabilities (~$3.8B), which sit in total_liabilities but are not interest-bearing debt. EBITDA = income from operations + D&A, both from the 10-K/press releases (2297 = 1936+361 FY2025), cross-checked vs stockanalysis EBITDA. TREASURY RETIREMENT: Chipotle retired ALL treasury stock in Q2 2024 (treasury_stock_abs 4282->4945->0->0); this crushed retained earnings (6057->1574->620) even though the company earns $1.6B+ net income/yr -- the RE collapse and 'absent' treasury flag are buyback-retirement artifacts, not deterioration. This zeros RE (3 pts) and TS (1 pt) and, by removing the treasury cushion, likely floors TSA-DSER. TS flag is the classic operator-call lever (cf. MSI/SHW): CMG buys back aggressively ($1.7B authorization remaining) but retires shares, so 'presence of treasury stock' reads absent. Goodwill flat at 21.94 all 4 yrs (no acquisitions) -> GW 0. No inventory concern (restaurant food inventory, small and rising). 50:1 stock split June 2024; stock ~$32-34, wheel-cheap.

GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Constructive. Buy consensus (~13 analysts, May 2026); Emerson raised FY2026 adjusted-EPS guidance ($4.78-$4.93, ~5.5% sales growth) on robust industrial-automation and software demand. Completed full buy-in of AspenTech (2025), simplifying the story around automation + AI decision-support software. Main debate is AI-related competitive risk vs. software/automation profitability inflection; price targets range ~$162-$205. A quality, dominant automation franchise - assignment would be celebratable - but the DCAP gift margin is thin and leans on a one-time divestiture gain (see notes).
research notes - methodology & caveats
Emerson Electric (industrial automation + AspenTech software). FY ends Sept 30; window FY2022-FY2025. EBITDA built from SEC XBRL as pretax earnings from continuing ops + net interest expense + D&A (FY22 2432+194+842; FY23 2903+34+1051; FY24 2020+175+1689; FY25 2934+237+1518). FY2024 EBITDA dips slightly (3,884 < FY23 3,988) because FY2024 pretax was depressed by National Instruments acquisition deal costs / heavier intangible amortization (D&A jumped to 1,689); a standardized/adjusted EBITDA (stockanalysis: 3534/4292/5432/5934) rises steadily and would flip I&NE's rise point +1 (12/17). Used reported figures faithfully. shareholders_equity = COMMON/parent stockholders' equity (matches Emerson's 'Common stockholders' equity' line: FY25 20,282); total equity incl. AspenTech NCI is higher (FY22 16,316 ... FY25 20,298) - do NOT use that. AspenTech minority was ~43% public until Emerson bought in the remainder (completed ~Mar 2025), which is why total equity fell FY25. Portfolio-transformation window: Climate Technologies (Copeland) majority sold to Blackstone May 2023 ($14B deal, ~$8.4B after-tax gain) and NI acquired Oct 2023. R&D flag = 'present': Emerson discloses material, rising R&D (385/523/781/771) in the notes, though not on the income-statement face. NOCF is GAAP total from the cash-flow statement; FY2023 is genuinely $637M (large cash tax payment on the Copeland gain) - not an error. WATCH: RE growth (2/3 tier, avg ~14.7% YoY) rides the FY2023 +42.8% jump that is entirely the ~$8.4B Copeland divestiture gain; strip that artifact and RE -> ~0/3, dropping the score below the 60% gift line (~9/17). This is an operator-judgment gift. EP (0/1) and RTA (0/2) fail structurally on large current liabilities/commercial paper and a goodwill-heavy asset base. Rescore after the FY2026 10-K when the divestiture year rolls out of the window.

Sentiment: Positive-to-neutral. Q1 2026 net sales +12% on share gains and broad-based demand; FY2025 recovered +9%. Analysts mixed on valuation, not quality: Jefferies upgraded to Buy (PT $52, Dec 2025), Baird Outperform (PT $50), DA Davidson initiated Neutral (PT $46, June 2026) - stock at ~$48 trades ~43x earnings, so the debate is price, not moat. Consensus forecasts ~8-9% annual earnings/revenue growth. Watch items: Q2 earnings July 13-14, 2026 and the CEO handoff (Jeff Watts) July 16, 2026 - a planned, well-telegraphed succession. Nothing that would make assignment un-celebratable: dominant fastener/MRO distributor with onsite/vending moat, near-zero debt, no goodwill, 1.9% dividend.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY2022-FY2025, calendar fiscal year (ends Dec 31), USD millions. EBITDA = operating income + D&A from EDGAR XBRL (OI 1453.6/1528.7/1510.0/1655.7 + D&A 176.6/177.3/175.4/179.2); matches stockanalysis.com EBITDA (1630/1706/1685/1835). PP&E is 10-K net PP&E per EDGAR XBRL - stockanalysis shows higher figures because it bundles operating-lease right-of-use assets. Total liabilities from stockanalysis, verified against EDGAR by identity (assets − equity matches to 0.1M each year). Goodwill: no goodwill line on Fastenal's balance sheet, never XBRL-tagged (distributor, organic growth) - genuine 0s. Treasury stock: no treasury line ever tagged; Fastenal retires repurchased shares, so buyback history exists but the TS parameter's balance-sheet test finds nothing. Preferred: authorized but 0 issued/outstanding all 4 years (EDGAR PreferredStockValue=0). No R&D line on income statement (distribution business). Retained earnings YoY: +4.3%, +7.6%, +7.0% - avg ~6.3%, likely just under the 7% bar (heavy dividend payout ~78% of net income suppresses RE growth). Q2 FY2026 earnings due July 13-14, 2026 (one week out); CEO transition to Jeff Watts with Dan Florness leaving the board July 16, 2026.

Sentiment: Bullish long-term consensus but a live operational overhang. Strong Buy consensus (~30 analysts, median PT ~$70, range $30-81) on copper's electrification/AI-datacenter demand leverage and a strong balance sheet. BUT the Sept-2025 Grasberg Block Cave mud rush (fatalities + production loss) is still unwinding: FCX cut 2026 copper sales guidance to ~3.1B lbs and gold to ~650k oz; Grasberg only ~60-65% of nameplate in H2-2026, ~80% mid-2027, full recovery not until late 2027. Morgan Stanley downgraded to Equal Weight (PT $66) on the slower ramp; stock fell ~11% on the output cut. Commodity-price and single-asset (Grasberg) concentration risk make this more volatile than a staples/utility name -- assignment celebratable on the franchise, but expect drawdown chop through the Grasberg recovery.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Freeport-McMoRan, world's largest public copper producer (copper/gold/moly); Dec 31 FY end. ALL figures from SEC 10-K primaries (FY2025 10-K for FY2024-25 balance sheet, FY2023 10-K for FY2022-23). EBITDA = operating income + DD&A (cash-flow line), both cited from SEC income statement & cash-flow statement, cross-checked vs stockanalysis (identical). NCI TRAP: FCX carries huge noncontrolling interests (PT-FI Indonesia): FY2025 total EQUITY 30,766 = parent stockholders' equity 18,899 + NCI 11,867. I recorded shareholders_equity as the PARENT 'Total stockholders' equity' subtotal (15,555/16,693/17,581/18,899) since RE and treasury are parent-section items; aggregators (stockanalysis) report the 30,766 total-equity figure, which would understate leverage/RSH. GOODWILL = 0 all years: FCX balance sheet carries NO goodwill line (verified in SEC BS). Inventory = total CURRENT inventories (product + materials&supplies + mill/leach stockpiles); excludes non-current long-term stockpiles. No R&D line (exploration expense instead) -> rnd absent. No preferred line -> absent. Treasury present (191M shares, $6,024M). WATCH: RE series is an accumulated-deficit sign-flip (-3,907 -> +1,385) -- the RE 3-pt award rides deficit-shrink/low-base growth math (same artifact as CAH/AON/BSX/CEG); strip those and score drops ~3pts to not-a-gift, an operator judgment call. RTA is a razor-miss (~10.7% avg NOCF/assets, <11% tier). TSA-DSER ~1.14 avg on parent SE (fails). EP passes 4x (miner EBITDA >> current liab, unlike working-cap-heavy names).

Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative1/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.84 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 7.1%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)

Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 195.2% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Buy consensus (23 analysts, Jun 2026) but price targets trimmed to ~$375-445 on weaker consumer demand, pressured margins (operating margin guided ~12.4-12.6%, down from mid-teens) and a soft housing/big-ticket backdrop. FY2026 guide: comps ~flat to +2%, EPS ~flat to +4%. Q1 FY2026 hinted at early stabilization. Strategy: aggressive Pro-ecosystem M&A (SRS, GMS, Mingledorff's) to attack the ~$700B Pro market -- boosts scale but has levered the balance sheet and diluted returns near-term. A dominant, wide-moat category killer we would be glad to own on assignment; the risk is cyclical, not franchise.
research notes - methodology & caveats
HD fiscal year ends late Jan / early Feb. Window = HD fiscal 2022-2025, period-ends Jan 29 2023, Jan 28 2024, Feb 2 2025 (53-wk), Feb 1 2026; latest 10-K (hd fiscal 2025) filed ~Mar 2026. EBITDA built as operating income + cash-flow D&A (per spec): OI 24039/21689/21526/20890 + D&A 2975/3247/3761/4121. SRS Distribution acquisition (~$18.25B, closed mid-2024) drove the step-up in goodwill (8.5B->19.5B), total assets (76.5B->96.1B) and LTD in fiscal 2024; GMS + Mingledorff's acquisitions further lifted goodwill to 22.3B in fiscal 2025. No R&D expense line (costs sit in SG&A). No preferred stock. Large treasury stock (~$96B) from decades of buybacks keeps common equity thin but POSITIVE all 4 years (1562/1044/6640/12813), so TSA-DSER applies (not excused). Retained earnings YoY growth avg ~7.1% -- razor-thin over the 7% tier. RE and equity/OCF cross-checked against the SEC 10-K (RE 89,533 @ Feb 2 2025 and 83,656 @ Jan 28 2024; SE 6,640/1,044; fiscal-2025 OCF $16.3B); operating income cross-checked against stocktitan (20,900/21,500/21,700/24,000/23,000 by period-end year).

HWM - Howmet Aerospace Inc. GIFT

Aerospace - Engine Components/Fasteners · price $277.91 · mcap $111.2B · vol 4,744,737 · PE 62.7 · PB 19.6 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 11.2% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.33 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.2 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.46 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 58.7%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 43.5% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Strongly positive. HWM is a high-quality aero-engine components supplier riding the commercial aerospace up-cycle (Boeing/Airbus build-rate recovery + record aftermarket/spares demand). FY2025 delivered record revenue, EBITDA 2329M, operating cash flow up ~45% to 1884M, LTD paid down to 2859M, preferred redeemed, and a $700M buyback. Analyst tone bullish; management raising guidance. Stock has been a strong multi-year performer. No governance or accounting red flags; assignment here would be celebratable. Only overhang is valuation/multiple after a large run and cyclicality of aerospace demand.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Howmet Aerospace (aerospace: engine products, fastening systems, engineered structures, forged wheels). Calendar fiscal year (Jan-Dec); FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026, so window is FY2022-FY2025. EBITDA built as operating income + D&A and reconciles across all four years (FY22 919+265=1184; FY23 1203+272=1475; FY24 1633+277=1910; FY25 2046+283=2329). EQUITY STRUCTURE (per FY2025 SEC annual report): common 402 + additional capital 2531 + retained earnings 4093 + AOCI (1673) = total 5353, NO treasury stock line -> Howmet RETIRES repurchased shares (approx 4.4M retired on the $700M FY2025 buyback), so treasury_stock_abs=0 all years and TS flag 'absent' despite an active buyback history (same retirement artifact as GOOGL/CMG). PREFERRED: the $3.75 Cumulative Preferred (546,024 shares) was fully REDEEMED Dec 17 2025 ($55M), so preferred is absent as of the latest filing -> PS point earned. R&D expense line IS present (FY25 $37M) -> costs the I&NE no-R&D half. Watch items for the scorer: EP likely fails on FY2022 only (EBITDA 1184 < current liab 1482, a post-COVID aero recovery-trough artifact; EBITDA clears current liabilities comfortably FY23-FY25). RE growth is GENUINE (all-positive, earnings-driven 1028->4093, ~59%/yr avg -> no sign-flip artifact). TSA-DSER weak: avg TL/SE ~1.46 (aerospace balance sheet still debt-laden post-Arconic split, though LTD paid down 4162->2859). Goodwill essentially flat (~4.0B, dipped FY24 to 4010 then 4022) -> GW point is marginal.

GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Solid and steady, not exciting: latest quarter revenue +4.6% YoY, EPS beat, FY2026 GAAP EPS guidance RAISED $0.10 to $11.10-$11.50 (+8% at midpoint). Consensus "Hold", avg price target ~$275; analysts split (some cite premium multiple vs modest organic growth, reliance on buybacks). A+ credit, 26%+ operating margins, dividend aristocrat ($6.44/yr, Q2 dividend paid Jul 10 2026). No scandals, litigation overhangs, or structural threats found - assignment at a discount to a 26%-margin, buyback-and-dividend machine would be celebratable. Main knock is valuation, which a below-market put strike partly addresses.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026 (SEC accession 000004982626000008). EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A (FY2022: 3790+410; FY2023: 4040+395; FY2024: 4264+402; FY2025: 4216+397) - matches stockanalysis.com EBITDA line exactly, and 10-K confirms op income 4,216 (2025) / 4,264 (2024). Retained earnings ("Income reinvested in the business") and treasury stock confirmed for ALL 4 years directly from the 10-K equity statement. OPERATOR FLAG (decisive, CHD-style): ITW's income statement carries R&D only inside a COMBINED "Selling, administrative, and research and development expenses" line ($2.78B in 2025) - no standalone R&D line. Recorded "present" (R&D is literally named on the face; ITW is a patent-heavy engineering house). If operators prefer the strict "no standalone R&D line" reading, flip to absent (+1 pt on I&NE). Preferred stock: 0.3M shares authorized, unissued, no commitment to issue → absent. Treasury stock $26.9B with active buybacks ($1.5B repurchased in 2025, ~$2.0B remaining authorization). 2026-Q4 note: $999M of 2.65% notes due Nov 2026 reclassified LTD→short-term in Q4 2025.

Sentiment: Strongly positive. Largest pharma by market cap; consensus Buy, targets raised into July 2026 (Cantor $1,350 on 7/6; JPMorgan bullish 7/7 on Mounjaro intl expansion). FY2026 revenue guidance raised to $82-85B. Zepbound beat Wegovy head-to-head; oral pill orforglipron (Foundayo) FDA-approved Apr 2026 and on Medicare GLP-1 Bridge at $50/mo copay, opening a large oral market. Novo Nordisk guiding to a 2026 sales DECLINE amid Lilly share gains. Assignment here would be celebrated: a dominant, still-compounding franchise. Risks to monitor: GLP-1 pricing/reimbursement pressure, competition (Novo, Viking), and a rich valuation.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Eli Lilly, FY ends Dec 31. Balance-sheet, PP&E-net, OCF, RE, goodwill, inventory, LTD, current liabilities all pulled from SEC XBRL company-facts (primary). shareholders_equity = PARENT Eli Lilly shareholders' equity (excludes noncontrolling interest), per house rule. EBITDA = operating income + income-statement D&A (stockanalysis, cross-checked vs macrotrends: 8650/7985/14666/28299). Carries R&D expense line (pharma) -> costs I&NE +1. treasury_stock is a small (~$44-50M) deferred-comp share line, genuinely present but immaterial vs equity; FY2025 not separately re-tagged in XBRL so carried flat at 49.5 (does not move TSA-DSER, which fails hard on pharma leverage regardless). RE compounding is GENUINE (Mounjaro/Zepbound earnings explosion), not a sign-flip or one-timer artifact: 10043->10312->13545->24470. Expected drags are structural large-cap-pharma: EP fails (current liabilities > EBITDA all 4 yrs, like JNJ), TSA-DSER fails (TL/SE ~4x), RTA below 17% tier, I&NE (R&D + FY23 EBITDA dip). PP&E rising fast on GLP-1 capacity buildout but no single year >60% YoY.

Sentiment: Celebration-worthy. Record Q1 2026: net sales $2.35B, +27% YoY, international +44.9% riding Coca-Cola's global distribution (China, India); beat revenue est ~9%, EPS ~10%. Buy consensus (17-24 analysts); Morgan Stanley OW, PT raised to $103. Stock at 52-week high (~$99, 7/7/2026) after recovering off the ~$58 Aug-2025 low. Two-year avg operating margin ~28.4%; fortress balance sheet, effectively debt-free. Cautions: rich valuation (PE ~47), input-cost/tariff pressure, and energy-drink category-shift/execution risk. Fundamentally a business we'd be glad to own at the strike.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Monster Beverage (energy drinks); Dec fiscal year, last 4 filed FYs. EBITDA = SEC-XBRL operating income (1584.7/1953.4/1930.3/2419.4) + cash-flow D&A (61.2/68.9/80.4/114.4); matches stockanalysis EBITDA series (cross-checked). Asset-light: outsources manufacturing to co-packers, distributes through Coca-Cola system; still carries inventory. Essentially debt-free -- LTD is $0 in FY22/FY23/FY25; the $373.95M at FY2024 is a residual term loan drawn to fund the June-2024 ~$3B modified-Dutch-auction tender, repaid by FY2025 (SEC-XBRL LongTermDebtNoncurrent). NO R&D expense line on the income statement. Watch items for the scorer: (1) RE dips FY22->FY23 (9,001->5,940) is a genuine treasury-retirement charge -- treasury fell $4.0B (6,600->2,566) and RE was charged ~$3.1B in 2023; not an earnings problem (net income positive every year), but avg YoY RE growth is dragged to ~5.7% by that -34% year. (2) PP&E jumps +72% FY22->FY23 (516.9->890.8) as Monster brought manufacturing/AFF capacity in-house -- a real >60% spike. (3) Goodwill steps down FY23->FY24 (1,418->1,332), a Bang/other adjustment, so not increasing. RE/treasury/LTD/operating-income all verified against SEC EDGAR XBRL (CIK 865752); balance-sheet/cash-flow lines from stockanalysis.com.

Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 51.4% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Cautiously optimistic but overhung. Central risk: Keytruda US patent cliff late 2028 (~half of pharma revenue, ~$25B at risk) - the reason the stock trades near a decade-low valuation. Narrative in mid-2026 shifting from 'Keytruda problem' to 'credible post-Keytruda bridge' (Winrevair, Capvaxive, subcutaneous Keytruda QLEX, Enlicitide, animal health); Q1'26 Keytruda +8% y/y. Analyst consensus Buy (19 buy / 10 hold of 29), avg PT ~$116.70; several rate Hold after a strong YTD rally that has caught up to fair value. Fundamentals durable (74.8% gross margin, 28% net margin, $12.4B FCF FY25); assignment celebratable at a discount, but the 2028 cliff is a genuine multi-year cloud to flag for a human call.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Merck & Co, FY ends Dec 31; latest 10-K FY2025 filed 2026-02-24. FY2023 is the dominant anomaly: Prometheus (~$10.2B) + other IPR&D/acquisition charges crushed operating income to $2,355M (EBITDA $6,227M via OI+D&A) and R&D spiked to $30,531M; this breaks I&NE (inventory dips FY24, EBITDA non-steady) and EP (EBITDA<current liab FY22 & FY23). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (stockanalysis). RE dropped FY23 (61081->53895) on the earnings collapse then recovered; avg YoY growth ~7.0% rides the RE 7% tier razor-thin (like [[hd-gift-thin-equity-re-knifeedge]] / [[chkp-near-miss-re-7pct]]) - if RE point drops it falls to ~10/17 = 58.8% not-gift, an operator call. Preferred authorized but none outstanding = absent. Goodwill essentially flat (~21.2-21.7B), FY25 dips slightly vs FY24 but stays >98% and newest>oldest. LTD light (avg LTD/assets ~0.30, LTD/EBITDA latest ~1.7). Big R&D line is inherent to pharma, not a red flag operationally but zeros the I&NE no-R&D bonus.

NBIX - Neurocrine Biosciences GIFT

Health Technology · price $174.10 · mcap $17.5B · vol 1,171,213 · PE 26.8 · PB 5.1 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA both rose steadily; carries an R&D expense line
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 14.8% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.00 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.43 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 537.7%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 20.0% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Positive/growth. Buy consensus (23 Buy / 3 Hold / 0 Sell of ~34 analysts, June 2026), median PT ~$170. Q1 2026 revenue +42%; Ingrezza guided $2.7-2.8B FY26, Crenessity ramping; total revenue projected +31% to ~$3.75B FY26. EPS estimate raised to ~$5.08. Profitable commercial franchise with visible earnings growth. Main watch item: April 2026 $2.8B Soleno acquisition (Vykat XR) - integration + goodwill/leverage risk. No red flags that would make assignment un-celebratable; a durably profitable, debt-free CNS-focused biopharma at a discount would be a welcome own.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Neurocrine Biosciences (Dec FY-end), commercial-stage biopharma; lead drug Ingrezza (tardive dyskinesia) + Crenessity (CAH). DEBT-FREE: no bonds/notes/term loans in any of the 4 years (2024 convertible notes settled before FY22); long_term_debt=0 across all years. The ~$439M 'debt' shown by third-party aggregators is operating-lease liabilities (noncurrent op-lease 415.3M FY25), NOT financial debt. PP&E is TRUE net property & equipment from the 10-K (58.6/70.8/82.6/89.8) - stockanalysis/aggregators bundle right-of-use lease assets into PP&E (ROU trap: ROU assets were 455.4M FY25, shown separately); using the ROU-inflated series would falsely spike PPE and mislabel the parameter. Carries inventory (biologic/drug product), rising. No preferred (0 issued/outstanding). No treasury stock: shares issued = shares outstanding (100.1M=100.1M FY25). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (OI 249.0/250.9/570.5/619.1 from 10-K; D&A 15.6/21.3/27.1/30.1). RETAINED EARNINGS is a genuine profitability inflection, NOT a buyback artifact: accumulated deficit -406.8 -> -157.1 -> +29.2 -> +447.7 as the company turned durably profitable; unlike deficit-shrink names (LYV/AON) FY24/FY25 are genuinely positive and compounding. The YoY-growth sign-flip may still over-reward RE 3/3 - flag for operator. Goodwill FY25 marked null: not separately stated on the FY2025 balance sheet (folded into 'Other noncurrent assets' 59.5M); it was an immaterial 5.4/5.8/5.7M FY22-24 and already dipped FY23->FY24, so GW does not cleanly increase regardless. R&D expense present and large (463.8/708.9/743.6/1033M) so I&NE forfeits its no-R&D point. SHELF LIFE: NBIX agreed to acquire Soleno Therapeutics (~$2.8B, Vykat XR) in April 2026 -> FY2026 will inject goodwill and possibly debt; rescore FY2026.

ODFL - Old Dominion Freight Line Inc. GIFT

Transportation - LTL Trucking · price $216.44 · mcap $45.0B · vol 1,691,485 · PE 45.5 · PB 10.3 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years1/1EBITDA covered current liabilities in every one of the 4 years
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 29.7% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.01 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.30 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 6.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)

Sentiment: Hold consensus (~17 analysts, Jun 2026); fair-value nudged up to ~$240. Best-in-class LTL operator leaning on pricing power (May 2026 revenue/day +12.3% YoY) to offset weak tonnage (-3.8% tons/day) through the freight recession. 2026 shows early signs of a modest freight rebound and tightening LTL capacity, positioning ODFL to capture demand. Overhangs: Amazon's entry into LTL, insider selling, and a rich multiple. Fundamentals pristine (near debt-free, ~30% NOCF/assets, no dilution) - assignment would be celebratable; the DCAP drag is structural buyback mechanics, not business quality.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Old Dominion Freight Line, LTL trucker, Dec FY-end. No inventory line (services/asset-heavy trucking carries no merchandise inventory) - genuine 0s, confirmed on face of FY2025 10-K balance sheet. No R&D line (trucking). No preferred. TREASURY = absent because ODFL RETIRES repurchased shares (NC law) rather than holding them - there is no treasury-stock line; buybacks reduce common stock + retained earnings directly. This retirement is also why RE growth is suppressed: FY22->23 +17.9%, FY23->24 -0.2%, FY24->25 +1.5% (avg ~6.4%, just below the 7% tier) despite strong earnings - capital returned via buyback rather than retained. GOODWILL is a constant $19.463M held within 'Other assets' on the face (no separate line); flat since 2008, no acquisitions, so GW param scores 0 (not increasing). LTD figures are the non-current 'Long-term debt' line only (excludes $20M/yr current maturities); ODFL is essentially debt-free and winding down its term loan (80->60->40->20). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (FY22 1841+276, FY23 1641+324, FY24 1544+345, FY25 1361+365); EBITDA declining on freight-recession tonnage even as margins hold, so I&NE earnings-rise leg fails. RE cross-checked against SEC XBRL primary (3397.3/4004.6/3995.2/4055.6) and stockanalysis; balance-sheet totals tie to the FY2025 10-K to the thousand.

Energy · price $87.43 · mcap $55.1B · vol 3,968,920 · PE 15.7 · PB 2.5 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years1/1EBITDA covered current liabilities in every one of the 4 years
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)1/2avg debt/assets 0.49 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 4.2 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.95 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 589.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 32.1% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Constructive-to-bullish. Analyst consensus Strong Buy / Buy, median PT ~$87 (range $72-114) across ~33 analysts as of mid-2026. 2026 guidance: adjusted EBITDA $7.9-8.3B, net income $3.19-3.71B; ~18% revenue growth on higher gas gathering/processing volumes; capex $2.7-3.2B on Permian/NGL/refined-products expansion. ~$475M cumulative EnLink+Medallion synergies realized by YE2025. Watch item: managing three large integrations (Magellan, EnLink, Medallion) simultaneously is operationally complex and leverage (LTD/EBITDA ~4.2x) is elevated post-M&A; a synergy miss could weigh on the stock. Assignment would be celebratable - a dominant Permian/NGL midstream franchise bought at a discount, wheelable with covered calls.
research notes - methodology & caveats
ONEOK - natural-gas/NGL midstream (gathering, processing, pipelines, refined products). FY ends Dec. Carries modest inventory (NGL/refined products), so I&NE inventory series is genuine, not zeroed. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (stockanalysis, corroborated by stocktitan). Major M&A drives the balance-sheet ballooning: Magellan Midstream closed Sep-2023 (refined-products, drove the +64% PP&E jump FY22->FY23 that trips the PPE spike rule, and the goodwill step 528->4,952), EnLink + Medallion closed Oct-2024 (assets/goodwill jump to ~66B/8.1B), EnLink public-unit buy-in completed Jan-2025. shareholders_equity is TOTAL stockholders' equity (assets-liabilities, balances the sheet); FY2024's $22,133M includes ~$5B EnLink noncontrolling interest (ONEOK-attributable ~$17B per stocktitan) which collapses back in FY2025 after the buy-in - that NCI inflates the FY2024 SE used in TSA-DSER/RSH. RE grows 50->868->1,579->2,373; the 3/3 RE growth rides an unusually low FY2022 base ($50M) so the YoY% is explosive but the dollar growth is real. Goodwill dips slightly FY25 (8,091->8,058) on EnLink PPA finalization. No R&D line (midstream). No preferred outstanding. Treasury stock present (~$829M).

Consumer Non-Durables · price $149.31 · mcap $347.7B · vol 9,502,311 · PE 22.1 · PB 6.6 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)2/2inventory & EBITDA both rose steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 14.7% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.20 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.1 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.40 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding0/1preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 5.0%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 44.6% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Neutral-to-cautious. Stock near two-year lows in mid-2026; consensus 'Buy' but ~50% of analysts at Hold, with price-target trims. FY2026 headwinds: ~$400M after-tax (~$1B pretax) tariff/commodity costs, flat organic sales, and declining core gross/operating margins in the Q2-FY26 print. Restructuring underway (up to 7,000 non-manufacturing roles cut, ~$2B program cost) plus a CEO transition. Capital return intact (~$15B FY26: ~$10B dividends + ~$5B buybacks; 60+ year dividend-raise streak). Business quality and balance sheet are undamaged; softness is macro/margin, not solvency. Assignment of a global staples compounder at a drawdown price is celebrate-able.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fiscal year ends June 30 (FY2025 = yr ended 6/30/2025). EBITDA = operating income + D&A from the cash-flow statement (FY22 17,813+2,807; FY23 18,134+2,714; FY24 18,545+2,896; FY25 20,451+2,847). Retained earnings and shareholders' equity taken from the SEC face-of-balance-sheet (RE 112,429/118,170/123,811/129,973); stockanalysis.com nets an ESOP-debt reserve and shows RE ~$700M lower each year, but the 10-K figure is authoritative and RE YoY growth is ~5% either way. Shareholders' equity is total equity including a small (~$0.3B) noncontrolling interest. rnd_expense 'absent' = P&G's income statement carries NO separate R&D line (R&D is embedded in COGS/SG&A), which is a genuine filing fact. preferred_stock 'present' = ESOP Series A/B convertible preferred outstanding (~$777M FY25). Treasury stock is the ~$139B accumulated cost of decades of buybacks - that huge treasury balance is what lets a thin-book-equity staple clear TSA-DSER (denominator SE+|treasury| ~ $191B vs $73B total liabilities). Classic staples drags remain: EP fails all 4 years (payables-heavy current liabilities > EBITDA) and RE growth ~5% (earnings returned via dividends+buybacks, not retained).

DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 16.4% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.13 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.3 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.61 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding

Sentiment: Positive/celebratable. Q1 FY2026 (May 2026) delivered record ~$22.8B TTM revenue; raised full-year EPS guidance; hiked dividend. Analyst consensus Buy (16 analysts, ~$220 avg target as of June 2026). Q2 guide comps +6-7%. Watch item: tariff/distribution cost pressure on margins - Ross has filed tariff-refund claims but excluded refunds from guidance (conservative). Off-price model benefits from trade-down in soft consumer environment; no company-specific overhang. Assignment at a discount would be welcome.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Off-price apparel/home retailer; fiscal year ends late Jan/early Feb (FY2022 ended 2023-01-28 ... FY2025 ended 2026-01-31; latest 10-K = fiscal 2025). All balance-sheet, cash-flow and income figures from SEC XBRL companyfacts (exact filed values). EBITDA = us-gaap OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization; cross-checked against stockanalysis.com EBITDA (2385/2727/3032/3217) - matches. PPE = us-gaap PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (true owned PP&E, EXCLUDES operating-lease ROU assets; stockanalysis inflates net PP&E to ~6.3-7.6B by folding in ROU - do not use that). Goodwill = 0: Ross carries no goodwill (no Goodwill XBRL tag, confirmed) - GW parameter structurally 0. Total liabilities derived as Assets - StockholdersEquity (no noncontrolling interest); matches stockanalysis. Treasury stock = us-gaap TreasuryStockCommonValue (present, growing buyback history). No preferred outstanding (no PreferredStockValue tag). No R&D line (retailer). Structural floors expected: EP (payables/working-capital - EBITDA < current liabilities every year) and TSA-DSER (large operating-lease liabilities inflate total liabilities ~1.3-1.9x adjusted equity) - the classic lease-heavy retail floor, but strong RE growth (~15-16%/yr), rapid LTD paydown (2.46B->1.02B), zero goodwill and clean equity make it a gift despite those.

SCCO - Southern Copper Corp GIFT

Chemicals & Materials · price $173.87 · mcap $145.0B · vol 1,467,429 · PE 29.1 · PB 12.2 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years1/1EBITDA covered current liabilities in every one of the 4 years
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 20.9% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.34 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.9 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative1/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.81 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -8.9%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 70.9% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Constructive business, mixed-to-cautious Street on VALUATION not quality. Record Q1-2026 net income; 2026 copper guidance raised to ~915kt and ~$16B revenue on a structural global copper deficit (EVs, grid, AI data centers). Fortress low-debt miner (LTD/EBITDA ~0.9x). Un-celebratable flags: (1) rich valuation - Barclays initiated Underweight $148, UBS Sell $145, vs price well above; (2) ~89% controlled by Grupo Mexico - low float, governance/related-party overhang, near-total dividend payout keeps retained earnings shrinking; (3) heavy capex cycle (>$15B planned incl. Tia Maria ~$1.8B, El Arco); (4) Peru political/permitting risk - Tia Maria flagged for possible permit revocation, first output ~2027. Copper cyclicality means EBITDA swings with the metal price.
research notes - methodology & caveats
All figures from SEC EDGAR XBRL (SCCO CIK 1001838), FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (2022: 4435.8+796.3; 2023: 4192.3+833.6; 2024: 5554.7+845.9; 2025: 7001.7+868.4); each year cross-checks stockanalysis EBITDA exactly. PPE = SEC PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet only; SCCO carries a separate 'leachable material, net' line that stockanalysis lumps into PP&E (inflating it to ~$11.5-12.1B) - excluded here so the series reflects true fixed plant. shareholders_equity = total equity incl. ~$60-67M noncontrolling interest (matches balance-sheet 'Total equity' and reconciles TL+SE=TA); parent-only equity is immaterially lower and still positive all 4 yrs. total_liabilities derived as TA - total equity (SCCO does not XBRL-tag a single Liabilities total). No R&D line (only 'Exploration expenses' ~$42-61M). No preferred stock. Treasury stock present (~$2.0-3.1B, declining as shares reissued). Goodwill flat at $41.9M every year (not increasing). Retained earnings DECLINE every year (7702->7034->6840->5797) - SCCO pays out nearly all earnings as dividends to ~89%-owner Grupo Mexico, so RE growth scores 0/3 despite record profits. STOCKANALYSIS FY2025 balance-sheet column is corrupt (TL+SE did not reconcile to TA, LTD/TL misaligned) - SEC used throughout.

DCAP 11/17 (65%)
gift at ≥60%

Sentiment: Stock ~$30.79, down ~40% over the past year with multiple analyst price-target cuts on weak sales growth - but full-year guidance maintained and consensus PT ~$46 (≈44% upside); Simply Wall St flags it as possibly ~30% undervalued post Russell value-index shift, though their DCF says ~$24. Q2 2026 earnings July 23, 2026 (call 10am ET, CEO Hal Lawton / CFO Kurt Barton) - lands inside the 30-45 DTE window for near-term CSP ladders. Fundamentals steady (EBITDA, NOCF, retained earnings all rising through FY2025); the drawdown is sentiment/valuation-driven, not balance-sheet-driven, which is exactly the setup where assignment at a discounted strike is celebratable - but flag the earnings date on any play.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Tractor Supply Co (NASDAQ: TSCO), rural-lifestyle retailer, ~2,300 stores. FY ends last Saturday of December (FY2022 ended 2022-12-31, FY2023 2023-12-30, FY2024 2024-12-28, FY2025 2025-12-27). EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A (SEC XBRL operating income: 1,434.9/1,478.9/1,467.5/1,467.4 + D&A 343.1/393.1/447.2/494.0), matching stockanalysis.com's EBITDA line. Retained earnings and operating income cross-checked to the dollar against SEC XBRL companyconcept API (10-K values). Retailer: no R&D line on income statement (SG&A only). Preferred authorized but none outstanding. Large and growing treasury stock ($4.9B→$6.4B) - active buyback history, shares NOT retired. Goodwill flat FY2023→FY2024 ($269.52M both years), jumped FY2025 to $398.76M (acquisition). EBITDA < current liabilities in all 4 years (retail working-capital structure: big AP/accrued base) - EP likely fails structurally, same pattern as other retailers/staples.

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.46 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.5 years of EBITDA (cap 4)

Sentiment: Fundamentally steady: EBITDA and retained earnings grinding up, equity rebuilding, best-in-class operating ratio among Class I rails. The story is the $85B Norfolk Southern merger: STB accepted the application May 28, 2026 but paused review pending supplemental info due July 27, 2026; decision expected late 2026/2027; rivals (BNSF, CN, CSX, CPKC) filing opposition. Analysts view UNP as the stronger compounder whether or not the deal closes. Assignment at a discount would be celebratable - a dominant duopoly-west railroad - but the merger adds regulatory/integration uncertainty and would rework the balance sheet.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY2022-FY2025, Dec-31 fiscal year end, $ millions, all values as-filed from SEC EDGAR XBRL (10-K data). EBITDA = operating income + depreciation (9,917+2,246 / 9,082+2,318 / 9,713+2,398 / 9,846+2,465), cross-checked against stockanalysis.com EBITDA (exact match all 4 yrs). Retained earnings and NOCF also cross-checked vs stockanalysis (exact match). Inventory = "Materials and supplies" line (MaterialsSuppliesAndOther). PPE = as-filed net properties incl. finance-lease ROU (stockanalysis shows ~1.0-1.7B higher because it adds operating-lease assets). LTD = debt due after one year incl. finance lease obligations. Goodwill: UNP's balance sheet carries NO goodwill line at all (no Goodwill XBRL tag in company facts) - recorded as 0s; the GW-increase parameter cannot score for a goodwill-free railroad. No R&D line (railroad); no preferred outstanding (common stock only on balance sheet); large and growing treasury stock ($58.8B, buybacks every year). MATERIAL: pending $85B Norfolk Southern acquisition (agreement July 2025) - if it closes (~late 2026/2027), the balance sheet transforms (large goodwill + debt added), so this DCAP profile has a shelf life.

Sentiment: Consensus Buy (28-30 analysts: ~43% Strong Buy, ~39% Buy, ~18% Hold, 0% Sell); avg PT ~$234 (~+30% upside), range $180-$272. Stock down ~25% over the past year despite FY2025 revenue +13%, adj. EBITDA +20% to ~$9.9B, EPS +22%, FCF ~$9.1B. Headwinds: management cut FY2026 revenue growth guide from low-double-digit to high-single-digit citing Middle East travel disruption (-2 to -3 pts room-night growth through mid-2026), plus AI-search/agentic-travel competition overhang. Post-split share price (~$180s) makes it wheel-affordable for the first time. Fundamentals durable; drawdown is sentiment/macro, not deterioration.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Booking Holdings (OTA platform) carries no inventory - 0s are genuine (services/marketplace, no goods). FY ends Dec; FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (FY22 5102+451, FY23 5835+504, FY24 7555+591, FY25 8825+623). NEGATIVE shareholders' equity FY2023-FY2025 driven by cumulative treasury stock ($54.3B by FY2025, > total equity base) from aggressive buybacks - accumulated deficit is NOT the cause (retained earnings are strongly positive and rising, +47% over window). This is a buyback-driven negative-book-value name, not a distressed one: EBITDA positive & rising all 4 years (RSH DANGER rule satisfied), NOCF ~$9.4B FY2025. TSA-DSER should be excused (SE negative → max 15). Treasury stock heavily present; no preferred; no separate R&D line (dev costs embedded in product/G&A, typical OTA). Goodwill flat-to-declining (FY22 2807 → FY25 2669), so GW point should not award.

Sentiment: Operationally excellent and improving: Q1 2026 system-wide comparable RevPAR +3.6%, net income $383M (from $300M), Adjusted EBITDA $901M; management RAISED FY2026 guidance to ~2-3% RevPAR growth, $4.02-4.06B Adjusted EBITDA, ~$3.5B capital return. Analyst tone constructive (21% Strong Buy, 43% Buy, 36% Hold, 0% Sell; PT ~$285). Headwind: Middle East conflict est. -0.5% to -1% on FY RevPAR, most acute in Q2. As a business, assignment would be celebratable - dominant capital-light lodging franchisor with pricing power. The DCAP weakness is purely capital-structure engineering (deep negative book equity from relentless buybacks), the same profile as BKNG/DPZ, not an operating problem.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Hilton Worldwide (HLT) - asset-light hotel FRANCHISOR/MANAGER; FY ends Dec 31. All figures from SEC 10-K primary filings (FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026; FY2023 10-K for FY2022 comparatives). Key judgment calls: (1) INVENTORY = 0 four years - Hilton owns almost no hotels; franchise/management model carries no merchandise inventory (no inventory line on the balance sheet). (2) PP&E is the SEC NON-ROU net figure (280/382/411/684); aggregators (stockanalysis) ROU-inflate this to 942/1000/978/1261 by folding in operating-lease right-of-use assets - do NOT use those. Note the FY24->FY25 PP&E jump 411->684 (+66%) is a genuine >60% spike (property acquisitions/Graduate Hotels/NoMad) so the PP&E point will likely be denied. (3) EBITDA = operating income + income-statement D&A (OI 2094/2225/2370/2693 + D&A 162/147/146/177), all SEC-sourced; cross-checks stockanalysis EBITDA (2294/2415/2566/2927, which adds total cash-flow amortization) - same trend, same parameter outcomes either way. (4) SHAREHOLDERS_EQUITY is parent-only 'Total Hilton stockholders deficit' ex-NCI (-1102/-2360/-3727/-5388); deeply NEGATIVE all 4 years from cumulative buybacks -> TSA-DSER excused (max 15) and RSH governed by the DANGER rule (4 yrs positive EBITDA -> passes). ARTIFACT FLAG for operator: RETAINED_EARNINGS is a shrinking accumulated deficit (-5190->-4207->-2822->-1508); the ~33% avg 'growth' is deficit burn-down (net income while equity is negative), not organic RE compounding - if it scores RE 3/3 that is a deficit-shrink artifact (same pattern as CAH/AON/BSX/CEG). Strip it and HLT falls well below the gift line. GOODWILL essentially flat (5032/5052/5035/5081, FY24 dips). EP fails all 4 years - current liabilities (deferred revenue + Hilton Honors loyalty liabilities) exceed EBITDA every year. LTD/EBITDA (12338/2870=4.3) and avg LTD/assets (0.64) both fail. Likely lands right at the 60% (9/15) threshold, gift-or-not hinging entirely on the RE artifact - an operator call, please flag.

LOW - Lowe's Companies GIFT

Retail Trade / Home Improvement · price $223.78 · mcap $125.5B · vol 2,858,726 · PE 19.2 · PB -13.5 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 9/15 (60%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 19.9% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)1/2avg debt/assets 0.76 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 3.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negativeN/Aexcused: shareholders' equity was negative or incomplete in the window, so this test is off the scale (max drops to 15)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 9.0%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1equity is negative (heavy buybacks can do this) but 4 straight years of positive EBITDA back it - DANGER-rule pass
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Home-improvement duopoly #2 behind Home Depot; wide moat, best-in-class retailer. Sentiment mixed-to-cautious: multi-year housing-turnover slump and high mortgage rates have pressured comps (DIY soft, Pro relatively resilient); FY2025 revenue roughly flat-to-down. Management leaning into Pro/online and the ADG acquisition to expand the Pro-installed and building-products TAM. Assignment of 100 shares of LOW at a discount would be genuinely celebratable -- durable franchise, prodigious cash generation (~$9-10B OCF), disciplined capital returns. The negative book equity is engineered (aggressive buybacks), not distress. Main watch items: housing cycle timing and integration of the ADG deal.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Lowe's fiscal years end late Jan/early Feb: FY2022=Feb 3 2023, FY2023=Feb 2 2024, FY2024=Jan 31 2025, FY2025=Jan 30 2026. EBITDA = operating income + D&A, both from SEC XBRL (OI 10159/11557/10466/10153; D&A 1981/1923/1972/2194). Shareholders' equity NEGATIVE all 4 years (buyback/share-retirement model charges repurchases to retained earnings) -> TSA-DSER excused, applicable max 15. PP&E = pure 'Property, less accumulated depreciation' balance-sheet line (18362/17649 for FY25/FY24 from the 10-K); operating-lease ROU assets (4303/3738) are a SEPARATE line and intentionally excluded to avoid the aggregator ROU-inflation trap. No inventory-genuine-zero issue: home-improvement retailer, real inventory (declined FY2023 destock 18532->16894 then rebuilt). No R&D expense line on the income statement. No treasury stock on the balance sheet: LOW retires repurchased shares (like HD/ORLY/MSI), so treasury_stock_abs=0 and TS point structurally fails. Preferred authorized but none outstanding. Goodwill flat at 311 for three years then jumps to 3945 in FY2025 from the Artisan Design Group (interior-finishes installer) acquisition plus Foundation Building Materials-adjacent deals -- an acquisition step-change, not organic goodwill growth. Retained-earnings deficit worsened FY2023 (-14862->-15637) then improved sharply FY2024/FY2025 (-14799->-10839) as earnings outran capital returns; any RE points here ride a still-negative deficit that is shrinking -- flag as the deficit-shrink artifact (cf. HD/CAH). Total assets jump FY2025 (43102->54144) reflects the acquisition + inventory/ROU growth.

Sentiment: Record operations: FY2025 revenue $25.2B, EBITDA ~$1.89B, both all-time highs on a strong live-events cycle. BUT a heavy antitrust overhang makes assignment hard to celebrate: an April 2026 federal JURY found Live Nation/Ticketmaster liable on every antitrust count (monopolization of primary ticketing, illegal bundling; $1.72/ticket damages). A March 2026 DOJ settlement avoids a forced breakup (~$200M to states + structural remedies separating Ticketmaster back-end from its consumer marketplaces), and is in a July 2026 public-review period - but many states are continuing to litigate and court-ordered structural relief (incl. breakup) remains legally possible. Net: fundamentally dominant and growing, but legal/structural tail-risk and a debt-heavy, deficit-legacy balance sheet are genuine reasons for caution.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Live Nation Entertainment (concert promotion, venues, Ticketmaster ticketing, sponsorship). FY ends Dec 31. TWO data traps corrected from primary SEC XBRL: (1) LYV DOES carry inventory (merchandise/concessions), ~$39-57M rising - stockanalysis reported none. (2) PP&E is ROU-inflated at aggregators (BJ trap); used pure XBRL PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (1488/2101/2442/3416), excluding ~$1.5-1.9B operating-lease ROU assets. shareholders_equity is Live Nation stockholders' equity/deficit ATTRIBUTABLE TO PARENT (SEC us-gaap:StockholdersEquity), NOT total equity incl. noncontrolling interests - LYV carries large NCI (~$0.6B permanent + ~$0.7-0.9B redeemable mezzanine). Parent SE negative FY2022 (-368) & FY2023 (-52), flipped positive FY2024 (+173) & FY2025 (+271) - so TSA-DSER should be EXCUSED (max 15) and total_liabilities is not scoring-relevant. RE is a deficit-SHRINK pattern (-2971 -> -1042 accumulated deficit, never positive): any RE points ride the artifact of a shrinking deficit, not genuine positive-RE compounding - strip it and DCAP falls toward a services/leverage floor (cf. AON/HLT/CAH deficit-shrink memories). RSH (1890/271 = 697%) is real but flattered by thin post-deficit equity. EBITDA = SEC operating income + D&A; FY24 EBITDA dipped (1602->1374) so I&NE both-rising likely fails despite rising inventory. No R&D line; no preferred outstanding; treasury stock present (6.9M held FY22-24, 30.4M FY25).

Sentiment: Positive. Record Q1 2026 results (sales +11% y/y, adj EPS +13%); management raised FY2026 adj EPS guidance with high-single-digit sales growth. Analyst consensus Moderate Buy with rising price targets; Deutsche Bank (6/30/26) flagged AME for significant 2027 earnings growth on short-cycle recovery plus the Indicor contribution. No scandal/overhang found; assignment on a quality niche-instruments serial acquirer would be celebratable - the DCAP drags are acquisition-driven balance-sheet noise, not franchise decay.
research notes - methodology & caveats
AMETEK Inc, FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed 2026-02-17 (accession 0001037868-26-000016). All figures from SEC EDGAR XBRL companyconcept API, cross-checked vs stockanalysis.com (exact match on EBITDA and retained earnings). EBITDA = OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationAndAmortization (cash-flow D&A). R&D flag: the consolidated income statement carries NO separate R&D line (only Cost of sales and SG&A - verified in the 10-K R3 statement); however AMETEK discloses R&D expense in the notes (us-gaap:ResearchAndDevelopmentExpense, $236.1M FY2025, ~3.2% of sales) - spirit-of-Buffett reading would flip this flag to present (CHD/ITW precedent, operator call). Current-liabilities spikes in FY2023 ($2.88B) and FY2025 ($2.84B) reflect short-term borrowings/current debt around acquisitions (Paragon Medical ~$2.7B closed Dec 2023), which likely breaks EP. Preferred: authorized but none issued (PreferredStockValue $0 all years). Treasury stock present and growing ($2.54B, 41.4M shares at FY2025).

Sentiment: Bullish fundamentals into mid-2026: record backlog ~$63B (+79% YoY) driven by data-center/AI power-generation demand; CAT plans to triple large reciprocating-engine capacity by 2030. Analyst consensus Buy (19 analysts, median PT ~$932). Main concern is valuation, not quality: ~47x trailing earnings (nearly 2x its 5-yr average), a software-like multiple on a still-cyclical construction/mining/energy business; some analysts flag it needs a 30-50% pullback for margin of safety. Assignment of a dominant industrial franchise at a discounted CSP strike would be celebrated; the cyclicality/valuation argues for a conservative (lower-delta) strike.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT), FY ends Dec 31; last 4 FYs = FY2022-FY2025. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (SEC XBRL: op income 7904/12966/13072/11151; D&A 2219/2144/2153/2262). shareholders_equity = total equity incl. noncontrolling interest (CAT NCI is negligible); all four years positive so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). Retained earnings = CAT's 'Profit employed in the business' line. Treasury stock large and growing (aggressive buybacks). R&D expense line present (1814/2108/2107/2148) -> I&NE loses the no-R&D half-point. Goodwill roughly flat (5288->5321), not a steady rise. Real industrial manufacturer -> carries genuine inventory & PP&E. Every line item cross-verified against SEC EDGAR XBRL companyfacts (CIK 0000018230) AND stockanalysis.com; operating profit/inventory/total assets additionally confirmed by CAT's official 4Q/FY2025 press release.

Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.20 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 6.9%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 32.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Quality franchise, but a transition year. New CEO Nadav Zafrir (appointed Dec 2024) is spending up on R&D and sales and made five tuck-in security acquisitions (Cyberint, Veriti, Lakera-AI); FY2025 revenue grew <7% and FY2026 guidance is only 4-8% (~$2.83-2.95B), with subscription growth 10-14%. Q4 2025 EPS beat but management cut the 2026 revenue outlook, calling near-term drag "transitory." Consensus rating "Buy" but analysts trimmed price targets sharply (avg ~$144-181 from ~$196; range $120 Wells Fargo to $260 TD Cowen); Guggenheim upgraded to Buy $188 on Jul 1 2026. Debt-free balance sheet until the Dec 2025 $2.0B 0% convertible notes (fund buybacks/M&A). Net: a durable, cash-gushing security incumbent being repositioned for growth - not distressed, but decelerating; assignment would be defensible, not a slam-dunk celebration.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Check Point Software (CHKP), Israeli cybersecurity/software vendor, FY ends Dec 31. INVENTORY=0 all years: pure software + security-subscription business, carries no inventory line on the balance sheet (not applicable). EBITDA = GAAP operating income + D&A (depreciation of PP&E + amortization of intangibles) from the cash-flow statement; e.g. FY2025 831.1 op inc + 92.9 D&A = 924.0. R&D expense present (FY2025 $456.7M line) so no-R&D I&NE credit does not apply. CURRENT LIABILITIES are large (~$1.9B) because ~$1.5B is deferred/unearned revenue (subscriptions), the classic software structure that makes EBITDA < current liabilities every year (EP structurally fails, like ADBE/ROP). PP&E NET is genuinely flat ~$81-83M: I deliberately used the SEC balance-sheet figure ($82.9M FY2025), NOT stockanalysis's $242.8M which wrongly bundles operating-lease right-of-use assets (the ROU trap). LONG-TERM DEBT is 0 for FY2022-24 then $1,972.1M in FY2025 = the $2.0B principal 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2030 (closed Dec 8 2025, carried net of issuance costs); still modest at 2.1x EBITDA and within 4 yrs. SHAREHOLDERS' EQUITY positive all 4 years (TSA-DSER applies, max 17). Treasury stock is enormous ($15.6B) from a long, aggressive buyback history; RE grows purely from retained net income (no dividend), so RE increase = net income each year (FY2025 +1,056.9 matches reported NI exactly). GOODWILL series is pure goodwill from stockanalysis; SEC reports goodwill+other-intangibles combined (1,295.5/1,748.5/1,897.1/2,118.5) - both series rise monotonically. WATCH: RE avg YoY growth ~6.93% (6.82%/6.42%/7.54%) sits a hair under the 7% first tier - a genuine razor-thin near-miss on the 3-pt RE parameter; if it cleared 7% the score would move from ~58.8% to ~64.7% (gift). Operator judgment worth noting given how close it is.

Sentiment: Neutral-to-constructive. Consensus rating "Buy"; avg price target ~$96 (range $86-105). FY2026 guidance: net sales +2-6%, organic +1-4%, GAAP gross-margin expansion, double-digit EPS growth; management touts accelerating emerging markets (Asia Pacific) and a 2030 plan. Q1 2026 beat on revenue ($5.32B vs $5.23B est) and adj EPS ($0.97 vs $0.94). Exiting private-label pet food (~20bps drag). No acquisition/litigation overhang that would make assignment un-celebratable; a durable, boring blue-chip staple.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Colgate-Palmolive, consumer-staples (oral/personal/home care + Hill's pet). FY ends Dec 31; all 12 series pulled directly from SEC XBRL (CIK 21665) and match stockanalysis.com. EBITDA = operating income + D&A(from cash flow: 545/567/605/630) = 3438/4551/4873/3936, reconciles to the reported EBITDA line (two-path cross-check). SHAREHOLDERS' EQUITY = parent-only (us-gaap:StockholdersEquity): 401/609/212/54M - positive but very thin; total equity incl. noncontrolling interests is larger (806/957/544/365M). Parent SE stays POSITIVE all 4 yrs, so TSA-DSER is NOT excused (17-pt scale applies) and treasury-adjustment (SE+|treasury| ~25.5-28.5B) makes leverage look modest despite tiny book equity. R&D: no separate R&D line on the income statement (embedded in SG&A) → flag absent. Preferred: none outstanding. Treasury: massive ($25-28B) buyback history. FY2025 operating income dipped ~$960M YoY (4268→3306) on non-cash charges while OCF rose to $4.2B; goodwill fell 3272→3122 (impairment/FX). Structural staples drags to expect: EP fails (current liabilities > EBITDA in FY2022 & FY2025), inventory not on a steady rise (2074→2032), PPE has a FY2024 dip, goodwill declining, and retained-earnings growth only ~2-3%/yr (mature high-payout dividend name) which caps RE.

Sentiment: Positive/high-quality franchise. Buy consensus (~19 analysts, target ~$97, range $90-108) as of Jul 2026. TAVR ~three-quarters of revenue; management raised 2026 guidance (constant-cx sales +9-11%, TAVR +7-9%, adj EPS $2.95-3.05). Tailwind from Medtronic TAVR data issues (analysts see 15-20% share capture), new LAA clip FDA clearance, EVOQUE/PASCAL/SAPIEN M3 adoption, favorable ACC/AHA guidelines. Clean balance sheet, market-leading moat -> assignment would be celebrate-able; the DCAP drag is structural/window-driven, not a business-quality concern.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Edwards Lifesciences (EW): structural-heart / TAVR med-device, FY ends Dec. Carries inventory (device manufacturer). DIVESTITURE-WINDOW CAVEAT: sold Critical Care product group to Becton Dickinson for $4.2B cash, closed Sept 3, 2024, with a ~$3.3B pre-tax gain. That gain inflates FY2024 retained earnings (+$4.2B YoY, 8992->13167) and total assets (9363->13055, from sale cash), so the RE-growth parameter and asset base are one-time-boosted; strip the gain and RE growth is ~13%/yr, not ~24%. Income-statement series (EBITDA, revenue) are stockanalysis continuing-operations restatements (ex-Critical Care), so EBITDA declines/is-flat over the window (1638->1454->1534->1421) despite headline revenue growth. Drags: EP fails FY2025 (curr liab 1814 > EBITDA 1421 as current liabilities surged); I&NE fails (EBITDA not rising + R&D line present); RTA dragged by FY2024 OCF trough of 542 (Critical Care transition + working capital); RSH latest EBITDA/SE ~13.7% (equity inflated by gain). Strengths: near-zero net leverage (LTD ~$597M flat, LTD/assets ~0.05, LTD/EBITDA ~0.42), TSA-DSER very low (~0.21), no preferred, large treasury/buybacks. R&D ~$1B/yr present. Goodwill rose 1164->1769 (Endotronix + acquisitions; JenaValve deal later cancelled). FLAG FOR OPERATOR: any 'gift' verdict would ride the FY2024 one-time Critical-Care gain via the 3-pt RE parameter; rescore after FY2026 10-K (early 2027) when the divestiture year rolls partway out of the window.

gift at ≥60%

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 14.5% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.25 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.6 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.26 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)

Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 214.8% on equity - needs ≥23%
