Sentiment: FY2025 was strong: gross bookings +8% FY (+11% Q4), revenue +8%, Q4 adj EBITDA +32% with margin +368bps; net income $1.29B (+5%). 2026 guide 6-9% revenue growth. Analyst consensus Buy (25-37 analysts), avg PT ~$276-306, 15-20% upside; BofA raised to $306. Dominant overhang: AI disruption to online travel agencies (agentic AI / Google bypassing OTAs) drives peer-multiple compression across EXPE/BKNG; bulls counter that loyalty, service, and supply depth are hard to replicate. Near-term demand risk from geopolitics noted in softer guidance. Business is healthy and growing; the DCAP miss is structural (float model), not distress.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Expedia Group (OTA/online travel), FY ends Dec 31. Last 4 FY: FY2022-FY2025. UNITS USD millions. INVENTORY 0s: OTA carries no inventory (services/marketplace) - genuine zero, not missing. EBITDA = GAAP operating income + cash-flow D&A: FY22 1085+792, FY23 1033+807, FY24 1319+838, FY25 1871+887 = 1877/1840/2157/2758. Matches stockanalysis's reported EBITDA line exactly (cross-check). Note macrotrends shows higher EBITDA (~1840/2762/3156) because it adds back stock-based comp / uses an adjusted definition; I used the clean OI+D&A per spec. PP&E: used SEC XBRL PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (2210/2359/2413/2447), NOT stockanalysis (2573/2716/2718/2743) which inflates with operating-lease ROU / capitalized-software presentation - the aggregator ROU trap. FY25 2447 matches the Q4-25 earnings release. SHAREHOLDERS' EQUITY = PARENT-ONLY (us-gaap:StockholdersEquity, Expedia Group Inc. only): 2283/1534/1557/1284. Expedia carries large non-controlling interest (trivago etc., ~1.24-1.26B); total-equity-incl-NCI is 3728/2786/2799/2547. Aggregators mix the two inconsistently (stockanalysis gave FY22 3728 total but FY24 1557 parent). Parent-only SE is positive all 4 years -> TSA-DSER is NOT excused; 17-pt max applies. RSH uses latest parent SE (1284). TREASURY: absolute value of treasury-stock line (10869/13023/14856/16786) - large ongoing buybacks. Total liabilities not XBRL-tagged; FY24/FY25 (19589/21905) from earnings release, FY22/FY23 (17833/18856) confirmed via Assets - total-equity identity (FY24/FY25 tie out exactly). HUMAN-CALL FLAGS (per directive #4): 1) rnd_expense marked PRESENT: Expedia's GAAP income statement has NO line literally labeled "Research and development," but carries a large "Technology and content" expense (~$1.28B FY25) that is functionally R&D. I took the conservative reading (present -> denies the I&NE point). If operator reads it literally (no "R&D" line -> absent), it would add the I&NE tech point - but does not flip the verdict (see below). 2) RETAINED EARNINGS sign-flip: RE goes -1409 -> -632 -> 602 -> 1696, crossing from accumulated deficit to positive. Any RE-growth % the server computes off a negative base is an artifact, not real durable compounding. Flagging so RE points (if awarded) are treated skeptically. Structural read: this is an OTA float model - deferred merchant bookings inflate current liabilities far above EBITDA, so EP fails all 4 years and TSA-DSER leverage runs ~1.2-1.36 (fails). Same floor as ABNB/BKNG-type marketplaces. Best case ~10/17 (58.8%) even crediting the RE sign-flip and the R&D point; expect not-a-gift. Positive equity means no TSA-DSER excusal (unlike BKNG which cleared on neg-equity excusal).

DCAP 10/17 (59%)
gift at ≥60%

Sentiment: NEGATIVE / un-celebratable right now. Fiserv stock at decade/52-wk lows on execution repricing: abrupt CEO Mike Lyons departure to Truist, Takis Georgakopoulos elevated to CEO (reaffirmed 2026 guide of 1-3% organic rev growth, $8.00-8.30 adj EPS - below prior consensus). Clover POS growth slowdown + competitive pressure drove downgrades (Raymond James to Market Perform Mar 2026); Morgan Stanley Equal Weight $65. Activist involvement + debt moves in the news. Consensus 'Hold'. Assignment here would NOT be an easy gift today - leadership/credibility overhang; needs a human call.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FISV = Fiserv, Inc. (payments/fintech; ticker moved to NYSE:FI, broker shows FI inactive so used aggregator + SEC data). FY ends Dec; window FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed early 2026). NO INVENTORY: payments-processing/software services carry no physical inventory (0s intentional, not missing). EBITDA built as operating income + cash-flow D&A and reconciles exactly each year (3740+3212=6952; 5014+3162=8176; 5879+3138=9017; 5818+3207=9025); cross-checked vs stocktitan (same shape/magnitude). Retained earnings cross-checked vs stocktitan + FY24 10-K ($23,575M). KEY STRUCTURAL FLAG - PAYMENTS FLOAT: current_liabilities are inflated by settlement obligations (esp. FY2023 $33.6B / assets $90.9B spike from settlement float), so EP (EBITDA>curr liab) fails all 4 years like insurer/lender float; same float floors RTA (avg NOCF/assets ~6.8% on a goodwill-heavy ~$37B-goodwill, ~$80B asset base) and TSA-DSER (avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~1.2). Levers that DO pass: no R&D line, no preferred, large/growing treasury (aggressive buyback), LTD/EBITDA ~3.1x and LTD/assets ~0.29, latest EBITDA/SE ~35% (RSH). RE grows ~16%/yr avg (2/3 - just under the 17% top tier). SE positive all 4 years so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). Goodwill dips slightly FY24 then recovers (newest>oldest). Expect payments-float floor ~9-10/17, not-a-gift near-miss; flag for operator.

INTU - Intuit, Inc. not-a-gift

Technology Services · price $272.14 · mcap $74.4B · vol 5,854,455 · PE 16.7 · PB 3.7 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 10/17 (59%)
gift at ≥60%

Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 16.0% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.20 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.36 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 13.2%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 29.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Down ~40% YTD (five-year low) amid a broad software selloff on fears AI displaces tax/accounting products. Counter: fundamentals strong - FY2026 guidance raised to ~$21.3B revenue, TurboTax Live +47%, QuickBooks Online Accounting +22%; management calls AI a 'clear net tailwind.' But aggressively restructuring: cutting ~17% of workforce, closing Reno/Woodland Hills offices, $300-340M restructuring charges. Analyst tone constructive: 27 Buy / 6 Hold / 1 Sell, median target ~$460. Assignment would be a quality wide-moat SaaS franchise bought on an AI-fear drawdown - celebratable if it clears DCAP.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Intuit; FY ends July 31 (window FY2022-FY2025). SaaS (TurboTax, QuickBooks, Credit Karma, Mailchimp) - carries NO inventory, so inventory=0 across all years (I&NE inventory-rise leg N/A). EBITDA = operating income + total cash-flow D&A (OI 2571/3141/3630/4923 SEC-confirmed; D&A 746/806/789/809 = depreciation + acquired-intangible amort + other amort). PP&E uses SEC PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (888/969/1009/961) - NOT stockanalysis's ~1,440 figures which are ROU-lease-inflated; note FY25 dip 1009->961 breaks 'steady rise'. Current liabilities balloon FY24->FY25 (3790->7491->10370) on short-term debt/commercial paper + current portion of LTD, so EP (EBITDA>CL each yr) fails FY22, FY24, FY25. RE cross-checked vs SEC RetainedEarningsAccumulatedDeficit (exact match). All SE positive -> TSA-DSER applies (max 17). Full share retirement not used - treasury stock accumulates every year (14805->21543).

DCAP 10/17 (59%)
gift at ≥60%

Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 24.8% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.42 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.1 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 4.45 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 85.6%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 110.2% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Richly valued and near all-time highs after a recent stock split; broadly bullish analyst tone into 2026. BofA PT $317 (Buy), Wells Fargo $305 (Overweight), Cantor $325 (Overweight); Susquehanna the soft voice at $275 Neutral. 2026 WFE market guided >$140B (CFO hinted upside) with AI-infrastructure/HBM/leading-edge demand; management plans toward a $215B WFE market by 2030. Dominant, sticky process-control franchise - an assignment here would be celebrate-able as owning a semiconductor-capex toll-taker, though entry price/cyclicality warrant discipline.
research notes - methodology & caveats
KLA Corp - semiconductor process-control (inspection/metrology) equipment; ~56% market share, wide-moat monopoly. FY ends June 30, so window = FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 ended 6/30/2025, latest 10-K). Carries real inventory (equipment maker). EBITDA = operating income (stockanalysis) + D&A (SEC cash-flow tag DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization); reconciles to stockanalysis EBITDA within $1M. JUDGMENT CONTEXT: (1) KLA RETIRES repurchased shares rather than parking them in treasury - no treasury-stock line exists (SEC TreasuryStockCommonValue/TreasuryStockValue tags empty), so treasury_stock_abs=0 and TS flag 'absent' despite a heavy buyback history. (2) Those buybacks are charged against retained earnings, which SUPPRESSES the RE base (only $367M in FY22) and mechanically INFLATES RE YoY growth (~86% avg) - RE 3/3 is a low-base artifact, not organic accumulation. (3) Same buyback-thinned equity likely floors TSA-DSER (TL/SE ~2.4-8x). (4) FY2024 is the analog/memory-cycle trough: EBITDA dipped to $3,748M while current liabilities ballooned to $4,661M (current-portion debt + accruals), so EP likely fails on FY24. (5) Goodwill declining every year (2320->1792, amortization, no new M&A) - GW point likely 0.

Sentiment: Constructive. 16 analysts, consensus Strong Buy, avg PT ~$495 (Evercore Outperform $525) vs $422.83 close. FY2026 revenue guidance raised to ~$11.65B (+7.7%) including $185M from Silvus; $1.5B D-Fend counter-drone acquisition announced, expected to close Q4 2026. Stock fell ~11.4% post-earnings on tariff/cost-pressure worries despite the raise - a sentiment dip on a near-monopoly land-mobile-radio/911 command-center franchise with sticky government contracts and record backlog. Assignment at a discount would be celebratable; watch tariff margin pressure and D-Fend integration atop fresh Silvus debt.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (both EDGAR 10-K XBRL; matches stockanalysis.com cited EBITDA exactly all 4 yrs). PPE is the 10-K net PP&E line (927/964/1022/1165) - stockanalysis shows higher figures because it bundles operating-lease ROU assets. Shareholders' equity is parent-company (EDGAR StockholdersEquity); stockanalysis total equity runs ~$15-17M higher from noncontrolling interests. Total liabilities reconciles as assets minus total equity (incl NCI). Treasury stock: no treasury-stock line on the balance sheet - MSI RETIRES repurchased shares despite a heavy multi-year buyback history (equity was negative pre-2022 largely from buybacks); flag reads 'absent' per the filings, so the TS point is mechanically lost - spirit-of-Buffett judgment call for operators, same class as the CHD R&D flag. Preferred: $0 / 0 shares outstanding per FY2025 10-K. R&D expense line present ($779M→$970M). FY2025 jumps in total assets/goodwill/LTD reflect the ~$4.4B Silvus Technologies acquisition (Aug 2025). Retained earnings and OCF cross-checked EDGAR vs stockanalysis - exact match.

Electronic Technology · price $280.51 · mcap $70.8B · vol 4,650,506 · PE 26.0 · PB 6.3 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 10/17 (59%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)0/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; carries an R&D expense line
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 13.3% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.43 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.8 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.27 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 30.0%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 38.6% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Constructive/recovering. NXP (automotive + industrial/IoT semis) is emerging from a 2024-2025 cyclical downturn that dragged revenue (13.3B FY23 -> 12.3B FY25) and EBITDA lower across the 4-yr window. Q1 2026 revenue $3.18B +12% YoY with all end markets growing (Industrial&IoT +24%, Auto +6%); FY2026 revenue guided ~$14.1B, a return to growth. New CEO Rafael Sotomayor (2025, succeeded Kurt Sievers). Analysts Moderate Buy, ~30+ firms, targets clustering $250-260 (highs to $313). NXP also announced a 2026 debt payoff, improving forward leverage. Assignment would be a durable auto/industrial-semi franchise bought into a recovery - celebratable if the DCAP trailing-window drag (cyclical EBITDA + legacy Freescale leverage) is understood as a trough artifact.
research notes - methodology & caveats
All figures rebuilt from SEC 10-K primary filings (FY2025 10-K acc 0001413447-26-000008 covers FY2025/FY2024; FY2023 10-K acc 0001413447-24-000013 covers FY2023/FY2022) after stockanalysis.com was found materially wrong on this name. TRAPS CORRECTED: (1) stockanalysis mislabeled the ACCUMULATED DEFICIT as positive retained earnings (it showed +2,603 FY25; the 10-K reports Accumulated deficit -1,354 FY25). Real RE series is a still-negative but steadily SHRINKING deficit: -3,975 -> -2,793 -> -1,814 -> -1,354 (deficit-shrink RE artifact; strip it and the 3 RE pts vanish). (2) stockanalysis inflated total assets (~30.9B vs real 26.6B FY25) and PP&E via operating-lease ROU aggregation; ppe here is balance-sheet PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet ex-ROU (2,977 FY25). (3) stockanalysis EBITDA for FY24/FY25 was too high; real EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A(incl impairment) is DECLINING 5,047->4,767->4,342->3,879 (semi/auto downturn). My FY25 EBITDA 3,879 cross-checks gurufocus '$3.9B'. shareholders_equity = parent Total Stockholders' equity (excludes ~$291-395M noncontrolling interest); all four years positive so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). total_liabilities = total assets - total equity incl NCI. WATCH: EP is a knife-edge - FY2025 EBITDA 3,879 sits $2M BELOW current liabilities 3,881, so EP fails on the newest year by a razor (passes the other 3 years comfortably). Preferred stock authorized (euro-denominated) but 0 issued -> absent. R&D line present (-2,360 FY25). Treasury shares at cost present and rising (buyback history). FY ends Dec 31.

Sentiment: Fundamentally excellent, sentiment mixed-positive with a growth-deceleration overhang. Record FY2025: net income $11.3B (+33%), 82.1 combined ratio, record capital generation; company declared a very large special dividend (~$8.0B payable - trailing yield ~7%, paid July 10, 2026). Analyst mix 9 Buy / 13 Hold / 2 Sell, median target ~$230-252 vs ~$207 price; HSBC and UBS recently raised targets, but Wells Fargo downgraded to Underweight on slowing policy growth. CFO transition effective July 4, 2026 and Personal Lines president retiring Jan 2027 - orderly internal succession, worth monitoring. Next earnings July 15, 2026 - inside a typical 30-45 DTE window; note for stage-2 timing. Assignment at a sensible strike would be celebratable: dominant #2 auto insurer, best-in-class underwriting, huge float-driven cash generation.
research notes - methodology & caveats
All figures $ millions, FY2022→FY2025 (calendar fiscal year, Dec 31). P&C insurer specifics: (1) No inventory - insurance carrier, genuinely carries none; 0s recorded. (2) current_liabilities null all years - insurers file UNCLASSIFIED balance sheets (no current/noncurrent split; liabilities are unearned premiums, loss reserves, AP/accrued, debt); genuinely unavailable, not estimated - EP parameter correctly scores 0. (3) EBITDA derived as pretax income + interest expense + depreciation from SEC XBRL (922+244+306=1472; 4904+268+285=5457; 10713+279+284=11276; 14223+278+313=14814), matches stockanalysis.com EBITDA series exactly - dual-source cross-check passed. (4) long_term_debt = the balance-sheet "Debt" line; FY2025 10-K footnote states it "consists solely of long-term debt". LTD/EBITDA latest = 0.47. (5) Goodwill flat ~$228M FY22-24; the FY2025 10-K balance sheet no longer shows a goodwill line (folded into other assets, not separately disclosed) → null for FY2025, never rose. (6) Treasury stock: no dollar-valued treasury line (repurchases allocated to paid-in capital/retained earnings), but the FY2025 10-K share parenthetical discloses ~212M treasury shares held of 798M issued → flag present, abs $ 0. (7) Preferred: Serial Preferred Series B ($494M) fully redeemed February 2024 → absent. (8) Retained earnings cross-checked stockanalysis vs SEC XBRL - exact match. PPE declines FY22→25 (asset-light, tech-driven; not a rising series). SE strongly positive and rising all 4 years; TSA-DSER applicable but insurers run TL/SE ~3x by business model (policyholder float), so expect 0 there.

RCL - Royal Caribbean Group not-a-gift

Consumer Services / Cruise Lines · price $287.78 · mcap $77.2B · vol 2,824,270 · PE 18.1 · PB 8.1 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 10/17 (59%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)2/2inventory & EBITDA both rose steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 11.0% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)1/2avg debt/assets 0.53 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.7 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 3.98 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 8815.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 64.7% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Bullish. Consensus Buy across ~18-33 analysts, median PT ~$362 (~13% upside), 0% sells. RCL raised its annual profit forecast on strong cruise demand and high onboard spend; 2026 guidance came in "better than feared" (William Blair Outperform, ~11% EPS growth at midpoint) despite a modest $0.60 cut to FY2026 EPS guidance on geopolitical uncertainty. New Icon-class hardware (Legend of the Seas) launching summer 2026 with record bookings. FY2025: revenue $17.9B, net income $4.3B, Adjusted EBITDA ~$7.0B. Main caution for the wheel: assignment means owning a highly leveraged, deferred-revenue-funded, cyclical/discretionary-demand cruise operator - fine on sentiment, structurally penalized by the DCAP rubric.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd, cruise line, FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A (stockanalysis): FY22 -766+1407=641; FY23 2878+1455=4333; FY24 4106+1600=5706; FY25 4910+1718=6628. Company-reported Adjusted EBITDA is higher (FY25 ~$7.0B) because it excludes items; I used the clean OI+D&A definition per DCAP spec. FY2022 is the COVID-recovery trough (negative operating income; low NOCF $481M drags the 4-yr RTA avg to ~10.97%, a razor miss of the 11% bar). Structural drags: EP fails all 4 years because current liabilities are dominated by passenger customer deposits / deferred revenue (float floor, like other prepayment/deferred-rev businesses); TSA-DSER fails on cruise-line balance-sheet leverage (avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~4.0). RE swings deficit->positive (-1707 -> -10 -> 2612 -> 5925), a genuine post-COVID recovery but the near-zero FY23 base makes the growth% an artifact. Goodwill flat/slightly declining (809->808), no M&A. SE positive all 4 years so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). Treasury stock present (32.6M shares, $3,251M cost at FY25). No preferred outstanding. No R&D expense line (services business). Inventory = ship provisions/retail merchandise, small and rising.

Sentiment: Buy consensus (45 analysts, ~$750 median PT, range $504-$940) as of mid-2026. Stock fell ~15% over prior 30 days. Core debate: Eylea franchise erosion (US Eylea -10%, expected ~-20% in 2026) vs the transition to Eylea HD plus delayed biosimilar competition and fast-growing Dupixent (+33%) and Libtayo (+54%). Street models revenue rising from ~$14B (2025) toward ~$21B by 2030 -> not priced as a broken growth story, but the Eylea cliff is a real overhang. Not a clean "assignment is a gift" narrative given the patent-cliff uncertainty; flag for operator judgment.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Regeneron, homegrown biotech, FY ends Dec 31. Goodwill genuinely $0 all years (no us-gaap Goodwill concept in SEC XBRL; no material acquisitions) -> GW param structurally 0. Heavy R&D expense line ($3.8B->$6.0B) zeroes the I&NE R&D sub-point (spirit-of-Buffett avoid-R&D rule). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (stockanalysis EBITDA ties out: 4739+341=5080, 4047+421=4468, 3991+483=4474, 3578+544=4122). EBITDA drifting down FY22->FY25 (Eylea erosion) breaks the I&NE steady-rise and makes EP fail in FY2025 only (EBITDA 4122 < curr liab 4368). Fortress balance sheet: LTD flat ~$1.98B, SE positive & rising, huge treasury stock from ongoing buybacks. RSH latest EBITDA/SE ~13% (well under 23%). RE 4-yr avg YoY ~15.4% (2/3, misses 17%). Retained earnings cross-checked vs SEC XBRL (exact); LTD vs SEC XBRL (exact).

Sentiment: Bearish tape, constructive fundamentals: stock ~$355, down ~20% YTD and ~37% over 12 months on vertical-software multiple compression and AI-disruption fears. Q1 2026 beat expectations; dividend raised to $0.91/qtr; new $1.5B buyback. 27-analyst average target ~$456 (range $365-$550); GuruFocus GF Value calls it significantly undervalued. Q2 2026 earnings July 23 - inside a typical 30-45 DTE window, note for any chain sweep. Assignment case: serial acquirer of niche market-leading software with 30%+ FCF margins, though the AI-moat debate on vertical software is a live human-judgment question.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec 31. All figures from SEC XBRL (companyfacts CIK0000882835, 10-K tagged, full-year durations), cross-checked vs stockanalysis.com. EBITDA = operating income + D&A per cash-flow statement (SEC tags); stockanalysis EBITDA (2186/2510/2819/3145) within 0.5% - SEC-derived values used. FY2022 NOCF of $734.6M is GAAP-correct but depressed by ~$1.3B cash taxes on the 2022 majority divestiture of industrial businesses to CD&R - drags RTA avg; normalized OCF would be ~$2.0B. Current liabilities include large software deferred revenue (~50%+), a structural EP headwind like the insurer-float pattern, not a solvency issue. Inventory is minimal (vertical software roll-up, small residual product businesses). R&D flag: stockanalysis shows no R&D line, but SEC XBRL tags full-year ResearchAndDevelopmentExpense on the income statement (529.8/646.1/748.1/852.5) ~11% of revenue - flag is unambiguously present. Treasury stock jumped 16.5 → 516.1 in FY2025 (new $1.5B buyback authorization). Preferred: authorized but zero outstanding all 4 years.

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 7.3% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.19 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.6 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.36 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 10.0%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 24.6% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Bullish. Mobility spin-off (MBGL) completed July 1, 2026, 1-for-1 distribution; Street likes it - recent price-target upgrades from Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Wells Fargo, Mizuho; ~93% of analysts rate buy, median PT $543 vs ~$447 price (well off the $579 52-wk high). Post-spin 2026 guidance arrives with Q2 earnings July 28, 2026. Business is the ratings duopoly (with Moody's) + S&P index licensing toll-bridge + Market Intelligence/Commodity Insights subscriptions - deeply moated, capital-light, heavy buybacks. Assignment at a discount would be celebratable; main near-term uncertainty is the post-spin recast baseline and guidance on July 28.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY2022-FY2025, Dec-31 fiscal year end, $ millions, as-filed 10-K figures via SEC XBRL. Inventory = 0: data/analytics/ratings services business, genuinely carries no inventory (no line item on the balance sheet). EBITDA = as-filed operating income + D&A (2022: 4944+1013; 2023: 4020+1143; 2024: 5580+1173; 2025: 6478+1179); cross-checked vs stockanalysis standardized EBITDA (5930/5127/6710/7629, within 0.5%). Retained earnings and NOCF match stockanalysis exactly. Shareholders' equity = as-filed 'total equity - controlling interests' (us-gaap:StockholdersEquity); excludes the ~$3.4-5.0B mezzanine REDEEMABLE noncontrolling interest (CME/S&P Dow Jones Indices JV) that aggregators like stockanalysis fold into equity - RSH is sensitive to this choice (24.6% as-filed vs ~21% with mezzanine NCI included). PPE = as-filed net PP&E excluding operating-lease ROU assets. No R&D expense line on the income statement. No preferred stock outstanding (authorized, none issued - no PreferredStockValue XBRL facts). Treasury stock large and growing ($25.3B → $36.4B, consistent buybacks). Caveats: FY2022 was the IHS Markit merger year (NOCF depressed by merger costs; EBITDA dipped 2023 then recovered strongly - fails the 'steady rise' test on the 2022→2023 dip); Mobility (CARFAX) spun off July 1, 2026 as MBGL, so these historical consolidated figures include Mobility - recast post-spin financials land with Q2 2026 earnings July 28, 2026.

gift at ≥60%

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 10.5% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.46 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 3.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.70 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 7.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 228.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Neutral-to-cautious. FY2025 (ended Jun 28 2025) net earnings -6.5% to $1.8B; full-year adjusted net earnings +0.8% to $2.2B, Q4 adj EBITDA +1.8% to ~$1.3B -- steady but soft, foodservice volume growth muted by cautious restaurant traffic and food-cost deflation. Into FY2026 mgmt guides ~5-7% EPS growth (midpoint on long-term algo) with ~$1B dividends + ~$1B buybacks planned; Q3 FY26 EBITDA -5.1% to $864M (adj EBITDA flat +0.1%). Dividend aristocrat, #1 US broadline distributor with durable scale moat, but low-margin working-capital-intensive model and a levered balance sheet (LTD $12.4B, TL/(equity+treasury) ~1.7x) cap the DCAP score. No acute controversy; assignment would be ownable, just not a standout gift.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Sysco Corp, broadline foodservice distributor. FY ends Sat nearest June 30 (FY2022=Jul 2 2022 ... FY2025=Jun 28 2025). All line items SEC XBRL-verified (companyfacts CIK 0000096021, 10-K frames): inventory, current liabilities, total assets, LTD (LongTermDebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations), treasury stock, retained earnings (RetainedEarningsUnappropriated), goodwill, operating cash flow, operating income all pulled directly. PPE uses SEC pure PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (4456/4915/5497/6084), EXCLUDING operating-lease ROU assets (~0.7-1.1B) that stockanalysis folds into PP&E per this project's ROU convention. EBITDA = SEC operating income (2346/3039/3202/3088) + cash-flow D&A (881/889/997/1086) = 3227/3928/4199/4174, exact-matched to stockanalysis EBITDA. shareholders_equity = Total Sysco stockholders' equity (parent-only, SEC StockholdersEquity); NCI ~30M excluded; all four years positive so TSA-DSER applies. Distributor structure: EBITDA (3.2-4.2B) is below current liabilities (8.5-9.9B) every year -> EP fails on trade-payables float, same floor as COST/DG. FY22 was a COVID-recovery year with depressed OCF (1791M) which drags 4yr avg RTA toward ~10.5% (below 11%). Heavy buybacks (treasury 10.2->12.9B) keep equity thin (~1.8B) which inflates RSH (EBITDA/SE ~228%). RE YoY avg ~7.4% (razor-clears 7%). No R&D line, no preferred.

TGT - Target Corp. not-a-gift

Retail Trade / General Merchandise · price $126.10 · mcap $57.3B · vol 5,058,947 · PE 17.2 · PB 3.6 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 10/17 (59%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 11.7% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.26 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.7 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 3.12 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 23.6%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 51.0% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Under pressure but stabilizing. Fiscal 2025 (ended Jan 31 2026) net sales fell ~2% and net earnings dropped to $3.7B from $4.1B; the stock has been a multi-year laggard on soft traffic, DEI-boycott fallout and market-share loss to Walmart/Costco/Amazon. New CEO Michael Fiddelke (named Feb 2026, succeeding Brian Cornell) is running an aggressive turnaround - ~$5B capex (+$1B), merchandising overhaul ('more change than in a decade'). Q1 FY2026 beat and posted +5.6% comps, first positive comp in five quarters, and three firms upgraded in H1 2026 on turnaround optimism. Assignment would mean owning a dominant #2 general-merchandise retailer mid-turnaround at a discount - defensible but not yet a clean celebrate; execution risk on the recovery remains the swing factor.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fiscal year ends late Jan/early Feb; FY2022=ended Jan 28 2023, FY2023=Feb 3 2024, FY2024=Feb 1 2025, FY2025=Jan 31 2026 (fiscal 2025 10-K filed Mar 11 2026). All 12 series SEC-exact from Target's 10-K Consolidated Statements of Financial Position, Operations, and Cash Flows. PPE = the reported 'Property and equipment, net' line EXCLUDING operating-lease ROU assets (stockanalysis/wsj fold ~$3.4-3.7B ROU in and show ~$37B; SEC-exact ex-ROU is ~$33B, used here per our convention). EBITDA = operating income + total cash-flow D&A (captures D&A in cost of sales too): FY22 3848+2700, FY23 5707+2801, FY24 5566+2981, FY25 5117+3134. No R&D line (mass retailer). Treasury stock ABSENT: Target retires repurchased shares against common/APIC/retained earnings rather than holding treasury (shares out 455.6M->452.8M in FY25) - same buyback-retirement floor as MSI/ORLY/CMG, so TS scores 0. Goodwill flat at $631M all four years (Shipt/Grand Junction), so GW scores 0. Structural retail floors expected: EP fails all four years (EBITDA ~6.5-8.5B << current liabilities ~19-21B, dominated by accounts-payable working capital) and TSA-DSER fails (avg TL/SE ~3.1x, payables+lease leverage). RE 3/3 is real but flattered by the FY2023 +41.7% YoY rebound off a depressed FY2022 base (fiscal-2022 net earnings collapsed to ~$2.8B on the inventory-glut margin wipeout). Net: a near-miss around 10/17 (~58.8%), just under the 60% gift bar - the working-capital/leverage/retirement floor caps it. Server is authoritative.

DCAP 10/17 (59%)
gift at ≥60%

Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 1179.1%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 53.7% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Strongly positive. 2026 analyst consensus Strong Buy (25-29 of ~29 buy/outperform, 0 sells), mean target ~$262 vs ~$186 spot = ~40% implied upside. Mgmt raised FY2026 guidance (postpaid net adds 950k-1.05M, adj FCF $18.1-18.7B); ARPA +3.9%. Best mid-band 2.5GHz 5G position in US; expanding fiber via Oak Hill/Wren House JVs (GoNetspeed, Greenlight, i3). Stock sold off ~29% from Aug-2025 high of $261 to ~$186, near 52-wk low $166 - a drawdown entry on a wireless leader; assignment would be celebratable. Bear case: national-network capex intensity, saturated/competitive wireless market.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Dec fiscal year; FY2022-FY2025. Wireless carrier (T-Mobile/Metro brands). long_term_debt = SEC "Long-term debt" + "Long-term debt to affiliates" (Deutsche Telekom); FY2025 = 81,147 + 3,532 = 84,679 (matches stockanalysis series across all 4 yrs). EBITDA = operating income + D&A, cross-checked vs SEC 10-K (op inc FY23/24/25 = 14,266/18,010/18,279; D&A 12,818/12,919/13,508). No R&D expense line on income statement (typical carrier). No preferred outstanding. Treasury stock present and climbing on aggressive buybacks (3.0B->30.5B, 168.8M shares at cost FY25). PPE net is DECLINING (74.1B->66.8B) despite 5G capex, as legacy network/Sprint assets depreciate faster than adds - fails PPE steady-rise. Carrier-float floor: current liabilities exceed EBITDA in FY2022 (24.7B vs 20.2B) so EP fails. Retained earnings sign-flips from -223 (FY22 slight deficit) to +21,136 (FY25) - reconciles via net-income roll-forward net of dividends (started Q4-23); RE growth rides the sign-flip. Highly leveraged BS (TL/adj-equity ~1.86 avg) floors TSA-DSER, but LTD/assets ~0.36 avg and LTD/EBITDA 2.66x keep LTD 2/2. RTA avg NOCF/assets ~10.1% narrowly misses 11%. Likely near-miss ~10/17 like AT&T (see [[t-not-a-gift-carrier-leverage-floor]]) - carrier leverage floor on EP/RTA/TSA-DSER, but stronger BS discipline and RE sign-flip vs T. Server scores.

Sentiment: Positive. Raised FY2026 guidance (revenue growth ~9.5%, adj EPS $14.75-14.95); Q1-2026 organic bookings +24% with record $10.7B backlog; data-center cooling tailwind (HSWE chiller launch in Asia Pacific, LiquidStack acquisition). Donald Simmons appointed EVP/COO effective 2026-07-01 - orderly leadership expansion, not turmoil. 23 analysts, majority buy/outperform, avg PT ~$509-521 with raises to $555-585 (Citi/Barclays/KeyCorp). Stock up ~18% YTD 2026. Watch-outs: transport-refrigeration softness and tariff exposure offset by commercial HVAC/data-center demand; Q2 earnings ~late July lands inside 30-45 DTE windows. Assignment would be celebratable: dominant commercial HVAC franchise with recurring services mix.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Trane Technologies plc (HVAC/climate solutions), FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed 2026-02-05 (accession 0001628280-26-005731). EBITDA = operating income (EDGAR XBRL OperatingIncomeLoss: 2418.9/2894.0/3500.1/3967.4) + cash-flow D&A (323.6/348.1/379.4/376.3); matches stockanalysis.com EBITDA line within rounding all 4 years. Shareholders_equity is parent-company (EDGAR StockholdersEquity); totals incl. ~$20M noncontrolling interest are ~0.3% higher and move no ratio. R&D FLAG JUDGMENT CALL (CHD/ITW precedent): income statement face has NO R&D line (only Selling & administrative expenses - verified in 10-K R3 statement), but notes disclose R&D of $211.2M/$252.3M/$309.6M/$347.6M (~1.6% of revenue), tagged ResearchAndDevelopmentExpense in XBRL. Recorded 'absent' per the rubric's literal income-statement-line definition; operators may apply spirit-of-Buffett read. Treasury stock present (~$1.65B at cost FY2025); buybacks largely via retirement/other mechanisms so the treasury line is near-flat. Preference shares authorized but none outstanding. All 12 series cross-verified EDGAR XBRL vs stockanalysis.com - exact matches.

gift at ≥60%

Sentiment: Positive. Q1-2026 beat with raised FY guidance ($14.08-14.28 adj EPS); Skyrizi +31% y/y ($4.48B qtr, ~$21.6B FY26 guide) and Rinvoq +23% ($2.12B qtr, ~$10.2B guide) have more than replaced Humira (now $688M/qtr, −39%). 22 of 29 analysts buy/outperform, mean PT $249 vs ~$209. Post-Humira story considered proven; assignment would be palatable operationally, though the DCAP balance-sheet picture (negative equity, deepening accumulated deficit, $59B LTD) is the structural weak point, not the franchise.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Dec fiscal year-end; FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (op income verified against SEC EDGAR XBRL exactly; D&A from cash-flow statement). Shareholders' equity is the EDGAR us-gaap:StockholdersEquity series (attributable to AbbVie, excludes ~$30-40M noncontrolling interests; stockanalysis totals incl. NCI are 17,287/10,397/3,364/-3,228 - same trajectory, negative by FY2025 either way). SE negative in FY2025 → TSA-DSER expected N/A (max 15). Retained earnings deficit deepening (aggressive dividends + buybacks against Humira-era earnings base) - driven by capital-return policy, not operating losses; NOCF stayed $18.8-24.9B. FY2024 operating-income dip reflects large acquired-IPR&D charges (Cerevel/ImmunoGen). R&D expense present (pharma, $9.1B FY2025). Preferred authorized but none outstanding. Treasury stock present ($9.1B FY2025).

Sentiment: Constructive/high-quality franchise. FY2025 billings ~$7.6B; management guided 2026 billings growth ~13% (above ~11% consensus), Q1-2026 billings +31% YoY. Firewall hardware refresh cycle a tailwind through 2026-2027; SASE billings +40% YoY (~27% of billings). Avg analyst rating "Hold" with ~$114 12-mo target after a big YTD run; some valuation-driven downgrades and an ~8% pullback on 2026 guidance day despite the raise; a bull (TD Cowen) at $160 Buy. Debate centers on sustainability of hardware-refresh momentum, not franchise durability. Nothing here makes assignment un-celebratable.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fortinet (cybersecurity: firewalls/network security + SASE). FY ends Dec 31. Carries modest inventory (hardware appliances) so inventory is real, not zeroed. EBITDA built as operating income + D&A (FY22 969.6+327.4; FY23 1241+380; FY24 1803+417; FY25 2085+488); D&A independently corroborated by cash-flow statement (327.6/379.7/416.5/488.3). Shareholders' equity NEGATIVE in FY2022 (-281.6) and FY2023 (-463.4) -> TSA-DSER should be excused (max 15). This is a buyback artifact, not operating losses: Fortinet RETIRES repurchased shares (no treasury-stock line -> treasury_stock_abs=0, flag absent), charging buybacks against retained earnings/APIC, which drives the persistent accumulated deficit (-1546/-1862/-117.1/-507.9) and the early negative equity. Net income was positive throughout. RE deficit narrowed sharply FY23->FY24 then widened again FY25 as buybacks resumed -> RE growth is a messy sign/deficit series, server computes. LTD FY2025 dropped to 496.6 because the $1B 2026 Senior Notes were reclassified to current portion (2031 Senior Notes remain the 496.6 long-term). R&D expense line present (large, ~512-816M). No preferred stock.

Consumer Services / Restaurants · price $102.11 · mcap $116.4B · vol 7,947,598 · PE 79.6 · PB -13.8 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 8/15 (53%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 17.6% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.46 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 3.1 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negativeN/Aexcused: shareholders' equity was negative or incomplete in the window, so this test is off the scale (max drops to 15)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 0.1%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1equity is negative (heavy buybacks can do this) but 4 straight years of positive EBITDA back it - DANGER-rule pass
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Turnaround gaining traction under CEO Brian Niccol's 'Back to Starbucks' plan. Q2 FY2026 (reported ~Apr 2026): US comps +7.1% (2nd straight quarter), global comps +6.2% after seven quarters of decline; FY2026 guidance raised to >=5% global comps and adj EPS $2.25-2.45; all 10 largest intl markets positive for first time in 9 quarters. Stock up ~25% YTD 2026. North America margins still pressured by higher labor/input costs (sustainability of margin recovery is the debate). China: transitioning to a licensed JV, retaining 40% stake vs Luckin competition. Consensus Moderate Buy, avg PT ~$106-107 (modest ~4-5% upside). Assignment would be a recovering-but-margin-challenged franchise at a discount -- celebrable if the turnaround holds, but FY2025 was a genuine profit trough.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fiscal year ends late Sept/early Oct (FY2025 ended Sep 28, 2025; 10-K filed Nov 2025). NEGATIVE shareholders' equity all 4 years (heavy buybacks) -> TSA-DSER excused, applicable max 15. Starbucks RETIRES repurchased shares (retirement method) so there is NO treasury-stock line and no preferred stock -> treasury_stock_abs=0, TS flag absent, PS absent. EBITDA = GAAP operating income + cash-flow D&A (FY22 4618+1529; FY23 5871+1450; FY24 5410+1592; FY25 2937+1772), matching stockanalysis.com EBITDA series. FY2025 is a turnaround-year trough: 'Back to Starbucks' investment cratered operating income (5410->2937) and EBITDA (7002->4708). Net PP&E excludes operating-lease ROU assets (reported separately ~$8-9B). EP structurally fails: gift-card/stored-value-card + deferred-revenue float keeps current liabilities ($9-10B) well above EBITDA every year. Inventory series is destock-then-restock (not a steady rise). Retained earnings is an accumulated deficit that shrank FY22->FY23 then deepened FY24->FY25 (no consistent growth). Goodwill dipped FY23 (3218 vs 3284, ~98.0% of prior) before recovering to a 4-yr high -> knife-edge on the 'steady rise' test; recorded exact figures for the server to adjudicate. RSH: negative SE but 4 yrs positive EBITDA (DANGER-rule territory). FLAG FOR HUMAN: this scores near the 60% (9/15) threshold on the neg-equity-excused max; GW and RSH are the swing points.

Sentiment: Constructive/healthy business. Consensus Buy/Moderate-Buy (16-22 analysts), avg PT ~$367-373 (range $300-425); Goldman $400, Piper Sandler initiated Overweight $396. FY2025 revenue ~$72B; mgmt guides 9-10% revenue growth through 2026 on resilient premium/affluent-consumer spending; net losses & delinquencies running below seasonal norms and better than most card issuers. Some 2026 valuation-risk flags. Business quality is celebratable; the low DCAP is a structural artifact of the lender/float balance-sheet model, not deterioration.
research notes - methodology & caveats
AXP is a payments/lending financial (FY ends Dec; last 4 completed FYs = FY2022-FY2025, FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026). NO INVENTORY (services) -> 0s; this correctly fails the I&NE 'inventory rise' half but the no-R&D half still earns +1. EBITDA constructed as pretax income + D&A (pretax: 9585/10513/12895/13795; D&A from CF: 1626/1651/1676/1777). For a lender, funding interest expense is a core operating cost, so it is NOT added back (adding it would inflate EBITDA by ~$15B and misstate earning power) -- same treatment used for insurers. current_liabilities is a CONSTRUCTED figure: AXP files an UNCLASSIFIED balance sheet (no current/long-term split), so this is the aggregator's near-term-obligations total (largely customer deposits + short-term borrowings + other liabilities); EP fails structurally on this float regardless (EBITDA ~$11-16B << ~$124-169B), the same pattern as insurers/CME. TREASURY STOCK = 0 on the balance sheet: AXP RETIRES all repurchased shares (equity section reconciles exactly: Common+APIC+RE+AOCI = total SE every year), so the TS parameter scores 0 despite a massive multi-billion annual buyback program -- this is a buyback-retirement artifact, not absence of buybacks (same as MSI/ORLY/ANET), and is a potential operator-call lever. Preferred stock = 0 in every year per EDGAR XBRL (the aggregator's '$141M FY2024 preferred' is a misclassification of the common-stock par line). SE positive all 4 years so TSA-DSER applies, but avg TL/SE ~8x fails structurally (bank leverage).

Sentiment: Constructive/quality operator but a merger-integration year. FY2025 revenue $5.76B (+23% total, ~2.8% organic), adjusted EBITDAC margin ~36% (+70bps), ~$1.5B operating cash flow - 32nd consecutive year of revenue growth and a serial, disciplined acquirer (43 deals in 2025). The transformational Accession/RSC deal adds meaningful integration and leverage risk (total debt ~$7.6B) and dilution from the equity raise. Analyst tone generally favorable on the broker model's durability, but the assigned entity post-RSC is a larger, more levered business than the pre-2025 track record; earnings/organic-growth watch on integration. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable, but the merger-year optics inflate leverage and depress return-on-equity ratios this window.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Brown & Brown Inc - insurance brokerage/agency. FY ends Dec 31; latest 4 = FY2022-FY2025. NO INVENTORY (services business - commissions & fees), coded 0s. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (963+186, 1156+206, 1367+222, 1502+367), cross-checks to stockanalysis EBITDA line. Current liabilities carry a large fiduciary 'premiums payable to insurance companies' float item (broker-model), which structurally exceeds EBITDA every year - expect EP to fail like AON/insurers. FY2025 balance sheet is a MERGER YEAR: the Aug-1-2025 Accession/RSC Topco acquisition (~$9.8B, largest in company history) ballooned total assets $17.6B→$30.0B, goodwill $8.0B→$15.1B, long-term debt $3.6B→$6.9B, and shareholders' equity $6.4B→$12.6B via a large equity raise. That equity dilution drags RSH (latest EBITDA/SE 1869/12573=14.9% vs prior 1589/6437=24.7%) and RTA (asset base inflated by one quarter of RSC with a full year of debt/equity). Treasury stock present ($748M held flat FY22-24, $848M FY25); no preferred; no R&D line. Compare to [[aon-gift-on-deficit-shrink]] and insurer-float pattern in [[dcap-rubric-penalizes-insurers]].

Sentiment: Strongly positive/growth. Consensus Buy (as of 2026-07-07), avg PT ~$371, range $330-454. MATERIAL EVENT: the $16.4B (EV ~$29B) Calpine acquisition CLOSED Jan 7, 2026 - creates a ~60GW generation goliath, expected ~+20% to 2026 non-GAAP EPS, and materially enlarges/re-levers the balance sheet beyond what these FY2025 numbers show (the assigned entity today is bigger and more indebted). Primary bull thesis is AI/hyperscaler data-center power demand met by the largest US nuclear fleet + Calpine gas/geothermal (new CyrusOne 380MW deals, ~1GW nuclear uprates, $3.9B capex). Q1 2026 revenue roughly doubled on Calpine, EPS guidance affirmed. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable on business quality; the caution is a growth/leverage story (acquisition debt) plus commodity-price/hedge cash-flow volatility, not deterioration.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Constellation Energy - nuclear-led merchant power generator + retail supplier, spun off from Exelon Feb 2022 (FY ends Dec 31; first full-year 10-K is FY2022). EBITDA = operating income + total D&A (from cash-flow stmt): 495+2427, 1610+2514, 4352+2700, 3086+2601 - cross-checked SEC XBRL OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationAmortizationAndAccretionNet. KEY STRUCTURAL DRAG: net operating cash flow is NEGATIVE FY2022-FY2024 (-2353/-5301/-2464) then +4237 FY2025 - this is real (SEC-confirmed), driven by large collateral/margin postings on commodity hedges flowing through operating activities, not a business failure; it sinks RTA and makes EP fail 3 of 4 years. Total liabilities are dominated by derivative/deferred items, so TSA-DSER (TL/equity avg ~3.2x) fails hard, yet actual long-term debt is modest (LTD/assets ~0.13, LTD/EBITDA ~1.3x) so LTD should score 2/2. shareholders_equity is parent-attributable common equity (SEC us-gaap:StockholdersEquity); incl-NCI figures ~336M higher (11372/11286/13539/14853) - all positive, so TSA-DSER is NOT excused, full 17-pt scale. RETAINED EARNINGS caveat: FY2022 is a small accumulated DEFICIT (-496) that flips positive in FY2023 (+761) - the FY2022->FY2023 YoY growth is a sign-flip artifact, so any RE points ride on that flip (operator judgment, same pattern as APO/BSX/AON). No R&D expense line (power generator). No preferred stock. No treasury stock line - CEG retires repurchased shares under its buyback program, so TS scores 0 despite genuine buyback history (same quirk as MSI/ORLY). Inventory: FY2025 10-K reclassified inventory (it now tags FY2024 as 1600 vs 2397 as-originally-reported); series above is total "Inventories, net" as originally reported each year (FY2025 1736 matches SEC exactly) - does not affect scoring since EBITDA does not steadily rise. Goodwill jumped FY2022->FY2023 (small acquisitions) then flat/slightly down (425->420).

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)0/2avg debt/assets 0.63 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 4.3 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 8.16 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 28.2%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 105.4% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Bearish tape, structurally-challenged narrative. Stock ~-50% from its high; four straight quarters of broadband subscriber losses (Q1 2026 lost ~120k vs ~100k expected) as fixed-wireless (T-Mobile/Verizon) takes share. Street leans Hold: ~5 Buy / 9 Hold / 5 negative of ~15 analysts; mean target ~$274 implies large upside but the bull/bear spread ($455 vs $150) is a binary bet on whether broadband trends inflect before Charter is forced to compete on price. Bull case = Cox integration ($800M+ synergies, becomes largest US cable operator), mobile-line growth, accelerating 2026 buybacks, FCF inflection. Not an obviously 'celebrate the assignment' name today: durable network/moat but eroding residential broadband and a leverage-heavy, buyback-financed balance sheet with a pending mega-merger overhang.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (SEC XBRL: OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationAmortizationAndAccretionNet), not Charter's higher 'Adjusted EBITDA'. No separate inventory line - broadband/cable service business, so inventory=0 (I&NE inventory-rise sub-point will not score; the no-R&D sub-point should). shareholders_equity = TOTAL equity incl. noncontrolling interests (Advance/Newhouse A/N partnership); this preserves the balance-sheet identity (Assets = TL + total equity). Charter-attributable equity is lower: 9,119/11,086/15,587/16,054 - if the scorer expects parent-only equity, RSH/TSA-DSER shift, flag for operator. treasury_stock ABSENT: Charter runs an enormous buyback but RETIRES repurchased shares (no treasury-stock line in XBRL) - so TS scores 0 and TSA-DSER gets no treasury cushion, same pattern as MSI/AXP. retained_earnings is a deep accumulated deficit all 4 yrs (buyback-driven), improving -14,821 -> -5,393; on a negative base the YoY-growth formula likely yields 0 for RE despite genuine improvement - treat any RE points as a base-sign artifact and flag. long_term_debt = SEC LongTermDebtNoncurrent (differs slightly from aggregators that fold in finance leases/current portion). HEAVY leverage: LTD/assets ~0.61-0.66 and LTD/EBITDA ~4.3 - expect LTD 0/2. MATERIAL CORPORATE ACTION: the ~$34.5B Charter-Cox merger (FCC+DOJ cleared, expected to close summer 2026) will step-change PP&E/goodwill/debt/equity - rescore after close / FY2026 10-K.

CMCSA - Comcast Corp not-a-gift

Communication Services · price $23.38 · mcap $83.5B · vol 53,268,558 · PE 4.7 · PB 1.0 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 9/17 (53%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 10.9% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.36 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.5 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.90 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 9.1%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 38.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Neutral-to-cautious. Consensus Hold; avg 12-mo target ~$40-43. Structural overhang = broadband subscriber losses (Q1 2026 domestic residential net loss ~65k, though improving >100k YoY on simpler pricing). Bull case rests on Peacock reaching sustained profitability H2 2026, wireless growth, Epic Universe park ramp, and Project Genesis lowering capex intensity by 2027. Versant spin-off (Jan 2026) muddies YoY comparability. Strong cash engine but the cord-cutting narrative keeps the multiple compressed; assignment would be into a leveraged, slow-growth cable/media name in secular transition, not an obvious celebration.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Comcast Corp (cable/broadband + NBCUniversal media/parks). FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed 2026-02-03. No inventory line (service business) -> inventory series is 0s, so I&NE inventory-rise leg cannot score by construction; that is the correct no-inventory treatment. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (cited, consistent 4 yrs). FY2022 EBITDA (27,862) is depressed by an ~$8.6B Sky/goodwill+long-lived-asset impairment charge sitting inside operating income -> it lands $25M BELOW FY2022 current liabilities (27,887), so EP fails on that single razor-thin, impairment-driven year; on adjusted EBITDA EP would pass. shareholders_equity = Comcast Corporation shareholders' equity ATTRIBUTABLE to Comcast (excludes noncontrolling interests; aggregators' ~97,375 FY2025 figure includes NCI). All 4 SE years positive -> TSA-DSER applies (17-pt max), but cable leverage (TL/(SE+treasury) ~1.7-2.0x) fails both TSA-DSER tiers. LTD declining every year, LTD/EBITDA latest ~2.5x. RTA avg(NOCF/assets) ~10.9% sits just under the 11% first tier. RE avg YoY ~9% (2.5%/7.7%/17.0%). Treasury stock flat 7,517 (no new buyback activity in-window at the treasury line, but present -> TS credit). Goodwill up net (58,494->61,502) with a small FY2024 dip. HUMAN CALL: Versant spin-off (linear cable networks) closed Jan 2026, so the entity we would actually be assigned in mid-2026 is smaller than the FY2022-FY2025 consolidated financials shown here; a post-FY2026 rescore will reflect the slimmed-down connectivity+content+parks business.

Sentiment: Elite, dominant warehouse-club retailer; membership renewals near-record, ~6-7% comps ex-gas/FX, periodic special dividends. Consensus Moderate Buy (16 buy / 7 hold / 1 sell), avg PT ~$1,104 vs ~$1,000 price; Bernstein named it a top retail pick for H2 2026. Chief caution is valuation: ~40x earnings, a premium that leaves the stock sensitive to any comp/membership softness. Business-wise assignment is highly celebratable; the entry-price premium is the only real hesitation.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Costco fiscal year ends late Aug/early Sep (FY2025 ended Aug 31, 2025; FY2023 was a 53-week year). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (FY22 7793+1900; FY23 8114+2077; FY24 9285+2237; FY25 10383+2426). GOODWILL = 0 all years: Costco carries no goodwill line (grows organically, not acquisitive) so GW parameter cannot score. TREASURY STOCK absent: Costco retires all repurchased shares, no treasury line on balance sheet (equity is common stock + APIC + AOCI + retained earnings only) -> TS parameter scores 0 structurally, like a share-retirement name. No R&D line (warehouse retailer). Inventory is real (merchandise) but dips FY23 and FY25 so not a steady 4-yr rise. RETAINED-EARNINGS dip FY2024 (19,521 -> 17,619) is the ~$15/share (~$6.7B) special cash dividend paid Jan 2024, not deterioration; RE snaps back to 22,650 FY2025. Watch EP/TSA-DSER: warehouse-club payables (current liab ~32-37B) dwarf EBITDA (~10-13B), the classic retailer working-capital structure that floors both. SE positive all years so TSA-DSER is NOT excused (max stays 17). LTD low (~5.7B, LTD/EBITDA ~0.45).

Sentiment: Analyst tone strongly positive: consensus Buy from 28 analysts (13% Strong Buy, 75% Buy, 13% Hold, zero Sell), avg target ~C$125.86 / US$104.38; BofA, Barclays, and Susquehanna all raised targets recently. Q2 2026 earnings due July 29, 2026 - near-term volatility catalyst; stock recently oversold with sideways-to-bearish short-term technicals. Strategic angle: CPKC owns the only single-line Canada-US-Mexico rail network - a genuinely irreplaceable moat asset; assignment at a discount would be celebratable on business quality. Watch item: potential US transcontinental merger activity (UNP/NS) could reshape competitive dynamics.
research notes - methodology & caveats
All figures CAD millions, FY2022-FY2025 (FY ends Dec 31), from CPKC 10-K XBRL via SEC EDGAR, cross-checked against stockanalysis.com (exact match on every series). CRITICAL CONTEXT: Kansas City Southern was consolidated April 14, 2023 (previously an equity-method investment held in voting trust) - this drives the discontinuous 2022→2023 jumps in PP&E (22,385→51,744), goodwill (344→17,729), current liabilities, and D&A; these are acquisition-accounting artifacts, not organic spikes or deterioration. EBITDA = operating income + D&A, both from SEC XBRL (e.g. FY2025: 5,609 + 2,019 = 7,628). Inventory = 'Materials and supplies' line (us-gaap:MaterialsSuppliesAndOther). Long-term debt = us-gaap:LongTermDebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations (excl. current portion). Shareholders' equity = parent-attributable us-gaap:StockholdersEquity (excludes ~CAD 0.9-1.0B noncontrolling interests that appear from 2023; stockanalysis includes NCI). Treasury stock: no line on the balance sheet - as is standard for Canadian issuers, CPKC cancels repurchased shares, so the TS parameter reflects accounting convention rather than absence of buyback history. No preferred stock outstanding; no R&D expense line (railroad). Goodwill dip 2024→2025 (19,350→18,436) is FX translation on USD/MXN goodwill, not impairment.

Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 2.6%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)

Sentiment: Consensus Buy (23 analysts: 19 buy / 4 hold / 1 sell), avg 12-mo target ~$216 (range $170-236). Stock ~+3% on Jul 7 2026. Bull case: Hess integration lifted production +500 kbd YoY, $10-20B/yr buybacks. Caution: TD Cowen cut to $197 Hold Jul 2 2026 on near-term earnings pressure; the ~4.2% dividend is not fully covered by near-term earnings/organic FCF, raising capital-allocation risk in a soft-oil-price environment. Fundamentally healthy but structurally commodity-cyclical.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Integrated oil major, FY ends Dec. Window FY2022-FY2025 straddles the post-2022 commodity-price roll-off (2022 was a record-earnings year) which drives the declining EBITDA/RE trend. EBITDA built from primary income statement as pretax income + interest expense + D&A (D&A from cash flow), NOT stockanalysis 'operating income'+D&A: Chevron reports no clean operating-income subtotal and earns large equity-affiliate income (Tengiz/TCO, Chevron Phillips Chemical) that sits below the operating line, so OI+D&A understates true earning power and would wrongly fail EP in FY2024. My pretax+interest+D&A series (66509/47379/45382/41092) matches macrotrends EBITDA (66.5/47.4/45.4/~41.5) as a cross-check. FY2025 is distorted by the all-stock Hess acquisition (closed 2025): total assets 256.9B->324.0B, net PP&E 147.8B->219.7B (+48.7%, under the 60% spike threshold), LTD 20.1B->39.8B, and SE 153.2B->192.2B from shares issued to Hess holders -- which also explains treasury stock FALLING 74.3B->52.2B (treasury shares reissued in the deal) rather than growing on buybacks. No R&D line (has 'exploration expenses' ~$1.05B, not R&D). No preferred. Treasury stock present (buyback history). SE positive all 4 years so TSA-DSER is NOT excused; max stays 17. Retained earnings cross-checked against FY2025 10-K (205,365 / 205,852). Expect a re-score after the FY2026 10-K when the full first Hess year replaces FY2022 in the window.

Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 3.41 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding

Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 38.2% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Consensus Buy (28 analysts: 13 buy / 16 hold / 1 sell), avg 12-mo target ~$146 (range $111-175). Mid-turnaround: Q1 FY2026 beat, same-store sales +2% (traffic +1.4%, basket +0.5%), gross margin +65bps on lower shrink/damages, revenue +3.4% y/y. Stock had fallen ~30% over 3 months earlier but story is improving. CEO succession: JJ Fleeman (from Ahold Delhaize USA) named to succeed Todd Vasos effective Jan 1, 2027 - execution/handoff is the key watch item. Defensive discount-retail model performs well in a stretched-consumer environment; owning shares at a discount is defensible on business quality even where the lease-heavy structure caps the DCAP score.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Dollar General; fiscal year ends late Jan/early Feb (FY2022 ended Feb 3 2023 [53-wk], FY2025 ended Jan 30 2026). All figures from SEC 10-K XBRL (2 filings), $ in thousands converted to millions. EBITDA = operating profit + D&A (cash-flow D&A): FY22 3328.3+724.9; FY23 2446.3+848.8; FY24 1714.1+971.7; FY25 2203.7+1046.3. PPE TRAP AVOIDED: stockanalysis/aggregators report net PP&E ~$15.9-17.5B by bundling operating-lease right-of-use assets; SEC 'Net property and equipment' is $5.24B->$6.40B (used here). Long_term_debt = 'Long-term obligations' (debt only; long-term operating lease liabilities excluded). TREASURY STOCK = 0: DG retires repurchased shares (shares issued = shares outstanding), no treasury line on the balance sheet -> TS flag absent and treasury_stock_abs 0. Preferred authorized but $0 outstanding (blank line) -> absent. No R&D line (discount retailer). SE = total shareholders' equity (no noncontrolling interest). Total liabilities for FY22/FY23 derived as total assets - SE (matches; FY24/FY25 read directly). Watch-outs for scorer: RE growth is strong but partly an artifact - FY2022 RE ($1,656M) was depressed by heavy share buybacks (which DG charges to retained earnings on retirement); buybacks then paused, so RE recovered sharply (avg YoY ~40%). Lease-heavy retail balance sheet bloats total liabilities -> TSA-DSER structurally weak; consumables-heavy sales mean EBITDA < current liabilities each year (EP fails); NOCF/assets ~9% keeps RTA under the 11% tier. Goodwill flat at $4,338.6M every year (no acquisitions).

Sentiment: Constructive but merger-transition. DKS completed its ~$2.4B Foot Locker acquisition Sept 8 2025, creating a >3,200-store, ~$17B-revenue sporting-goods leader. Management guides FY2026 revenue +~27% (comps +2.5%, non-comps +24.4% from consolidating Foot Locker), $100-125M medium-term cost synergies, EPS-accretive in FY2026, with ~$150M of acquisition/integration costs this year. Early read positive: Q1 FY2026 revenue +~63% YoY, beating estimates; Seeking Alpha frames the deal as "bigger-than-expected winner." Core DKS comps healthy; risk is integration execution on a lower-margin, mall-based footwear business and merger-year balance-sheet noise. Stock ~$223 (near 52wk high $244), ~22x PE, 2.15% dividend. Assignment would be a quality retailer mid-integration - celebratable if you accept the Foot Locker bet.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Dick's Sporting Goods (specialty sporting-goods retailer). Fiscal year ends the Saturday nearest Jan 31: FY2022=Jan 28 2023, FY2023=Feb 3 2024 (53-wk), FY2024=Feb 1 2025, FY2025=Jan 31 2026. ALL figures from SEC EDGAR XBRL (primary). MERGER-YEAR CAVEAT: DKS closed the ~$2.4B Foot Locker acquisition Sept 8 2025, so FY2025 is a step-change - revenue $13.4B->$17.2B, inventory/assets/PP&E/goodwill all jump. PP&E TRAP AVOIDED: used SEC PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (1313/1638/2070/3513), NOT aggregator PP&E (3451/3896/4437/8107) which is inflated by operating-lease ROU assets. EBITDA = operating income (1463/1282/1474/1096, XBRL OperatingIncomeLoss) + D&A (365/394/400/489, XBRL DepreciationAndAmortization); cross-checks stockanalysis exactly. Long-term debt = XBRL UnsecuredDebt (senior notes) 1482/1483/1484/1905, plus $58M convertible-noncurrent in FY2022 -> balance-sheet LTD 1541; excludes operating-lease liabilities. Total liabilities = total assets - stockholders' equity (no noncontrolling interest / minority interest concept exists). No R&D expense line (retailer). Preferred stock = $0 all years. Treasury stock present and growing (3771->5031) from ongoing buybacks. SE positive all 4 years so TSA-DSER applies (max stays 17). Watch: the Foot Locker step-change inflates FY2025 PP&E (>60% YoY spike), pushes treasury-adjusted leverage back over 1.0, and dilutes EBITDA/RTA/margins in the merger year - classic merger-year drag; re-score after FY2026 10-K once Foot Locker is fully in the base.

Sentiment: Healthy, well-regarded industrial. Consensus Buy (BofA raised PT to $274 on solid results); strong Q1 2026 (revenue +10% y/y, +5% organic, adj EPS +11%), reaffirmed FY2026 guidance (5-7% revenue growth, double-digit adj EPS). No distress or scandal - a business we would be happy to own. The DCAP shortfall is structural (industrial working-capital + R&D + ESG-divestiture window artifacts), not a quality red flag.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Dover Corp, diversified industrial manufacturer (Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, Imaging & ID, Pumps & Process Solutions, Climate & Sustainability Tech). FY ends Dec 31; window FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026). EBITDA = operating earnings + D&A (SEC XBRL: OI 1279.6/1219.3/1206.4/1373.4 + D&A 295.7/305.0/337.8/379.6); matches stockanalysis EBITDA line. NOCF = total-company (continuing + discontinued) net cash from operating activities: FY2024 is depressed to 748.4 because discontinued-ops operating CF was -339.5 around the Oct-2024 sale of Environmental Solutions Group (ESG) to Terex (~$2.0B); continuing-ops OCF was 746.8/1219.5/1087.8/1338.0. The ESG divestiture gain (~$598M pretax, in discontinued ops) also inflated FY2024 net earnings ($2.70B) and drove the FY2024 retained-earnings jump (10995.6->13409.6, +22%), so RE avg growth (~11.9%) flatters the underlying trend. PP&E is pure SEC PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (NOT ROU-inflated; ROU sits separately); FY22->FY23 PP&E dip to 97.4% of prior just misses the 98% steady-rise gate. Total liabilities = total assets - stockholders equity (no material noncontrolling interest). Goodwill uses SEC XBRL (FY2022 4425.4, vs aggregator 4669). rnd_expense flagged PRESENT: Dover incurs ~$150-165M/yr R&D (XBRL-tagged, ~2% of revenue) but it is NOT a separate face line on the income statement (folded into COGS/SG&A) - an operator reading the flag literally as "no face-line R&D" = absent would add the I&NE no-R&D point (10/17, 58.8%); the honest business read (R&D genuinely present) = 9/17. Both are not-a-gift. RSH (latest EBITDA 1752.9 / SE 7405.2 = 23.7%) clears the 23% gate by a hair. Structural drags: EP fails all 4 yrs (current liabilities > EBITDA), RTA avg ~8.8% (below 11%), inventory not on a steady rise. Clean balance sheet otherwise (LTD 2/2, TSA-DSER ~0.48 avg 2/2, GW rising, preferred absent, buyback history intact).

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.13 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.8 years of EBITDA (cap 4)

Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -176.0%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)

Sentiment: Healthy, well-run business. FY2026 record results: sales +6% to $13.21B, adj EPS $10.64 (+11.4% y/y), blended SRS positive. Analyst consensus Buy (24 analysts, ~$226 PT); Citi Buy, Mizuho PT $235. LongHorn very strong (Q4 SRS +9.5% vs 7.1% est); Olive Garden softer (Q4 SRS +2.4% vs 3.2% est) drove a post-earnings dip. Constructive FY2027 guide (SRS +2.5-3.5%, EPS $11.10-11.35). Dividend raised, new $1.5B buyback. Nothing un-celebratable operationally; the DCAP ceiling here is structural (lease-heavy balance sheet + aggressive share retirement thins equity), not a business-quality red flag.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Darden Restaurants (Olive Garden, LongHorn, etc.). Fiscal year ends last Sunday of May; FY2026 ended May 24, 2026. FY2026 10-K not yet filed (Darden files mid-July); FY2026 figures taken from the SEC-filed Q4 FY2026 8-K exhibit 99.1 (full-year condensed consolidated statements), FY2023-FY2025 from filed 10-Ks. PP&E ROU TRAP AVOIDED: used SEC XBRL 'Land, buildings and equipment, net' (core PP&E: 3725/4184/4716/5049) NOT aggregator 'Net PP&E' which lumps in operating-lease right-of-use assets (~$4-4.9B extra; Darden leases most restaurant sites) and shows ~7.1/7.6/8.3B. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (OI 1201.8/1314.2/1362.3/1582.8; D&A 387.8/459.9/516.1/561.1); cross-checks stockanalysis reported EBITDA exactly for FY2023-FY2025. NOCF = net cash from operating activities of CONTINUING operations (SEC cash-flow statement); total-ops incl. small discontinued adj. differs by ~$5-8M. TREASURY STOCK ABSENT: Darden RETIRES repurchased shares (equity = 'Common stock and surplus' + 'Retained earnings (deficit)', no treasury line), which is why RE hovers near zero/negative despite ~$1B/yr net income; heavy buybacks (new $1.5B authorization FY2026) charged against RE widened the deficit to -108.4 in FY2026. FY2026 note: $693.6M current portion of LTD reclassified to short-term, so LTD fell 2128.9->1637.7 while current liabilities jumped to 3005.4. No inventory-free issue (carries food/beverage inventory). Preferred: 25.0M authorized, none issued/outstanding. No R&D line (restaurant operator). Expect a lease-heavy/buyback-thin-equity restaurant floor: EP fails (curr liab > EBITDA), TSA-DSER fails (TL/SE ~3.6-4.8x on thin retired-share equity), RE/TS zero on retirement -- contrast debt-free CMG (gift). Server scores.

Sentiment: Strongly positive / celebratable assignment. Buy consensus (~19 analysts, mean PT ~$450, ~10% upside as of Jun 2026). Foundational beneficiary of the AI data-center + grid electrification buildout - management cited ~240% growth in data-center-related orders, backlog growth, 8% FY25 organic growth, record FY25 EPS $10.45 (+10%) / adj $12.07 (+12%), and raised 2026 organic guidance to ~10%. Watch items: near-term margin pressure from capacity ramp, ~$11B of Q1-2026 M&A, and the planned Mobility spinoff (end of Q1 2027). No solvency/quality red flags - a business we'd be glad to own at the strike.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Eaton Corp plc - power management (electrical, industrial). Irish-domiciled plc; FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA built as operating income + D&A per DCAP spec (OI 3019/3885/4632/5209 + D&A 954/926/921/1006 = 3973/4811/5553/6215), matching stockanalysis EBITDA row. Carries a dedicated R&D expense line ($665/754/794/797M FY22-25) → rnd_expense present, which caps I&NE at 1/2. Treasury stock is nominal (~$1M FY22-24, none shown FY25) - Eaton as an Irish plc retires/cancels repurchased shares rather than holding treasury, so treasury_stock flagged absent and treasury_stock_abs is ~0 (adds nothing to TSA-DSER denominator). SE positive all 4 years, so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). Net PP&E series (3716→5084) looks like core PP&E, not ROU-inflated, and rises steadily with no >60% spike. Note FY25 saw ~$11B of Q1-2026 acquisitions (Boyd Thermal, Ultra PCS) and a planned Mobility-segment spinoff by end of Q1 2027 - both post-window events that could reshape goodwill/PP&E in the FY2026 10-K; rescore after that files.
