FERG - Ferguson Enterprises not-a-gift

Distribution Services / Plumbing-HVAC Distribution · price $228.92 · mcap $44.4B · vol 1,377,822 · PE 25.2 · PB 7.6 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 9/17 (53%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 11.6% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.23 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.3 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.42 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -1.5%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 51.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Constructive. Q1 CY2026 (quarter ended Mar 31, 2026): adjusted EPS $2.28 beat estimates (~$2.19); revenue $7.47B down 2.0% YoY on soft housing/construction, margins stable. Calendar-2025 results were strong and 2026 guidance issued; quarterly dividend $0.89 (payable 2026-07-08); analyst average target ~$275, some fair-value lifts to $300-325. #1 North American plumbing/HVAC distributor with dense branch network and scale-based moat - assignment would be owning the dominant distributor in a fragmented market, which we would celebrate; main cyclical risk is prolonged housing/repair-remodel softness.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fiscal years end July 31 (FY2022 = Aug 2021-Jul 2022). Company changed FY-end to Dec 31 after FY2025 (transition period Aug-Dec 2025), so these are the last 4 full fiscal years. EBITDA = operating income + D&A from 10-K XBRL (FY22: 2,820+301; FY23: 2,659+321; FY24: 2,652+335; FY25: 2,606+373); company's own 'adjusted EBITDA' runs slightly higher (FY22 $3,153M). PPE is the 10-K net PP&E line excluding operating-lease ROU assets (aggregators show ~2x by including ROU). IMPORTANT context on two series: Aug 1, 2024 re-domiciliation merger (Ferguson plc → Ferguson Enterprises Inc., new Delaware entity, CIK 2011641 vs old 1832433) retired ~$4.4B of treasury shares against equity - that is why treasury stock drops 3,936→899 and retained earnings drops 9,589→6,776 in FY2025. The RE decline is an accounting recapitalization, not operating losses (FY2025 net income $1,856M); the RE-growth parameter's zero is structural, same flavor as the SPGI merger-year drag. Inventory is real and large - plumbing/HVAC distributor. No R&D line (distributor). No preferred outstanding (authorized but none issued). LSE secondary listing cancels 2026-07-20 (housekeeping, NYSE primary unaffected).

Sentiment: Positive/high-quality franchise: consensus Buy/Strong Buy (~84% buy, 17 analysts), avg PT ~$90; Goldman PT $98 (2026-04-22). March 2026 DARTs +25% y/y to 4.33M, accounts 4.75M and growing; launched crypto trading (11 assets) in Europe. Best-in-class low-cost automated broker, debt-free, strong earnings growth. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable on business quality - the low DCAP is purely the structural broker-float/balance-sheet floor, not a red flag.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Interactive Brokers Group (electronic broker-dealer). FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed 2026-02-27. NO INVENTORY (financial services) -> inventory 0s. NO R&D line on income statement. GOODWILL ~0 and LONG-TERM DEBT ~0: IBKR is debt-free (Debt/Equity 0.00, no LongTermDebt/Goodwill XBRL tags) -> LTD/GW set 0. UP-C STRUCTURE TRAP: IBKR Group Inc consolidates IBKR LLC but owns a minority; total equity incl NCI = 11,615/14,067/16,597/20,472 with non-controlling interest 8,767/10,483/12,317/15,109. I use PARENT-attributable stockholders' equity (2,848/3,584/4,280/5,363) so SE, retained earnings and treasury stock are all parent-consistent (same convention as EQT/FCX/CMI Up-C parent-SE trap). RSH passes on either basis (latest EBITDA/parent-SE=91%, or /total-equity=24%); TSA-DSER fails on either. UNCLASSIFIED BROKER BALANCE SHEET: no current/non-current split; nearly all liabilities are payables to customers (segregated cash) due on demand, so current_liabilities is set = total_liabilities -> EP fails all 4 yrs (EBITDA $2-5B vs customer float $100B+), the same float-floor that hits AXP/CME/insurers. Huge, volatile OCF (3.97B->15.8B) is driven by movement in customer/broker segregated balances, not core earnings -> RTA ~3-8% (floored by the $115-203B balance sheet). Bright spots: LTD 2/2 (debt-free), RE 3/3 (retained earnings 1.29B->3.37B, ~38%/yr, all positive - genuine, not a sign-flip artifact), PS/TS/RSH. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (stockanalysis), cross-checked vs macrotrends EBITDA (3.17/3.81/4.60B FY23-25) - same steady rise. PP&E declines 93->60 (asset-light) so PPE param fails steady-rise.

Sentiment: MAJOR CORPORATE-ACTION OVERHANG -- human call. On 2025-08-25 KDP announced an $18.4B all-cash acquisition of Dutch coffee co JDE Peet's; stock fell ~11% on the news. Combined net leverage guided to ~4.5x, and KDP will then split via tax-free spinoff into TWO independent public companies: a Refreshment Beverage co (~$11B sales: Dr Pepper, 7UP, Canada Dry, A&W) and a Global Coffee co (~$16B sales: Keurig, Jacobs, L'OR, Peet's). Acquisition targeted to close ~early April 2026 (updated financing plan filed 2026-02-23). Assignment today is NOT celebratable in the classic sense: the entity is about to be dramatically re-levered and broken apart -- the 4-yr balance-sheet snapshot below will not describe either successor company. Underlying fundamentals of the current beverage/coffee business are steady, but the structure is in flux. Flag for operator.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Keurig Dr Pepper (CIK 1418135), beverage/coffee staples; FY ends Dec 31; all 12 series pulled from SEC EDGAR XBRL companyconcept (primary source) and match stockanalysis.com to the dollar. EBITDA built as operating income (EDGAR OperatingIncomeLoss: 2605/3192/2591/3575) + D&A (stockanalysis cash-flow: 709/720/733/753) = 3314/3912/3324/4328; the two sources' EBITDA agree. NOCF from EDGAR NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities. Carries real inventory (beverages/brewers) so 0s not used. NO treasury-stock line exists in the XBRL (no TreasuryStockValue/TreasuryStockCommonValue tag) -> treasury_stock_abs=0, flag absent (KDP returns cash via dividends/retirement, not a held treasury account). Preferred stock = 0 every year (EDGAR PreferredStockValue) -> absent. No separate R&D expense line on the income statement (typical beverage co) -> absent. SE positive all four years so TSA-DSER applies (max stays 17). Expected staples working-capital ceiling: EBITDA << current liabilities (EP fails 4x), low NOCF/assets (~4% -> RTA fails), TL/SE ~1.12 (TSA-DSER fails), EBITDA/SE ~17% (RSH fails). RE growth avg ~17.1% is genuine (steady climb off 2020-21 base, no sign-flip) but razor-thin at the 17% top tier -- it is what props the score up; strip RE and this is ~6/17.

Sentiment: Constructive. May 2026 Buy consensus (~12 analysts), PT ~$106; Q1 2026 revenue +14% YoY, EPS beat by ~21%, net income +31%. Adenza integration going well with rapid post-deal deleveraging; FinTech ACV bookings +50% YoY, crypto/digital-asset institutionalization tailwind. Revenue forecast ~6.9%/yr next 3 yrs (above industry). Main risk: FinTech client decision-making slowdown or Adenza integration shortfall. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable - dominant exchange franchise.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Nasdaq, Inc. (exchange + fintech/index-data operator), FY ends Dec 31. All figures from SEC XBRL companyconcept (primary), cross-checked vs stockanalysis.com. INVENTORY = 0 (exchange/software business carries none). EBITDA = operating income + income-statement D&A (SEC us-gaap DepreciationAndAmortization: 258/323/613/632; step-up FY24 = full year of Adenza intangible amortization, AmortOfIntangibles 153/206/488/487); confirmed identical on stockanalysis. PP&E = clean SEC PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (532/576/593/728) EXCLUDING operating-lease ROU assets - stockanalysis shows ~$976-1175M because it folds ROU in (PP&E ROU trap). SHAREHOLDERS_EQUITY = SEC parent-only StockholdersEquity, positive all 4 yrs (no TSA-DSER excusal). RETAINED_EARNINGS corrected: stockanalysis mislabels FY2023 as -$1,924M, which is actually accumulated OTHER comprehensive loss; true FY2023 RE per 10-K = $7,825M (AOCI-mislabel trap). Structural drags expected: exchange/clearing collateral (default funds & margin deposits) inflates current liabilities and total assets, flooring EP (EBITDA well below curr-liab all 4 yrs) and RTA (avg NOCF/assets ~6.8%); Adenza-financed leverage (LTD jumped 4.7B->10.2B FY23) plus clearing liabilities floor TSA-DSER (avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~1.79). Goodwill dips slightly FY23->FY24 (14112->13957) on FX of non-USD goodwill then recovers. RE avg YoY ~10% (genuine, one-tier). Exchange/clearinghouse pattern like CME/ICE/CBOE.

Sentiment: Strongly bullish (July 2026): ~78 analysts, 37 Buy / 12 Hold / 1 Sell, consensus Strong Buy. Q1-2026 revenue +16% YoY to $12.25B on ad-tier + price hikes + membership growth; 2026 guide 12-14% revenue growth, 31.5% operating margin, FCF raised to ~$12.5B, ad revenue target doubled to $3B (ad tier now 250M+ MAU), $30B+ buyback authorized. No governance/accounting overhangs; assignment would be celebrated on fundamentals - the DCAP drag is structural (content-liability float depresses EP/RTA, thin equity depresses TSA-DSER), not a red flag on the business.
research notes - methodology & caveats
NFLX fiscal year = calendar year (ends Dec 31); latest 10-K is FY2024. INVENTORY: Netflix carries no traditional inventory line - its content library sits in a separate "content assets" section (streaming content, current+non-current), so inventory=0 by nature, not missing data; this zeros the I&NE inventory-rise sub-point. GOODWILL: Netflix reports no goodwill line (no material M&A), so goodwill=0,0,0,0 and GW cannot score "increasing" - structural, not a data gap. R&D FLAG = present: the income statement line "Technology and development" ($2.27B→$2.93B FY21-24) is Netflix's R&D line (stockanalysis maps it to R&D); marking present costs the I&NE no-R&D sub-point. EP watch: EBITDA < current liabilities in ALL four years (FY24 10,747 vs 10,755 - misses by $8M), driven by large current content liabilities + deferred revenue float - EP likely 0/1. RTA dragged by FY2021 near-breakeven operating cash flow ($393M) before the 2022-24 FCF inflection. TSA-DSER watch: SE positive all years but avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~1.2 (thin-equity + $14B debt) likely 0/2. Genuine strength is RE growth (~35%/yr, all three tiers) and RSH (~43%). Treasury stock jumped FY23-24 ($824M→$13.2B) on the reinstated $30B+ buyback program.

Sentiment: Strong, growing cybersecurity leader (network + cloud + AI security + now identity via CyberArk, closed 2026-02-11). FY2026 Q3 revenue +31% YoY, full-year guidance raised to ~$11.42-11.43B; positive analyst tone with multiple price-target hikes (fair-value ~$218). Near-term GAAP profitability pressured by CyberArk/Chronosphere integration costs (EPS est. cut to ~$1.45), expected to fade over 12-18 months. No solvency/quality red flags - it is a business one would be happy to own; the low DCAP score is structural (deferred-rev float, thin book equity from buyback-and-retire), not a distress signal.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Palo Alto Networks; FY ends July 31 (latest completed = FY2025, 10-K filed 2025-08-29). All figures from the FY2025 10-K (FY25+FY24) and FY2023 10-K (FY23+FY22) consolidated statements. EBITDA = GAAP operating income + the cash-flow 'Depreciation & amortization' line (textbook): FY22 -188.8+282.6=93.8; FY23 387.3+282.2=669.5; FY24 683.9+283.3=967.2; FY25 1242.9+343.4=1586.3. NOTE: broad data-provider EBITDA (stockanalysis) also adds amortization of deferred contract costs / capitalized commissions (~$480M FY25), giving ~531/1087/1412/2092; that only lifts RSH (latest EBITDA/SE 26.7% vs 20.3%) and does not change the not-a-gift verdict. PPE = pure net property & equipment from the 10-K, EXCLUDING operating-lease ROU assets (ROU-inflated series from aggregators shows FY24 747>FY25 734 = a false decline; the clean line rises 357.8->354.5->361.1->387.3 and passes). No separate inventory line is disclosed (hardware built by contract manufacturers; inventory immaterial/lumped in prepaid & other) -> inventory=null, not 0. Long-term debt = 0 all years: the convertible senior notes were classified as CURRENT liabilities (near-term/convertible) and were $0 by FY25 (converted/settled) - no LTD line ever. Treasury stock absent: shares issued = shares outstanding (PANW retires, no buyback held in treasury). Preferred: none issued/outstanding. Structural read: deferred-revenue float ($6.3B current + $6.4B LT) puts current liabilities ~$8B, so EBITDA never covers CL (EP 0x4) - the classic SaaS deferred-rev floor. Thin equity (SE only $210M in FY22) makes avg TL/SE ~17x -> TSA-DSER 0/2. RE 3/3 rides a sign-flip (accum deficit -1667->-1227 then flips positive +1350->+2484) - an artifact, not organic compounding. RTA avg ~16.85% just misses the 17% second point. Post-window: CyberArk acquisition closed 2026-02-11 (identity security) will add goodwill and reshape FY26.

Sentiment: Consensus leans Buy (~11 Buys / 2 Holds / 3 Sells), avg PT ~$113; some recent trims ahead of earnings (Stifel $110, Barclays $95). FY2026 adj EPS guided $5.30-5.40 (+9% YoY), reported sales +2-4%. Growth story broadening beyond pool cycle - Flow +11% in Q1'26 (infrastructure/commercial demand); Water Solutions soft; residential cyclicality the main risk. Steadily improving free cash flow and de-levering (LTD 2317->1639). Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable - a durable, well-run water franchise, though the DCAP math lands short of gift.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Pentair plc - Irish-domiciled water treatment/filtration (Water Solutions, Flow, Pool segments). FY ends Dec 31. Carries genuine R&D line (~$92-100M/yr) so I&NE no-R&D point is unavailable; inventory also DECLINES (790->632.6) so I&NE fails outright. NO treasury stock line on the balance sheet - as an Irish plc Pentair retires repurchased ordinary shares (shares issued fell 164.8M->163.2M FY24->FY25), so treasury_stock='absent' and treasury_stock_abs=0 (buyback economics run through APIC, which fell 1501.7->1313.1). Equity section is only ordinary shares/APIC/RE/AOCI - no preference shares. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (reconciles exactly to stockanalysis EBITDA). All four years FY2022-FY2025 verified against SEC 10-Ks (FY2025 and FY2023 filings); stockanalysis matches to the decimal. Watch industrial working-capital floor pattern: EP borderline (EBITDA < current liabilities in FY22 & FY23), RTA ~9.7%, TSA-DSER avg TL/SE ~1.00 (razor).

Sentiment: Positive/high-quality but DCAP-penalized. FY2025 (ended 9/30/25) was a record year: record net revenues $14.07B, record net income $2.13B, record EPS $10.30; record Q4 revenues $3.73B. Smooth CEO transition - Paul Shoukry succeeded long-tenured Paul Reilly. Durable, well-capitalized wealth-management-led franchise with sticky advisor/client base and conservative balance sheet (low senior-note leverage). Nothing un-celebratable about owning the business; the sub-60% DCAP is structural - the rubric penalizes bank/broker float leverage (EP/RTA/TSA-DSER) the way it does IBKR and other financial-float names, not a red flag on RJF quality.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Raymond James Financial: diversified financial-services holding co (Private Client Group wealth mgmt, Capital Markets, RJ Bank, Asset Mgmt). FY ends Sept 30; FY2025 ended 2025-09-30. UNCLASSIFIED bank/broker balance sheet - no current/non-current split; current_liabilities is stockanalysis's synthetic proxy dominated by bank deposits + brokerage payables to customers/brokers (customer float), so EP (EBITDA ~$2.9B vs ~$72B) fails 0/4 as a float artifact, not weakness. NOCF whipsaws on segregated-cash/brokerage flows (FY23 -$3.5B), flooring RTA. Inventory=0: broker-dealer carries no merchandise inventory (trading securities are not DCAP inventory). EBITDA = pretax income + D&A, SEC-derived (pretax = SEC NetIncomeLoss + IncomeTaxExpenseBenefit: FY22 1509+513, FY23 1739+541, FY24 2068+575, FY25 2135+579; D&A DepreciationAmortizationAndAccretionNet 145/165/179/195); matches stockanalysis EBITDA in FY22/23/25 (stockanalysis FY24 was a corrupt duplicate of FY25). PP&E, retained earnings, treasury stock, goodwill, equity, assets, liabilities all from SEC XBRL (authoritative). SEC RE (8843/10213/11894/13604, ~15.4% avg growth) is materially higher than stockanalysis's mis-netted 8843/10213/10051/11684; treasury stock rises monotonically 1512->4022 (buy-and-hold, no retirement); pure Goodwill line 1422->1451 rises then flat (stockanalysis's intangibles-inclusive 1931->1847 falsely declines). LTD = senior notes payable (2038/2039/2040/3520; FY25 new issuance); LTD/assets 0.04 and LTD/EBITDA ~1.2 both pass. Shareholders' equity positive all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies (no neg-equity excusal) but bank leverage -> avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~5.4, fails 0/2. RSH razor: latest EBITDA/SE 2909/12503 = 23.3% (>=23%). Preferred stock outstanding (FY22 $120M, FY23-25 $79M) -> PS 0. Expected structural read ~9/17 not-a-gift: classic broker/bank float floor (EP/RTA/TSA-DSER + preferred zeroed), consistent with IBKR 9/17 broker-float pattern; genuine strengths are LTD 2/2, RSH, RE 2/3, PPE, TS, GW.

Sentiment: Analyst tone bullish-to-moderate-buy: 29 analysts, median PT $400 (range $268-$420), 15 Buy/10 Hold/1 Sell; ~5.6% upside to mean PT. Beat expectations in 3 of last 4 quarters; FY2026 guidance $11.50-$11.90 adjusted EPS (~9% growth). Management cites 'continued demand choppiness' (housing/DIY softness) but architectural-paint franchise, pricing power, and 4,800+ company-owned stores make assignment celebratable - dominant, boring, durable. New Square partnership for pro customers. Watch item: Q2 2026 earnings July 28, 2026 (outside a 30-45 DTE window opened today, but inside longer ladders).
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fiscal year = calendar year ending Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (stockanalysis series), cross-checked vs company-reported EBITDA in the FY2025 year-end press release (FY2024 $4,491.5M, FY2025 $4,480.1M - within ~1%, minor definitional variance). CRITICAL DISTORTION (FERG-like): in Q4 2025 SHW retired 29.5M treasury shares, reducing retained earnings by $7,996M and treasury stock by $8,584M (per FY2025 10-K). This is an accounting reclassification, not a business deterioration - it collapses FY2025 retained earnings to $1,029M (zeroing RE-growth points) and shrinks treasury_stock_abs to $84.3M (hurting the TSA-DSER denominator in FY2025). Underlying buyback history is long and heavy (~$1.5-1.8B/yr repurchased). Treasury flag = present ($84.3M remains). R&D flag: no separate R&D expense line on the income statement (R&D costs are absorbed into cost of goods sold and disclosed only in the notes) - flagged 'absent' per rubric wording; SHW does conduct paint/coatings R&D, so this is another CHD-style spirit-vs-letter call if it proves decisive. Retained earnings and OCF/assets/equity series cross-checked vs stocktitan.net.

Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 28.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)

Sentiment: FY2025 was a blowout: 290M paid subscribers in Q4, EUR 2.2B operating income and ~EUR 2.21B net income (second consecutive annual profit), a genuine margin-turnaround story. Analyst tone bullish through 2025 on the profitability inflection, but the stock trades at a rich growth multiple near all-time highs - assignment would be buying a high-multiple growth name, not a discounted fortress. Not the celebrate-an-assignment profile: the DCAP fails structurally on earning power (loss-history + subscriber-prepayment/deferred current liabilities floor EP), thin early equity (TSA-DSER), no inventory, and a still-negative retained-earnings deficit.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Spotify Technology S.A. (Luxembourg foreign private issuer; files 20-F/6-K, reports in EUR; FY ends Dec 31). No inventory line - music/podcast streaming service carries none (set to 0). R&D expense line present (~EUR 1.4B/yr). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (D&A from cash-flow stmt: 171/158/121/102), self-consistent with stockanalysis EBITDA. Big structural artifacts to flag for the scorer: (1) EP fails hard - FY22/FY23 EBITDA negative (pre-turnaround losses) and even FY24/FY25 EBITDA below current liabilities. (2) LTD FY25 = 0 because the EUR 1.5B 0% Exchangeable Notes (EUR 1,539M non-current at 12/31/24, ~EUR 1,654M carrying) were RECLASSIFIED to current liabilities at 12/31/25 as they come due 2026 - this is why current liabilities balloon to 6,085 and LTD drops to 0 (an AKAM-style reclass artifact, not deleveraging). (3) RE stays a deep accumulated deficit all four years (-3,647 -> -4,182 -> -3,044 -> -832) but shrinks sharply on FY24 (+EUR 1,138M NI) and FY25 (+EUR 2,212M NI) turnaround. (4) Goodwill declines net (FX on USD acquisitions Gimlet/Anchor/Megaphone). (5) Treasury stock jumps 262 -> 701 on FY25 buybacks. TSA-DSER: SE positive every year so parameter applies, but avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~1.48 (early thin equity) misses. Preferred stock: none outstanding (ordinary shares + non-economic beneficiary certificates only).

Sentiment: Constructive 2026 tone but not an 'assignment we'd celebrate' on quality grounds. Consensus Moderate Buy (~15 Buy of 23-29), avg PT ~$30, stock +18% YTD 2026 on fiber momentum (targeting 40M+ passings, 4M annual fiber adds); FCF $18B+ covers ~$8B dividend (yield ~4.2%, FCF payout ~21-22%) with $10B+ left for deleveraging. Bull case is fiber/mobility compounding; bear case is heavy debt load amplifying refi cost in a higher-rate world plus intense wireless competition (BNP downgraded to Neutral). Fundamentally a leveraged, capital-intensive carrier - improving but structurally not DCA fortress quality.
research notes - methodology & caveats
AT&T Inc. (T), Dec FY-end; last 4 FYs FY2022-FY2025 from FY2025 10-K (filed Feb 2026). Telecom-carrier balance-sheet floor. Key context: (1) FY2022 GAAP EBITDA ($13,434M) is depressed by ~$25B of impairments (WarnerMedia spin + Latin America/wireless) that drove GAAP operating income to -$4,587M; the huge FY22->FY23 EBITDA jump and FY24 dip mean I&NE 'steady rise' will not clear. (2) EP fails all 4 yrs: current liabilities (~$47-56B) exceed EBITDA (~$13-45B) every year - structural for a carrier. (3) RTA avg NOCF/assets ~9.2% (<11%). (4) LTD strong: avg LTD/assets ~0.31, latest LTD/EBITDA ~2.82. (5) TSA-DSER avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~2.1 (>1.0) - carrier leverage floor; SE positive all 4 yrs so parameter applies (max stays 17). (6) Preferred PRESENT: 5.00% Series A + 4.75% Series C perpetual preferred outstanding -> costs PS point. (7) RE is a sign-flip series: accumulated deficit -19,415 -> -5,015 -> +1,871 -> +15,768 (deficit erased, WarnerMedia-spin dividend reset); server computes YoY growth. (8) TS present (ongoing buybacks). (9) RSH latest EBITDA/SE ~35% (>=23%). (10) GW declining (67,895 -> 63,425, Warner-era write-downs) -> no point. Not expected to be a gift; behaves like the cable/telecom-leverage floor (CMCSA/CHTR).

Sentiment: Constructive. Consensus Buy (~14 analysts, avg PT ~$108.5 vs ~$102 spot); stock +16.6% past month, +15.6% y/y, near 52-wk high ($82.45-103.39 range). Analysts turned more optimistic into Q2 earnings (2026-07-28, inside 30-45 DTE window - flag for chain runs); projected Q2 EPS -4.65% y/y as the P&C pricing cycle softens off the hard market. Recent reinsurance/mortgage leadership changes read as continuity, not disruption. Key risks: catastrophe exposure, mortgage-credit sensitivity, softening reinsurance pricing. PE ~7.9, PB ~1.5 - assignment at a discount to a best-in-class specialty underwriter would be celebratable; the DCAP rubric's insurer-float ceiling (EP/RTA/TSA-DSER pattern) is the structural drag, not business quality.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Arch Capital Group Ltd (Bermuda) - specialty P&C insurance/reinsurance + mortgage insurance; Dec-31 FYE. Insurer caveats: no inventory (genuine 0s, insurance carrier); unclassified insurance balance sheet - no current-liabilities subtotal exists (nulls, not estimated), so EP cannot score, the known insurer-float pattern (PGR/TRV/WRB). Net PPE not disclosed (folded into 'other assets'; no PropertyPlantAndEquipment XBRL tag in company facts) - nulls. Balance-sheet series (assets, liabilities, SE, LTD, treasury, RE, goodwill) and NOCF all from SEC XBRL companyconcept/companyfacts (primary, FY2025 10-K filed 2026-02-26); retained earnings cross-checked vs stockanalysis - exact match all 4 yrs. Goodwill is PURE goodwill (us-gaap:Goodwill 342/345/571/607, rising on 2024 MidCorp/MCE acquisition) - stockanalysis's 'goodwill' 804/731/1351/1222 is goodwill+intangibles, not used. LTD = us-gaap:SeniorLongTermNotes (~$2.73B senior notes, flat). EBITDA from stockanalysis (op income + D&A); SEC-derived cross-check (pretax income + interest expense + D&A: 1799/3798/5050/5500) within ~7%, same steady-rise shape. SE includes $830M Series F/G non-cumulative preferred; positive all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies. Treasury stock present and growing ($6.41B, 240.8M shs at cost per FY2025 10-K) - active buyback, incl ~$1.9B repurchased in 2025. Preferred present -> PS point structurally lost. FY2022 EBITDA/NI depressed by cat losses (Hurricane Ian) + mark-to-market; 2023-25 hard-market underwriting drove the surge.

Sentiment: Analyst consensus Buy; median 12-mo targets ~$260-287 with modest upside (Jefferies raised to $265 Jun-2026; Morgan Stanley Overweight, $255 Jul-2026). AssuredPartners integration described as on track - adjusted EBITDAC margins holding ~32%, management guiding 5-9% organic growth for 2026 despite a softening P&C pricing cycle. Key risks: delivering ~$160M 2026 synergies on the largest deal in company history, and soft-market pressure on organic growth. Q1-2026 revenue $4.76B, diluted EPS $3.16. Nothing that would make assignment un-celebratable: durable #3 global broker, recurring-revenue model, 40+ year dividend history.
research notes - methodology & caveats
USD millions, FY2022→FY2025 (Dec-31 FYE). Insurance brokerage - genuinely carries no inventory (services), so zeros. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (stockanalysis.com); cross-checked bottom-up from EDGAR (NI+tax+interest+D&A = 2182/2183/3091/3623) - the 2023 gap is a one-time non-operating pension settlement charge below the operating line. Retained earnings verified by roll-forward (RE + NI − dividends ties within ~$7M each year, dividends from EDGAR). PP&E is as-filed EDGAR net PP&E (excludes lease right-of-use assets; stockanalysis's standardized figure includes them). Structural caveat: brokerage balance sheet carries large fiduciary premium payables/receivables, inflating current liabilities and total assets - this depresses EP and RTA versus operating reality. Equity roughly doubled in FY2024 from the ~$8.5B Dec-2024 stock issuance funding the $13.45B AssuredPartners acquisition (closed Aug 2025), which also drove the FY2025 goodwill jump to $22.6B. No treasury stock line in the XBRL filings; no preferred outstanding; no R&D line.

Sentiment: Bullish/constructive as of Jul-2026. AUM crossed $1T with record fee-related earnings (Q1 2026); Piper Sandler Overweight, PT raised to $157; 19-analyst consensus Buy, avg PT ~$150 (~+24% upside); FY2026 EPS consensus rising ($6.67->$7.67). Apollo/Athene Q2 2026 preliminary update: ~$350M pre-tax alt net investment income (~9% annualized). No governance/scandal red flags surfaced; assignment would be owning a premier alternative-asset/retirement-services franchise. Caveat: earnings are sensitive to credit spreads and Athene's spread-based insurance economics; the low DCAP score is a structural artifact of consolidating a large insurer, not a durability warning.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Apollo Global Management, Inc. (NYSE:APO) - alternative asset manager fully consolidated with Athene (retirement services/annuities) since the Jan-2022 merger, so the balance sheet is dominated by insurance assets/liabilities (~$461B total assets FY2025). FY ends Dec; window FY2022-FY2025. NO INVENTORY (asset manager/insurer) -> 0s. PPE = null: net property & equipment is not a separate face-of-balance-sheet line and is not XBRL-tagged (immaterial for a financial; grouped in other assets) -> genuinely unavailable, not estimated. current_liabilities: Apollo's balance sheet is UNCLASSIFIED (no current/non-current split); the figures are stockanalysis's 'current liabilities' which are essentially insurance-float liabilities (interest-sensitive contract liabilities, future policy benefits) -> EP/RTA will fail structurally on this insurance float, same pattern as insurers/CME clearinghouse in prior sweeps. shareholders_equity and retained_earnings are ATTRIBUTABLE TO APOLLO (ex-noncontrolling/Athene minority; total consolidated equity incl NCI is larger, e.g. $42.5B FY2025) and are positive all 4 years -> TSA-DSER applies (not excused). FY2022 EBITDA negative (-$4,290M) reflects Athene mark-to-market losses in the rate spike, not operating impairment. Treasury stock ABSENT: no treasury line in XBRL (repurchased shares retired). Preferred PRESENT: 6.75% Series A Mandatory Convertible Preferred ($1,398M, ~28.7M shares) issued Aug-2023 (mandatorily converts ~2026). Goodwill jump FY2025 (4,327->5,920) from acquisitions. RE YoY off a FY2022 negative base is a sign-flip artifact. Expect a structurally low DCAP (insurance-balance-sheet penalty on EP/RTA/TSA-DSER) despite a high-quality, growing business -> flag for operator judgment: the penalty is structural, not a quality warning.

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.06 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.2 years of EBITDA (cap 4)

Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 13.1%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 17.4% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Moderate Buy consensus (26 analysts: 10 strong buy, 13 hold, 2 sell-side), avg target ~$349. 33rd consecutive annual dividend increase (+5.2%) and a NEW $7.5B buyback authorization effective Jul 1 2026 - buyback culture intact. Beat EPS estimates 4 straight quarters; Q2 2026 earnings Jul 21, 2026 (note: inside a 30-45 DTE window - flag for stage-2). Main bear point: softening property-insurance pricing cycle could pressure even well-positioned carriers. Nothing un-celebratable about assignment: dominant global P&C underwriter, combined-ratio discipline, rising book value.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY2022→FY2025, calendar fiscal year, USD millions. P&C insurer specifics: (1) inventory 0s - underwriting business, genuinely carries none (no Inventory tag anywhere in SEC XBRL facts); (2) current_liabilities null all 4 yrs - Chubb files an UNCLASSIFIED balance sheet (standard for insurers; no LiabilitiesCurrent tag exists), so EP cannot be scored, correctly no-point; (3) shareholders_equity = parent StockholdersEquity per XBRL, excludes noncontrolling interest ($6,022M NCI appears FY2025 from Huatai consolidation); (4) EBITDA = Fiscal.ai/stockanalysis operating income + D&A (7144+285, 9362+310, 11173+323, 12511+301), cross-checked against independent XBRL derivation pretax income + interest expense + intangible amortization = 7340/10508/12519/14109 - same rising shape, recorded series is the conservative one; stockanalysis D&A exactly equals XBRL AmortizationOfIntangibleAssets; (5) PPE net from 10-K disclosures (rounded to $0.1B in filing), steady rise, max YoY +20.8%; (6) goodwill FY2024 dip (19,686→19,579, −0.5%) was FX translation, resumed rising FY2025; (7) LongTermDebt is the XBRL long-term debt line, excludes repurchase agreements/short-term (total debt incl. those: 15,185/14,803/15,598/17,649); (8) retained earnings double-sourced SEC XBRL = stockanalysis exactly; (9) preferred XBRL tags exist but are all pre-2010 legacy ACE facts - no preferred outstanding.

CI - The Cigna Group not-a-gift

Health Services · price $281.98 · mcap $74.6B · vol 1,907,785 · PE 12.2 · PB 1.8 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 8/17 (47%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence

Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 6.6% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.19 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.6 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.52 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 8.1%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 28.7% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Bullish street: consensus Buy/Strong Buy, PTs $345-379 vs ~$286 spot (~12x PE, cheap). Reaffirmed 2026 outlook (adj income >=$30.35/sh); Q1 2026 adj EPS $7.79 beat $7.60. FY2025 net income $6.0B ($22.18/sh) up from $3.4B. Key overhang making assignment less-celebratable: 2026 PBM reform (Consolidated Appropriations Act, signed Feb 3 2026) forces flat service-fee compensation in Medicare Part D + spread-pricing transparency, and an FTC settlement (Feb 4 2026) requires Express Scripts PBM changes by Jan 1 2027 - direct pressure on Evernorth, Cigna's profit engine. Business quality high; structural insurer/PBM balance sheet is what caps the DCAP score, not deterioration.
research notes - methodology & caveats
The Cigna Group (CI): managed-care insurer + Evernorth PBM (Express Scripts). FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026. EBITDA = operating income + D&A per spec (FY22: 8450+2937; FY23: 8536+3035; FY24: 9417+2775; FY25: 9200+2775) - D&A is genuinely 2775 in both FY24 and FY25 per SEC XBRL (two separate filings), not a scraping artifact. shareholders_equity is equity attributable to Cigna (excludes ~$160-210M noncontrolling interest that aggregator 'total equity' includes); SE positive all 4 years so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). Inventory is real (Evernorth home-delivery/specialty pharmacy drug inventory). 'current_liabilities' = reported LiabilitiesCurrent (Cigna uses a partially classified balance sheet); note insurance-liability structure means EBITDA<<current liabilities so EP fails structurally like other insurers/PBMs. LTD = LongTermDebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations (excl. current maturities). PP&E FY2023/FY2024 (3695/3654) from stockanalysis; FY2022/FY2025 (3774/3651) confirmed via SEC XBRL - PP&E is flat-to-declining (asset-light health services). Goodwill dipped FY2023 then recovered but net below FY2022 (Express Scripts legacy). No R&D line; no preferred; treasury stock present and growing on buybacks ($3.6B repurchased FY2025).

Sentiment: CSX +~35% YTD into mid-2026, buoyed by railroad M&A speculation: the pending UNP/Norfolk Southern merger casts CSX as a potential acquisition target for BNSF or CPKC, which could boost valuation. However, BNSF/Berkshire (Buffett) publicly cooled near-term merger talk; CSX and BNSF instead signed an Aug-2025 coast-to-coast intermodal service pact (not a merger). Analysts cautious: flat volumes, declining free-cash-flow margins, EBITDA down FY2024->FY2025. Wide-moat dominant Eastern-US duopoly rail, but assignment enthusiasm is tempered by the open corporate-action overhang and softening cash flows.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Class I railroad; FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (OI 5954/5499/5245/4521; D&A 1502/1607/1658/1680) - matches stockanalysis EBITDA series exactly. PP&E = XBRL PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet ('Properties, net'), NOT aggregator PP&E which folds in ~$0.5-1B of operating-lease ROU assets. Inventory = 'Materials, supplies and other' (railroads carry real inventory). Equity = parent StockholdersEquityIncludingPortionAttributableToNoncontrollingInterest; positive all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies (not excused). NO treasury-stock line: CSX RETIRES repurchased shares (equity = Common stock & other capital / Retained earnings / AOCI only), so treasury_stock_abs=0 and TS flag 'absent' despite an aggressive buyback history - buybacks are charged against retained earnings, which is why RE is roughly flat (~1%/yr avg) despite ~$3.4B/yr net earnings. RE FY2022=10229 uses the FY2024 10-K restated comparative for internal consistency with FY2023=9609 (as-originally-reported FY2022 was 10363). Goodwill = us-gaap:Goodwill (declining 319->325->239->80); aggregators show a higher ~502->267 series that bundles other intangibles - both decline, GW scores 0 either way. No R&D expense line (railroad). EBITDA and NOCF both declining FY2024-25 = freight-volume softness / cyclical, not distress.

gift at ≥60%

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 7.3% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)

Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.46 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding

Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 44.9% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Cyclical ag downturn: FY2025 net income ~$5.0B, roughly half the FY2023 peak ($10.2B). FY2026 net-income guidance raised to $4.5-5.0B but still ~6% lower YoY at midpoint; US/Canada large-ag industry demand guided down 15-20%, with CEO John May calling 2026 the cycle bottom. Tariffs and weak farm income (USDA forecasts 2026 net farm income −0.7%) are headwinds. Moat is real (dealer network, precision-ag/technology stack, buyback history), and Q1 FY2026 beat with upgraded guidance, but the stock (~$636, ~35x P/E) trades well ABOVE the ~$526 mean analyst target - assignment would mean buying a great business at a price that already prices in the recovery, near all-time highs. Assignment celebratable on business quality, less so on valuation.
research notes - methodology & caveats
All figures $ millions, fiscal years FY2022→FY2025 (FY ends late Oct/early Nov; FY2025 ended 2025-11-02, a 53-week year). Balance-sheet series (inventory, total assets, long-term borrowings, total liabilities, total stockholders' equity incl. noncontrolling interests, treasury stock, retained earnings, goodwill) taken from/verified against the 10-K consolidated balance sheets and EDGAR XBRL - exact matches with stockanalysis.com. PPE = the 10-K line 'Property and equipment - net' (6,056/6,879/7,580/8,079), which EXCLUDES lessor operating-lease equipment; stockanalysis.com shows ~15.7B by including it. Deere's balance sheet is unclassified (combined industrial + captive-finance), so current_liabilities is the stockanalysis.com standardized series; FY2025 reconciles exactly to short-term borrowings + short-term securitization borrowings + accounts payable/accrued (13,796+6,596+13,909=34,301). EBITDA = stockanalysis.com figure (their operating income + D&A; D&A series 1,895/2,004/2,118/2,229 confirmed via EDGAR XBRL). Caveat: Deere's captive finance arm makes EBITDA definition-sensitive (Deere's own XBRL OperatingIncomeLoss nets financial-services interest expense and runs ~2-3.5B lower), but no DCAP parameter verdict flips between definitions (EP fails and LTD/EBITDA≤4 + RSH≥23% pass under both). R&D expense line present ($2,311M FY2025). No preferred stock outstanding on the balance sheet; treasury stock present and growing (heavy buybacks). Capital note: at ~$636/share a CSP ties up ~$63.6k/contract - position-cap flag.

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 6.0% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.20 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.9 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative1/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.87 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 11.7%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)

Sentiment: Buy consensus (2026-07): ~26 Buy / 4 Hold / 1 Sell across 42 analysts, median PT ~$131 vs ~$99 spot (~+30%). Bull case = DTC streaming turning profitable, strong Cruise Line forward bookings, steady parks. Recent caution: Raymond James cut PT to $111 (still Outperform) citing Comcast Epic Universe theme-park competition. FY25 was a strong cash year (OCF $18.1B, +30% YoY) as streaming inflected to profit. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable; a discounted entry into a recovering franchise blue-chip.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Disney FY ends late Sep/early Oct (FY2025 ended 2025-09-27). EBITDA = operating income + D&A per DCAP spec (stockanalysis EBITDA line, which computes exactly this): FY22 6533+5163, FY23 5100+5369, FY24 8319+4990, FY25 13013+5326. Macrotrends reports a broader EBITDA (~$13.6B/16.8B/19.2B for FY23-25, incl. equity-method income and non-operating items); the definition difference changes NO DCAP parameter here (EBITDA is far below current liabilities ~$29-34B in every year, so EP fails 4x either way; EBITDA/SE and LTD/EBITDA outcomes also unchanged). shareholders_equity is PARENT-ONLY Disney shareholders' equity (excludes noncontrolling interests) per project convention: 95008/99277/100696/109869. Note: stockanalysis.com labels a "Total Shareholders' Equity" of 108378 (FY22) / 113012 (FY23) that lumps in Hulu redeemable NCI (~$9.0-9.5B mezzanine) - NOT used; the true total equity incl. NCI is 98879/103957/105522/114612. total_liabilities excludes the redeemable NCI (clean per balance sheet). Inventory is real (parks merchandise/content), rising steadily. Treasury stock climbing (907→7441) on FY24-25 buybacks. Goodwill declining (77897→73294) on impairments/dispositions. No R&D line, no preferred. LTD falling steadily (45299→35315) - deleveraging post-Fox.

DUK - Duke Energy Corp not-a-gift

Utilities · price $125.97 · mcap $98.2B · vol 3,864,858 · PE 19.8 · PB 1.9 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 8/17 (47%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)2/2inventory & EBITDA both rose steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 5.5% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)1/2avg debt/assets 0.40 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 4.9 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 2.65 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding0/1preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)3/3retained earnings grew 28.5%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 31.5% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Hold consensus (12 analysts, Jul 2026); price ~$123, avg PT ~$137-140. Healthy, boring, dominant regulated utility riding a data-center load-growth tailwind: enterprise load +1.5-2% 2026 accelerating to 3-4% 2027-2030; 7.6 GW of executed data-center service agreements (+2.7 GW in Q1), 7.8 GW high-confidence pipeline; $103B 2026-2030 capex plan, +14 GW capacity by 2030. Reaffirmed 2026 adj EPS $6.55-6.80 and 5-7% long-term growth. Q1'26 beat (rev +8.1%, EPS +13%). Nothing un-celebratable operationally; the DCAP ceiling is structural (leverage/capital intensity), not a red flag.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Regulated electric/gas utility, FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed, so window is FY2022-FY2025. EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A (FY22 6012+5843, FY23 7070+6084, FY24 7926+6419, FY25 8626+7704); OI confirmed against SEC XBRL OperatingIncomeLoss. Utility carries genuine inventory (fuel/materials). No treasury stock line on Duke's balance sheet (treasury_stock_abs=0, flag absent). Preferred stock outstanding ($973M FY24/25; $1,962M FY22/23) -> preferred present, PS scores 0. No R&D line (utility) -> gives I&NE the no-R&D point. shareholders_equity = total COMMON stockholders' equity (excludes preferred/NCI), consistent with prior utility handling; immaterial to TSA-DSER (fails ~2.6x either way) and RSH (~31% either way). WATCH: RE avg YoY growth ~28.5% (2637->2235->3431->5056) is a low-base artifact -- Duke's retained earnings is tiny (~$2-5B) vs ~$52B equity because ~65-70% payout, so small absolute swings = huge percentages; strip the 3 RE points and this is ~5/17. Structural utility floor otherwise: EP fails (EBITDA < current liabilities all 4 yrs), RTA ~5.5% (capital intensity), TSA-DSER ~2.6x (heavy leverage), LTD/EBITDA 4.9x latest.

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 11.0% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.33 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative1/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.87 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 11.3%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 38.0% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Healthy, well-regarded business (not a distressed name). Consensus Buy: of ~28 analysts, 17 Strong Buy / 2 Moderate Buy / 9 Hold; avg PT ~$317 (~12% upside). FY2026 EPS guide $8.03-8.23 came in slightly below the $8.37 consensus (mild guidance trim), Q2'26 EPS seen ~$2.08 (+10% YoY). No merger/spin/corporate-action overhang. Quality compounder trading rich; the DCAP shortfall is structural (working-capital/current-liability ceiling + razor-miss RTA), not a red flag about the company.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Ecolab Inc - water/hygiene/infection-prevention services + specialty chemistry; FY ends Dec 31, so window is FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026). All figures SEC-XBRL verified (CIK 0000031462). PP&E ROU TRAP AVOIDED: used SEC core PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (3293.4/3474.6/3752.4/4276.6) NOT aggregator figures (stockanalysis showed 3742/4028/4476/5043, inflated by capitalized software / lease-adjacent assets). Shareholders_equity is PARENT-ONLY StockholdersEquity (7236.1/8044.7/8757.3/9770.8); aggregators show FY24/FY25 as 8789/9805 by lumping ~$32-34M noncontrolling interest - kept parent-only. EBITDA = SEC OperatingIncomeLoss (1562.5/1992.3/2802.4/2737.6) + D&A (Depreciation 618.5/616.7/634.9/672.6 + AmortizationOfIntangibleAssets 320/307/301/304); cross-checked vs stockanalysis EBITDA (2501/2916/3738/3714) - agree. Retained earnings SEC-exact and cross-checks stockanalysis. R&D FLAG NUANCE: income-statement FACE carries NO separate R&D line (folded into cost of sales / SG&A), but Ecolab discloses material R&D in the notes ($190/192/207/202M FY22-25, XBRL-tagged ResearchAndDevelopmentExpense) - flagged 'present' (spirit-of-Buffett: the business genuinely spends on R&D). An operator reading it 'absent' (no face line) would add the I&NE +1 but does NOT flip the verdict (see below). Preferred: authorized but NONE outstanding -> absent. Structural picture: EP fails all 4 yrs (current liabilities 4210-5535 >> EBITDA 2501-3714, like a staples/services working-capital floor); RTA razor-misses tier-1 (avg NOCF/assets = 10.97%, under 11% by 0.03pp); TSA-DSER 1/2 (avg TL/(SE+treasury) 0.865, clears 1.0 not 0.8); RE 1/3 (avg YoY ~11.3%). PP&E/LTD/RSH/PS/TS strong. Even best-case (R&D absent + GW credited) tops out ~10/17 (58.8%) - a not-a-gift by the structure, not a data gap.

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 24.3% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.00 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)

Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -7.8%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 47.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Neutral. Analyst consensus is Hold - ~19 analysts, median 1Y PT ~$128 (range $95-166), a mix including a couple Strong Sell. Business is fundamentally sound: 1Q2026 EPS $1.72 (+16% YoY), revenue $2.78B (+4.4%), net income +13%; airfreight tonnage +5% and double-digit growth in customs brokerage/transcon/distribution offset ocean-freight volume −4%. Main overhang is structural ocean overcapacity and broad freight-demand softness. Nothing scandalous - assignment here would not be un-celebratable; the strategy question is purely whether the DCAP quality bar is cleared.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Expeditors International - asset-light, non-asset-based freight forwarder / 3PL; FY ends Dec 31; all figures from SEC EDGAR XBRL 10-K facts (CIK 746515). (1) INVENTORY = 0 all years: pure logistics service business, no inventory line on the balance sheet. (2) EBITDA built = operating income + D&A from SEC (OI: 1824.37/939.93/1041.32/1052.55; D&A: 57.34/67.76/61.09/56.77). Note stockanalysis mislabels FY2025 EBITDA as 1053 (= OI only, forgot D&A) - trap avoided. FY2023 EBITDA depressed by the post-COVID freight recession (revenue 17.1B→9.3B). (3) LONG-TERM DEBT = 0 all years: EXPD is famously debt-free - no LongTermDebt/LongTermDebtNoncurrent XBRL tag; only obligations are operating-lease liabilities (~423-460M noncurrent), which are NOT debt. (4) PP&E = SEC core PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (~450-502M); aggregators ROU-inflate it to ~1,010M by bundling operating-lease right-of-use assets - ROU trap avoided. PP&E declines then ticks up (502→479→449→462), so no steady rise. (5) TREASURY STOCK = 0 / flag absent: EXPD constructively retires all repurchased shares (no TreasuryStockValue tag), so heavy buybacks show up as shrinking retained earnings rather than a treasury line - a retirement artifact that costs the TS point and drives RE growth negative (RE 3311→2581→2455→2538). (6) SE = parent stockholders' equity (all positive → TSA-DSER applies); NCI is tiny (~1-4M). Total liabilities derived = total assets − total equity incl. NCI (matches stockanalysis 2477/2132/2529/2536). Preferred stock value = 0 (tag present, all zero). No R&D line (service business).

Finance - exchanges/data · price $134.93 · mcap $76.3B · vol 5,315,142 · PE 19.4 · PB 2.5 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 8/17 (47%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 2.8% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.13 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.9 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 3.89 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 10.7%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 22.4% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Constructive. Buy consensus (8 analysts: 38% Strong Buy, 50% Buy, 13% Hold, 0% Sell), avg PT ~$192 vs ~$154 last (+25%). Record FY25: net revenue +7%, operating income +14.4%, diluted EPS +20.7%; FY26 adj-EPS est +17.4% to ~$8.16; beat consensus last 4 quarters. Mgmt guides mid-single-digit exchange growth for 2026. Slight recent top-line miss in Exchange + Mortgage Technology segments; rising expenses a margin watch. Announced GPU compute futures with NATIVX (TD Cowen lifted PT to $193, Buy). Quality franchise, wide moat - a name we would be comfortable owning; the low DCAP score is a rubric artifact of consolidated clearing float, not a business-quality concern.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE) - exchange/data/mortgage-tech operator; FY ends Dec, latest 10-K = FY2025. NO physical inventory (exchange/software/services) → inventory 0s; no R&D expense line on income statement. CLEARINGHOUSE FLOOR (like CME 7/17): ICE Clear margin deposits & guaranty funds are consolidated as pass-through current liabilities/assets, inflating current_liabilities to $84-149B and total_assets to $137-194B - this structurally fails EP (EBITDA<<curr liab all 4 yrs), RTA (NOCF/assets ~2-3%), and TSA-DSER (TL/(SE+treasury) ~2.9-5.9). The FY2022 spike (curr liab $149B, assets $194B) is clearing-balance fluctuation, not operations. Goodwill jumps $21.1B→$30.6B FY2022→FY2023 from the Black Knight acquisition (closed Sep 2023), then flat - still net-increasing so GW scores. EBITDA built as operating income + D&A (no separate EBITDA line): OI 3638/3694/4309/4929 + D&A 1031/1215/1537/1560. Treasury stock present & growing (buyback history). RSH razor-miss: latest EBITDA/SE = 6489/28991 = 22.4% vs 23% tier - misses by 0.6pp. SE positive all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). Contrast CBOE (13/17 gift): CBOE's options clearing runs through OCC off-balance-sheet, so it escapes the pass-through float that floors ICE/CME.

Sentiment: Assignment is hard to celebrate right now: KMB is mid-transformation. Nov-2025 it agreed to acquire Kenvue (~$40B, stock + $6.7B cash), roughly DOUBLING share count (~280M new shares) and materially raising leverage on an already high-debt, weak-dividend-coverage balance sheet; deal reshapes KMB into branded consumer health. Concurrently selling 51% of its International Family Care & Professional (tissue) business into a Suzano JV for ~$1.7B, closing Q3 2026 - so the FY2022-25 financials scored here will not describe the post-deal entity. Stock ~14% below the ~$114 analyst target; EPS forecast +10%/yr, but analysts flag weak dividend coverage and acquisition-financing debt risk. Operator call warranted: pending mega-merger + JV carve-out make this a moving target regardless of DCAP.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Kimberly-Clark, consumer staples (tissue/personal care), FY ends Dec 31; window FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026). Carries real inventory (raw pulp/finished goods). EBITDA = operating income + D&A, both confirmed via SEC XBRL (OI: 2681/1928/2700/2351; D&A: 754/753/781/805). shareholders_equity is TOTAL equity incl. noncontrolling interests (958/1094/1012/1652) so TL+SE=TA reconciles exactly; SEC us-gaap:StockholdersEquity is parent-only (547/915/840/1502) - NCI trap, but both positive all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER is NOT excused and neither SE basis changes any parameter outcome. Equity is razor-thin (heavy treasury/buybacks ~$5-6B) - classic staples working-capital/thin-equity floor: current liabilities > EBITDA every year (EP fail), TL/(SE+treasury) ~2.4 (TSA-DSER fail). Inventory declining and PP&E/goodwill dipped in FY24 (I&NE inventory leg, PPE, GW all miss). RE growth ~5.5%/yr avg (FY22 8201 -> FY25 9611) misses 7% tier. Bright spots: RTA ~17.8%, LTD/assets ~0.41 + LTD/EBITDA ~2.1, no preferred, treasury present, RSH ~191%. MAJOR corporate-action overhang, see sentiment. Cross-checked RE and operating income against SEC EDGAR primary (exact match to stockanalysis).

Sentiment: Buy-leaning consensus (mid-2026): ~11 analysts Buy, median PT ~$74 vs ~$58.5 last (implied ~25% upside), but stock near 52-wk low ($54.15 on 2026-07-01) off a $76.58 March high. FY2026 guidance held: ID sales ex-fuel +1-2%, EPS $5.10-5.30, FCF $2.7-2.9B. E-commerce +19% YoY (~$16B run-rate). Renewed M&A: $1.65B Giant Eagle deal (~$9B sales) after the collapsed Albertsons merger (paid $1B breakup fee, hit FY2024 equity). Margin pressure (freight, diesel, egg deflation) weighing on sentiment. Durable dominant grocer but thin-margin staples business - assignment is ownable, not celebratable.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Kroger Co. (grocery retailer), fiscal year ends Saturday nearest Jan 31: FY2021=Jan29-2022, FY2022=Jan28-2023, FY2023=Feb3-2024, FY2024=Feb1-2025. PP&E = balance-sheet "Property, plant and equipment, net" (incl finance-lease ROU, EXCL operating-lease assets) from SEC XBRL: 23789/24726/25230/25703 - the ROU trap: aggregators (stockanalysis) report PP&E of ~30-32B because they fold in the separate operating-lease-asset line (24726+6662=31388); XBRL clean figure used instead. EBITDA = operating income + total (cash-flow-statement) D&A: OI 3477/4126/3096/3849 + D&A 3429/3579/3750/3849 (income-statement D&A is lower at 2824/2965/3125/3246 because Kroger classifies some depreciation in merchandise costs; used total D&A for completeness). long_term_debt = XBRL LongTermDebtNoncurrent (excludes finance-lease obligations, which add ~1.5-1.8B). shareholders_equity all positive but FY2024 fell to 8.3B on heavy buybacks + $1B Albertsons breakup fee. No R&D line (grocer). Preferred authorized but none outstanding. Large active treasury stock. Inventory from stockanalysis (XBRL InventoryNet dimensional). Expect a classic grocery-staples/retail working-capital floor: EBITDA << current liabilities (EP fails 4x), RE growth ~6%/yr under the 7% tier, goodwill declining (GW 0), TSA-DSER >1 on treasury-thinned equity - comparable to COST 9/17 and my other staples names; not-a-gift.

Sentiment: Very positive/high-quality. Wide-moat industrial-gas duopoly (with Air Liquide); 2026 consensus "Strong Buy"/"Buy" across 24-27 analysts, mean PT ~$543 (~8% upside), Street-high $585. Q1 2026 beat, guidance low-end raised; BMO/RBC/BofA raised targets citing pricing power, US refining/electronics/manufacturing demand, hydrogen backlog (~$10B project backlog, Woodside low-carbon H2). Assignment would be genuinely celebrate-able as a business; the DCAP drags are structural artifacts (working-cap EP floor, buyback-driven RE volatility), not quality concerns.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Linde plc (LIN), world's #1 industrial gas company; FY ends Dec 31; USD millions. EBITDA = GAAP operating profit + D&A (from cash-flow stmt): FY22 5369+4204=9573, FY23 8024+3816=11840, FY24 8635+3780=12415, FY25 8923+3763=12686. FY2022 GAAP OI depressed by Russia/cost-reduction charges (adj OP ~$9B). shareholders_equity = PARENT 'Total Linde plc shareholders' equity' per SEC us-gaap:StockholdersEquity (40028/39720/38092/38245); aggregators (stockanalysis) report ~41387/41095/39488/39741 INCLUDING ~$1.3-1.5B noncontrolling interests -> using parent equity per project convention (NCI trap). Watch RE: FY2023 RE cratered 20541->8845 because Linde charged a large treasury-stock retirement/buyback program against retained earnings, then rebuilt to 16608 by FY25 -> RE net-declines oldest->newest and avg YoY ~+5.8% likely 0/3. EP: current liabilities exceed EBITDA every year (industrial working-cap/short-term-debt floor) -> EP likely 0/1. Real R&D line (~$143-150M/yr) so no I&NE R&D point. Preferred authorized but none outstanding. Treasury stock held (not fully retired). PP&E is genuine plant (asset-heavy gas plants), not ROU-inflated; FY25 +14% is capex, not a spike.

gift at ≥60%

Sentiment: Mixed-to-constructive. FY2025 strong recovery: sales $75.0B (+6%), record backlog ~$186-194B, cash from ops $8.6B, net earnings $5.0B ($21.49 EPS) after a rough FY2024 marred by classified-program losses. Tailwinds: F-35 (cleared the stored-jet delivery backlog by mid-2025), PAC-3, CH-53K, missile demand, Golden Dome missile-defense push. Overhangs: lost NGAD/F-47 to Boeing, F-35 execution/cost scrutiny, Q1-2026 EPS miss + cash-flow slump. Analyst consensus ~Hold, median PT ~$595-665. Defense prime is a durable franchise, but this is a structural DCAP floor, not a quality-driven low score.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Defense prime (Lockheed Martin), FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = reported consolidated operating profit + D&A (from cash-flow stmt): FY22 8348+1404, FY23 8507+1430, FY24 7013+1559, FY25 7731+1687. FY24 EBITDA dip reflects ~$1.7B Q4-2024 classified-program loss reserves + Aeronautics charges; recovered FY25. LMT reports no separate R&D expense line (company-funded IR&D runs through cost of sales) -> rnd 'absent'. LMT RETIRES repurchased shares against retained earnings, so there is no treasury-stock line (treasury_stock_abs=0, TS flag 'absent') and retained earnings declines every year (16,943->15,398->14,551->14,034) despite profitability -> RE growth 0/3, a share-retirement artifact like LHX/CSX/COST. No preferred outstanding. IMPORTANT DATA TRAP: stockanalysis.com mislabels FY24/FY25 'retained earnings' as the accumulated-other-comprehensive-loss line (-8,452 / -7,542); real RE is 14,551 / 14,034 per the 10-K and Q4 earnings release. SE positive all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies (not excused); thin buyback+pension-AOCI-eroded equity (SE ~6-9B vs TL ~44-53B) floors TSA-DSER. Expected ~8/17 (not a gift): defense working-cap/contract-liability floor kills EP (EBITDA<current liabs 4x); TSA-DSER 0; RE 0; TS 0. Bright: LTD 2/2, PPE, PS, RSH, GW rising, RTA ~14%. Compare [[lhx-defense-prime-goodwill-floor]] (6/17).

Sentiment: Recovering from the worst of the 2024-25 semi downturn. Q3 FY2026 (reported Feb 2026) showed broad-based demand recovery: April bookings near a 4-year high, book-to-bill >1, inventory correction and underutilization costs easing. Analyst tone positive post-Q3: JPMorgan raised PT $85->$95, Evercore $83->$93, avg rating "Buy", 12-mo target ~$113 (~27% upside). Growth drivers: data-center solutions ($303M FY25 -> ~$500M FY26 projected, PCIe 6.0/CXL retimers for AI), edge-AI MCUs, PQC devices. Risks flagged: dividend NOT covered by earnings (~8x) or cash flow (~120% payout) though maintained; possible 32-bit MCU share loss; auto/industrial still soft. Assignment celebratable as a dominant embedded-control franchise bought at a cyclical low - but the FY25 trough will suppress several DCAP parameters until FY2026 numbers roll in.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Microchip Technology; fiscal year ends March 31 (FY2025 = year ended 2026-03-31... note: FY labels per Microchip convention, FY2025 ended Mar 31 2025). Semiconductor (MCU/analog) name in a SEVERE cyclical trough: FY2025 revenue ~-42% y/y, EBITDA collapsed from $3.45B (FY24) to $1.05B (FY25) on an industry-wide inventory correction + factory underutilization charges - classic cyclical-zeros story like AMAT/LRCX. Carries inventory (fabless-lite/IDM chipmaker). EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A and reconciles to the income-statement EBITDA line each year. Retained earnings DROPPED FY24->FY25 ($6,760M->$5,781M) because the ~$1B annual dividend (94 consecutive quarters) exceeded depressed FY25 net income - RE growth pt likely dented. Has a large 10-K R&D line (~$1B/yr), so I&NE no-R&D pt cannot score. Treasury stock present and rising (buyback history). No preferred. Goodwill essentially flat (~$6.67-6.69B) with only tiny upticks - GW-increasing pt is razor-thin. LTD/EBITDA elevated in FY25 (5,630/1,046 ~5.4x) due to trough EBITDA. Rescore after FY2026 (recovery underway: Q3 FY26 bookings at ~4-yr high, book-to-bill >1).

Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 18.9% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Mixed/pressured. FY2025 and Q1 2026 defined by record cocoa-cost inflation: Q1 2026 adj. operating income -19% cc, net margin fell to 6.6% from 9.9% y/y despite a revenue/EPS beat. Organic revenue growth resilient (~3%) but volumes soft amid weak US consumer confidence and price elasticity. Cocoa coverage now ~10 months (highest in a while), so 2026 reaffirmed; analysts see margin recovery as a pricing/cost-structure story into 2027. Stock +15% YTD 2026. No litigation/governance overhang; a durable snacking franchise but currently earning below its own norm - assignment defensible but not celebrated at a full DCAP tier.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Mondelez International; FY ends Dec 31. Global snacks/confectionery (Oreo, Cadbury, Milka, Toblerone) - carries real inventory. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (cash-flow-stmt D&A): FY22 3534+1107, FY23 5502+1215, FY24 6345+1302, FY25 3548+1358; matches stockanalysis EBITDA series. No R&D expense line (reports SG&A). No preferred outstanding. Treasury stock large and rising (active buybacks, ~$2.4B in FY2025). DATA TRAP: stockanalysis.com shows treasury stock $0 for FY2024/FY2025 - that is a data-mapping artifact; the actual 10-K treasury-stock-at-cost is $29,349M (FY24) and $31,533M (FY25); FY22/FY23 ($25,794/$27,174) from stockanalysis are consistent with the rising series. shareholders_equity = total equity incl. NCI (NCI ~$50M, immaterial). KEY DYNAMIC: FY2025 EBITDA/operating income collapsed (7647→4906) on record cocoa-cost inflation crushing margins - this drags I&NE (EBITDA not on a steady rise), RSH (latest EBITDA/SE ~19%), and RE growth (FY25 RE flat/slightly down). Classic consumer-staples working-capital floor: EBITDA well below current liabilities all 4 years (EP fails). Treasury adjustment likely carries TSA-DSER (avg TL/(SE+|treasury|) ~0.79).

DCAP 8/17 (47%)
gift at ≥60%

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.38 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.7 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.37 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 2.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)

Sentiment: Neutral-to-improving. Q1 2026 (reported Apr 2026) beat: revenue $19.44B vs $18.94B est, core EPS $1.61 vs $1.55; PepsiCo Foods North America returned to +2% volume growth, a turnaround from prior softness. FY2026 guidance reiterated: organic revenue +2-4%, core cc EPS +4-6%, back-half weighted. Analyst consensus Hold, PTs ~$168-170 (limited upside); 8/22 Strong Buy, 13 Hold, 1 Strong Sell. Assignment would be celebrated: a dominant global beverage+snack franchise (Frito-Lay, Gatorade, Pepsi, Quaker) with durable moat, decades of dividend growth, bought at a discount.
research notes - methodology & caveats
PepsiCo, fiscal year ends late December (FY2022=12/31/22, FY2023=12/30/23, FY2024=12/28/24, FY2025=12/27/25). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (verified each year: 11512+3280=14792, 11986+3518=15504, 12887+3815=16702, 11498+4178=15676). D&A cross-checked against cash-flow statement. Retained earnings confirmed exactly against SEC XBRL (67800/70035/72266/72788). No separate R&D expense line on the income statement (staples/beverage-snack maker) -> rnd_expense absent. Preferred stock: ESOP convertible preferred fully redeemed in 2018, $0 outstanding since -> absent. Large treasury stock (~$40-42B, buyback history) -> present. Carries substantial inventory (physical goods maker), so inventory is genuine, not a zero. shareholders_equity is total equity (balances total assets = total liabilities + equity). FY2025 total assets/LTD stepped up (~$107B assets, $42B LTD) partly on higher debt.

Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.46 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 3.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative1/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.97 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding0/1preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -126.1%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 36.3% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Consensus "Buy"/Hold (19 analysts: ~30% buy, ~69% hold, 0% sell); 12-mo target ~$328, near spot. UBS Neutral PT $314, Citi Buy PT $363. Soft near-term fundamentals: management guides FY2026 same-store revenue -2.2% with possible NOI decline; limited 2026 demand catalysts (weak housing turnover), stabilization thesis hinges on rate cuts. FY2025 net income fell ~14% y/y largely on $215.6M FX losses (Canadian expansion). Durable franchise but a cyclically soft, rate-sensitive REIT - assignment would be owning a quality name into a flat-demand year, not a bargain-of-the-decade gift.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Public Storage - self-storage REIT, FY ends Dec. No inventory (REIT - real estate, not goods; 0s genuine). No treasury stock line (REIT). Large preferred stack (~$4.35B outstanding, in total equity). shareholders_equity = total equity as reported (includes preferred + noncontrolling interests). current_liabilities from stockanalysis's classification of accrued/other liabilities (PSA's B/S is largely unclassified - storage tenants pay monthly, so working-capital liabilities are tiny vs EBITDA). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (FY25: 2236+1152=3388). Retained earnings is a growing accumulated deficit (REIT distributions exceed cumulative earnings): -110→-268→-699→-1219, monotonically more negative → RE 0/3. Goodwill lumpy (Simply/Ezstorage/BREIT acq's + FX), not a steady rise → GW 0. Preferred present → PS 0. No treasury/buyback → TS 0. Positive equity all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies (avg TL/SE ~0.97 → 1/2). Low working-cap liabilities let EP pass 4x and low LTD/EBITDA (~3.0x) gives LTD 2/2 - richer than tower/net-lease REIT floors (AMT 4/17), but distribution model + preferred + lumpy GW cap it. Expect ~8/17 - storage-REIT floor, not a gift.

Sentiment: Cyclical energy name under active Elliott Management pressure - Elliott ran a 2025 proxy fight (partially seated board) pushing to break up/spin the midstream business to unlock sum-of-parts value; PSX survived it. Analyst tone mixed-to-constructive: avg PT ~$140 (upside ~30%+ from screen levels), high targets to $220 (TD Cowen Buy). Refining remains mature/cyclical (FY24 margin trough) while midstream/NGL export growth is the bull case. Assignment would be owning a discounted, well-capitalized energy conglomerate mid-restructuring - celebratable but with breakup/cyclicality overhang worth a human call.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Phillips 66 (refining + midstream + chemicals JVs + marketing), FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A; matches stockanalysis EBITDA exactly and includes equity earnings from affiliates (CPChem, WRB, DCP), which is a large, genuine part of PSX earnings. FY2024 is a refining-margin trough (EBITDA 3,653 vs 9,903 in FY2023, 11,189 in FY2022), a cyclical low, not a structural break. shareholders_equity series is TOTAL equity including noncontrolling interests (so TL+SE=total assets closes); SEC parent-only Phillips 66 stockholders' equity runs ~$1.1B lower (29,494/30,583/27,408/29,093) but that choice does not cross any TSA-DSER or RSH threshold. All SE positive so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). No R&D line (refiner). No preferred outstanding (SEC PreferredStockValue absent). Large, growing treasury stock from sustained buybacks. Inventory, current liabilities, total liabilities, D&A, retained earnings, goodwill, total assets, treasury (2022) all confirmed exactly against SEC XBRL.

Sentiment: Hold consensus (28 analysts: ~23% buy, 65% hold, ~12% sell); wide price-target dispersion $47-$105 reflects genuine turnaround uncertainty. Core worry is competitive: Apple Pay ~55% US mobile-wallet share vs PayPal+Venmo ~30%; Stripe/Adyen gaining in unbranded processing; branded checkout TPV growing only ~1%. Q1'26 TPV +11% but net income -14% YoY; guidance flat-to-slightly-positive with a high-single-digit Q2 adjusted EPS decline. Bright spots: Venmo revenue +20%, BNPL volume >$40B. A quality balance sheet, but a decelerating, margin-pressured franchise - assignment would not be an obvious celebration; branded-checkout stagnation is the human call.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026. Fintech/payments processor - carries NO physical inventory, so inventory=[0,0,0,0] (genuine, not missing). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (income stmt has no separate D&A line; D&A taken from cash-flow stmt): FY22 3837+1317, FY23 5028+1072, FY24 5325+1032, FY25 6065+963 - all reconcile. Big current-liabilities figures are settlement float ("funds payable and amounts due to customers" + customer accounts) - same structural driver that floors EP/RTA on payments names (GPN/FIS/FISV). R&D expense line present (~$3.0-3.3B/yr), so I&NE loses the no-R&D point. Aggressive buyback: treasury stock grew $16.1B→$33.1B over the window; equity stays flat as buybacks offset retained-earnings growth. RE compounds strongly (~$18.95B→$32.47B, ~19.6% avg YoY) on steady net income.
