Sentiment: Constructive but a utility, not a wheel gift. 24-analyst consensus Hold, avg PT ~$101 (~4% upside); ~3.25% yield, 25 consecutive annual dividend raises (78-yr payment record). Genuine tailwind: data-center load - Q1'26 data-center demand +42%, 28 contracted large-load projects (~11 GW) with a >75 GW pipeline (Google/Meta/Microsoft), Vogtle 4 units fully online (4,800 MW). 2026 adj-EPS guide $4.50-4.60 (+6%). Offsetting risk: $81B 2026-2030 capex plan funded by rising debt + equity issuance (leverage already ~3x TL/equity), rate-case/regulatory dependence, and rate-sensitivity of the stock. Assignment would hand us a well-run, defensive dividend utility - fine to own, but capital-intensive/levered structure caps DCAP quality.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Southern Company (SO) - regulated electric + gas utility, FY ends Dec 31; last 4 filed FYs are FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026). Balance-sheet items are SEC-exact from the consolidated 10-K R-pages. shareholders_equity = reported TOTAL stockholders' equity INCLUDING noncontrolling interests (34,532/35,225/36,674/38,867); common stockholders' equity ex-NCI is lower (30,408/31,444/33,208/36,016) - NCI is mostly tax-equity/renewable partnerships + residual subsidiary preference. long_term_debt = SEC noncurrent 'Long-Term Debt' line (includes finance-lease obligations); stockanalysis strips leases and shows ~750 lower. ppe = SEC net 'Total property, plant, and equipment' (94,570→114,369); stockanalysis adds nuclear fuel/CWIP (~1.2B higher) - used SEC for internal consistency. inventory = materials+supplies + fossil fuel + natural-gas-for-sale (matches stockanalysis components). No preferred/preference stock line in the consolidated equity section (parenthetical shows only common) → preferred flagged absent. No R&D line (regulated utility). Structural utility floor expected: EP fails all 4 yrs (large regulated current liabilities exceed EBITDA every year: 10.1/11.4/12.9/13.9B EBITDA vs 15.7/13.5/16.0/16.9B curr-liab); RTA floored by low asset turnover (NOCF/assets ~4.7-6.7%, avg ~5.8%); TSA-DSER floored by heavy leverage (TL/(SE+treas) ~2.9-3.0x); goodwill flat at 5,161 all 4 yrs (no accretion) → GW 0. Likely-passing params: I&NE (inventory + EBITDA both rise steadily, no R&D), PPE (steady CapEx rise, no >60% spike), LTD 1/2 (avg LTD/assets ~0.40 but LTD/EBITDA ~4.7 >4), PS (absent), RE 1/3 (~8.8% avg YoY), TS (present), RSH (latest EBITDA/SE ~35.8%). Expect ~8/17 - not a gift; consistent with the REIT/utility structural-floor bench (NEE/DUK/ETR/ATO).

DCAP 8/17 (47%)
gift at ≥60%

Sentiment: Bearish/challenged. FY2026 net sales $9.14B down 10%; beer shipments -3.8% though still gaining US share; Wine & Spirits reshaped by divestitures (net sales -51%, margin 1.3%). Comparable EPS $11.82 down 14%. FY2027 guide soft: organic net sales -1% to +1%. Most recent analyst rating Sell with $150 PT; TipRanks AI Neutral. Stock near 52-wk low (~$132 vs $178 high). Tariff/aluminum and Hispanic-consumer-demand overhang on the beer franchise. Not currently an assignment we'd celebrate given the earnings-power volatility.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Constellation Brands (beer/wine/spirits); fiscal year ends end of February, so FY2026 = year ended Feb 28 2026 (10-K filed ~Apr 2026). Used latest 4 FYs. EBITDA taken per DCAP spec primary definition = operating income + D&A. FY2025 EBITDA collapses to $800.6M because a ~$2.85B wine & spirits goodwill impairment sits inside operating income (op income $354.9M + D&A $445.7M); goodwill line confirms it (7,980 -> 5,126.8). This is the same impairment-inclusive treatment used for SJM/KHC/GIS. Macrotrends reports a normalized FY2025 EBITDA of ~$3.6B (it adds the non-cash impairment back). HUMAN CALL: which EBITDA convention swings EP and I&NE. FY2025 was a genuine GAAP-loss year (prior-year loss per FY26 release). FY2026 shows a rebound (op income back to $2.72B) plus the wine & spirits divestiture (Wine & Spirits net sales -51%). Retained earnings dipped FY2025 (net loss + dividends) then recovered FY2026. Carries R&D-free income statement, no preferred, heavy treasury stock (buyback history, $8.1B). Balance-sheet series cross-checked against the FY2026 earnings-release balance sheet (equity/goodwill/inventory/assets/LTD/total-liab/NOCF all match to the decimal).

gift at ≥60%

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 16.9% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)

Sentiment: Bullish. Analyst consensus Buy/Strong-Buy (0 sells; PTs raised to $525 BofA, $550 Cantor) on Teradyne's position in the ATE test duopoly (vs Advantest) levered to the AI cycle: AI/HPC now ~70% of semi-test revenue, HBM and complex SoC test demand strong into 2026, record Q1-2026 orders. Risks that dent celebratability: customer concentration in a few AI ASIC/GPU clients, U.S.-China trade exposure, and high multiple - stock fell ~19% on cautious Q2 guidance, and it is a cyclical (FY23 was a test-equipment trough). A high-quality dominant franchise we'd be content to own, but the financial rubric is dragged down by structural buyback-driven RE volatility and short-cyclicality.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Teradyne (semiconductor ATE test + robotics), FY = calendar year (Dec 31). All balance-sheet lines are SEC-exact from the FY2025 10-K (FY24/FY25) and FY2023 10-K (FY22/FY23). PP&E is net PP&E EXCLUDING operating-lease ROU assets (ROU shown separately: $73.7M/$73.4M/$70.2M/$76.6M FY22-25); stockanalysis/aggregators fold ROU into PP&E - I used the SEC ex-ROU figure for a clean capex series. EBITDA = income from operations + full D&A (depreciation + intangible amortization); SEC income-from-operations confirmed exactly (831.9/501.1/593.8/650.1) and cash-flow depreciation confirmed. Long-term debt = 0 all four years: Teradyne retired its convertible notes and is essentially debt-free; the $200M that appears in FY2025 is SHORT-TERM debt drawn under a revolver (sits in current liabilities), which is what spikes FY2025 current liabilities to $1,115M. Treasury stock ABSENT: shares issued = shares outstanding (156.088M FY25, 161.722M FY24) - Teradyne retires repurchased shares rather than holding treasury, so buybacks are charged against retained earnings (this is why RE is non-monotonic: 725.7 -> 706.5 -> 970.8 -> 744.4). No preferred stock. R&D is a distinct income-statement line (~$441M/$418M/$461M/$505M FY22-25). Likely score profile: EP fails (FY23 EBITDA 612 < CL 660; FY25 EBITDA 778 < CL 1,115 on the revolver draw); I&NE fails (inventory non-monotonic + R&D present); RTA ~16.9% avg NOCF/assets just under the 17% second point; RE 0/3 (buyback-driven volatility); GW dips FY24 (395 vs 416) so 'increasing' likely fails; TS 0 (retires shares). Offsets: near-debt-free (LTD 2/2), low leverage (TSA-DSER ~0.40 avg, 2/2), RSH ~28%.

Sentiment: Mixed-to-neutral analyst tone: consensus Hold, avg PT ~$67. Goldman upgraded to Buy ($73 PT) on underwriting margins/ROE; Argus downgraded to Hold on P&C competitive pressure; Wolfe Research downgraded on casualty concerns and premium P/E vs peers. Q1-2026: EPS $1.31 (vs $1.05), net income +23%. Shareholder-friendly: special $0.50 dividend + regular dividend raise paid 2026-07-02. Mitsui Sumitomo took a ~15% strategic stake in 2025 (validation of franchise quality). Next earnings ~2026-07-20 - inside the 30-45 DTE window for any chain sweep. Assignment would be owning a disciplined specialty underwriter with 4 straight years of RE growth - celebratable as a business, but the DCAP rubric structurally penalizes insurance float.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Commercial-lines P&C insurer (specialty/excess & surplus), FY ends Dec 31. No inventory - insurance carrier, genuinely carries none (0s). Current liabilities null x4: insurers file unclassified balance sheets (no current/noncurrent split in the 10-K) - same structural gap as PGR. EBITDA is nonstandard for insurers; series = pretax income + interest expense + net D&A/accretion (reconstructed exactly from SEC XBRL components: e.g. FY2025 2,280.5 + 126.9 − 48.1 = 2,359), matching stockanalysis.com's published EBITDA. Note WRB's cash-flow D&A line is net of investment accretion and goes negative in FY2023-FY2025. Shareholders' equity is SEC parent-only common equity (stockanalysis incl. noncontrolling interests runs ~$11-19M higher - immaterial). LTD = senior notes (~$1,830M) + subordinated debentures ($1,010M per SEC XBRL), flat ~$2.84B all 4 years. Preferred: authorized but 0 issued/outstanding all years per SEC XBRL. Treasury stock present and growing ($3,251M → $4,339M) - active buyback history, held not retired. Goodwill essentially flat (185.5 → 174.6 → 184.3 → 184.3) - dipped FY2023, so not 'increasing'. Expect insurer-structural fails on EP (no CL), RTA (float-inflated asset base), TSA-DSER (reserves count as liabilities) per the PGR/CME precedent.

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 20.7% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.08 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.0 years of EBITDA (cap 4)

Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 32.1% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Celebratable-if-assigned profile: consensus Buy (26-33 analysts), mean PT ~$150 vs ~$148 price; DA Davidson raised to $162 on 2026-07-01. Stock -3% YTD vs positive S&P (mild underperformance, no distress). FY2025 revenue +10% to $12.2B, adj EBITDA margin ~35%, NOCF $4.6B. Near-term tailwind: FIFA World Cup hosted US/Canada/Mexico Jun 11-Jul 19 2026 inside current option windows. Watch: $2B convertible maturity Mar 2026 already funded by $11B+ cash/investments.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Marketplace platform - genuinely carries no inventory (no InventoryNet tag in any ABNB filing). EBITDA = operating income + D&A from 10-K cash-flow/Adj-EBITDA reconciliations (op income 1802/1518/2553/2544; D&A 81/44/65/91 - FY2025 10-K no longer breaks D&A out on the cash-flow statement, folded into Other-net; D&A cited from Adj-EBITDA reconciliation tables). LTD FY2025 = 0 is real: the $2.0B convertible notes mature Mar 2026 and were reclassified to current liabilities, which also inflates FY2025 current_liabilities (13,649). Retained earnings is an accumulated deficit that DEEPENS despite positive net income because ABNB retires repurchased shares against accumulated deficit (~$3.5B/yr buybacks) - buyback-retirement pattern, same operator-policy question as MSI/SHW/ORLY. FY2023 net income ($4.8B) included a one-time deferred-tax valuation-allowance release; EBITDA series is unaffected. R&D flag: income statement carries Product development (1502/1722/2056/2354). Preferred: authorized, zero issued/outstanding. Current liabilities structurally huge: includes funds payable / amounts payable to customers (guest prepayments held before check-in) - EP comparison fails on float mechanics much like insurers, though this float is also why NOCF/RTA is stellar.

Sentiment: Buy consensus (21 analysts, avg PT ~$448 vs ~$389 spot; recent raises to $550 Cantor, $498 Stifel). Stock +62.6% y/y; Q2 FY2026 beat with above-consensus Q3 guide on industrial recovery + AI infrastructure and aerospace/defense backlog. Risks: P/E >56x leaves multiple-contraction risk; $1.5B all-cash Empower Semiconductor acquisition adds integration risk. Nothing un-celebratable about assignment; valuation, not quality, is the caveat.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Sat nearest Oct 31 (FY2022 2022-10-29, FY2023 2023-10-28, FY2024 53wk 2024-11-02, FY2025 2025-11-01). EBITDA = operating income (EDGAR XBRL 10-K) + cash-flow-statement D&A (stockanalysis: 2298/2293/2104/1999); matches stockanalysis' cited EBITDA within $1M each year. total_liabilities per stockanalysis, verified = Assets - StockholdersEquity from EDGAR exactly. No treasury-stock line: ADI retires repurchased shares (EDGAR TreasuryStockValue/TreasuryStockCommonValue both absent) despite a heavy buyback program - MSI-style operator-call caveat on the TS flag. Preferred: 471,934 shares authorized, none outstanding -> absent. R&D expense line present ($1.77B FY2025). FY2024 was the analog inventory-correction trough (revenue -23% y/y to $9.4B), which breaks the EBITDA rise, RTA, RE-growth and RSH series; FY2025 recovered to $11.0B.

Sentiment: Overhang largely resolved: SEC settled Jan-2026 for $40M (former execs inflated Nutrition segment via intersegment pricing; no consolidated impact), DOJ closed with no further action. Stock $77.93, upper end of 52wk 52.23-85.37, recovering on finalized RFS biofuel mandates; FY2026 guide $3.60-4.25 adj EPS, dividend raised (50+yr dividend-growth record). Fundamentals cyclically depressed: EBITDA fell from $5.5B (FY2022 ag-supercycle peak) to $2.3B (FY2025) as crush/origination margins normalized - the DCAP window catches the full downswing. Commodity processor: thin margins, huge working capital, earnings hostage to crop cycles and biofuel policy - moat is scale/logistics, not pricing power. Assignment at a discount would be tolerable (solvent, investment-grade, dividend aristocrat) but this is a cyclical commodity business, not a Buffett-style DCA compounder.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Calendar FY (Dec-31). All balance-sheet/cash-flow series from SEC EDGAR XBRL 10-K facts (CIK 0000007084), cross-checked vs stockanalysis.com. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (stockanalysis-cited; D&A verified vs EDGAR cash-flow tag). Broader pretax+interest+D&A runs ~$0.7B/yr higher (Wilmar equity earnings); no DCAP point flips on the definition choice. Total liabilities = Total Current + Total Long-Term Liabilities lines from the 10-K balance sheet; temporary equity (redeemable NCI: 299/320/253/287) sits in mezzanine and is excluded from both TL and SE. SE = balance-sheet 'Total Shareholders' Equity' incl. small NCI ($7-33M); positive all 4 years, TSA-DSER applicable. Treasury stock: 235.5M shares held at 12/31/2025, recorded as reduction of equity; dollar balance disclosed 2022-2024 in prior 10-K XBRL (1,700/2,700/2,300) but NOT disclosed for FY2025 -> null, never estimated. PPE is EDGAR net PP&E (excludes right-of-use assets; stockanalysis folds ROU in). No R&D line on the consolidated income statement (ag processor; R&D spend disclosed only in footnotes) -> flag absent. Preferred: 500,000 shares authorized, none issued (10-K Note 16). FY2023 10-K was restated (intersegment sales, Nutrition); SEC settlement confirms adjustments did NOT affect consolidated earnings/balance sheet/cash flows, so these series are unaffected. Candidate raw note flagged the restatement for human context - carried here.

Sentiment: Neutral-to-defensive. As of early July 2026 consensus is Hold (price ~$182, targets $184-187; Mizuho/Truist trimmed targets in May 2026). Fundamentals are steady and predictable: FY2026 EPS guidance raised to $8.40-8.50, reaffirmed 6-8% annual EPS & dividend growth through FY2027, ~$4.2B FY2026 capex on safety/reliability driving rate-base growth. No franchise or governance red flags - a boring, well-run monopoly utility. The DCAP score will read low, but that reflects the regulated-utility capital structure (heavy debt + equity issuance to fund rate base), not any deterioration; assignment would not be embarrassing, just capital-inefficient versus the wheel's premium math. Nothing that makes ownership un-celebratable.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Atmos Energy - regulated natural-gas distribution + pipeline utility; fiscal year ends Sept 30 (FY2025 10-K filed Nov 2025). EBITDA = operating income + D&A, taken directly from the 10-K comprehensive-income statements (FY22 920.982+535.655=1456.6; FY23 1067.147+604.327=1671.5; FY24 1355.362+669.972=2025.3; FY25 1559.971+734.745=2294.7); note WSJ/Yahoo report a different 'operating income' that is ~$200M higher and mislabeled - the SEC filing is authoritative. Inventory = 'gas stored underground' (utility working gas). LTD includes securitized long-term debt (FY23 +85.1, FY24 +76.9, FY25 +68.2; FY22 none). Total liabilities derived as total assets - shareholders' equity (all four tie out exactly). No preferred stock and no treasury stock line in the equity section across all filings; Atmos funds growth via steady equity issuance (share count 145M->159M), not buybacks - so TS parameter is a structural 0. FY2022 current liabilities spiked to $3,603M because of $2,201M current maturities of long-term debt that year, so EP (EBITDA > current liabilities) fails FY2022 - structural, not operational. NOCF FY2021 was actually -$1,084M and FY2023 spiked to $3,460M due to Winter Storm Uri (Feb 2021) gas-cost outflow then securitized-recovery inflow; this whipsaw drags the 4-yr avg NOCF/assets (~8.5%) below the RTA 11% tier despite a healthy business. Expect a low DCAP: heavy rate-base leverage caps TSA-DSER and RSH, capital intensity caps RTA, flat goodwill and no buybacks each cost a point - a classic regulated-utility ceiling. Retained-earnings series cross-checked against the SEC 10-K exactly (3211.2/3666.7/4216.6/4861.6); EBITDA/op-income cross-checked stockanalysis vs the 10-K line items.

Sentiment: Constructive/healthy. Analysts 7 buy / 1 hold, no sells; CBRE raised FY2026 core EPS guidance to $7.60-7.80. U.S. CRE fundamentals resilient into Q1 2026 (industrial leasing +14%, office rents +2.2% y/y), 2026 volume forecast upgraded. Overhang: some concern about AI's impact on brokerage. Q2 2026 earnings scheduled 2026-07-29 (lands in typical 30-45 DTE chain windows -- flag for options timing). Business quality is not the issue; any not-a-gift here is structural DCAP mechanics, not sentiment.
research notes - methodology & caveats
CBRE Group (commercial real estate services), FY ends Dec 31; window FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026). INVENTORY: services/brokerage business, no merchandise inventory line on the balance sheet -> series is 0s (genuine, not missing). PPE: used SEC XBRL PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (core property & equipment, 836/907/914/1049) NOT aggregator PP&E (2041-3757) which inflates with operating-lease ROU assets [[bj-ppe-rou-trap]]. SHAREHOLDERS_EQUITY: SEC XBRL StockholdersEquity = parent-only equity (7853/8267/8411/8878); aggregators report total equity incl. noncontrolling interests (8606-9626) -- used parent. EBITDA = SEC operating income (1512/1117/1413/1753) + cash-flow D&A (613/790/869/928); cross-checks stockanalysis EBITDA line exactly. TREASURY: no treasury-stock line in XBRL and retained earnings (9916) exceeds total equity (8878) -> CBRE constructively retires repurchased shares despite a heavy buyback program (TS flag 'absent' is a retirement artifact, like MSI/SHW [[msi-near-miss-retired-buybacks]] [[shw-retirement-flips-verdict]]). No preferred, no R&D line (services firm). Expect a structural not-a-gift: asset-light services with large current liabilities vs modest EBITDA fail EP/RTA/TSA-DSER (float-like drag), RE growth only ~4%/yr (capital returned via buybacks), TS 0 on retirement. Non-REIT real-estate exception on stage-1 [[comms-media-re-sweep]].

Sentiment: Constructive but not euphoric. Q1 2026 net sales $5.68B (+9.2% YoY) beat; management raised FY2026 gross-profit growth guide to low-to-mid single digits on strong order/backlog activity. Consensus clusters Buy/Hold, ~$210 12-mo target (~+17%). Tailwinds: AI deals described as margin-accretive (services attach + recurring), 'Geared for Growth' program targeting $100-200M run-rate savings by 2027-28. Macro caveat: management guides U.S. IT market to only low-single-digit growth for 2026, aiming for 200-300bps outperformance - a cyclical, spend-dependent VAR, not a moat compounder. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable, but DCAP quality is capped by the distributor balance-sheet structure.
research notes - methodology & caveats
CDW Corp - IT products/solutions reseller & integrator (VAR/distributor), FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (D&A from cash-flow statement): FY22 1735.2+290.6; FY23 1680.9+270.7; FY24 1651.3+275.3; FY25 1655.6+295.6; cross-checked vs stockanalysis.com EBITDA (2026/1952/1927/1951). PP&E uses XBRL PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (188.8/195.5/192.0/171.5) - stockanalysis inflates it to ~338/324/312/308 by folding in lease ROU assets; used the true net PP&E line. Total liabilities via balance identity (assets − equity), matches stockanalysis. Retained earnings is a large accumulated DEFICIT (LBO legacy: Madison Dearborn/Providence era) that is SHRINKING (-1763.8→-1273.9) while SE stays positive and rising - verified against SEC EDGAR XBRL. Treasury stock: CDW RETIRES repurchased shares (no TreasuryStockCommonValue line since 2018) → treasury_stock_abs=0, flag absent; expect TS param 0/1. No R&D expense line (ResearchAndDevelopmentExpense tag empty in XBRL) → I&NE no-R&D point should credit. Preferred stock $0 all years. Structural distributor drag expected on EP/RTA/TSA-DSER: huge accounts-payable-driven current liabilities (4.9B→7.2B) and total liabilities dwarf EBITDA & equity (TL/SE ~5-7x), so EP (EBITDA>CL) fails all 4 yrs and TSA-DSER 0/2 - same working-capital pattern that caps distributors. Inventory DECLINING (destock), not a steady rise. Goodwill rising (bolt-on M&A). RSH strong: latest EBITDA/SE ~75%.

CME - CME Group not-a-gift

Finance / Exchanges & Clearing · price $234.76 · mcap $85.1B · vol 4,330,305 · PE 20.2 · PB 3.2 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 7/17 (41%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 2.3% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.02 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 0.8 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 4.86 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)2/3retained earnings grew 13.8%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 15.9% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Positive-to-mixed. Record June 2026 ADV of 30.6M contracts; launching single-stock futures July 27 and 24/7 crude/gold micro contracts - growth initiatives intact. Orderly succession announced: CEO Terry Duffy to Executive Chairman March 2027, President/CFO Lynne Fitzpatrick named CEO. Analyst consensus neutral-to-buy (6 buy/6 hold/3 sell), ~$300 avg target vs ~$273 price; Rothschild upgraded to Buy ($323 PT), TD Cowen trimmed PT to $273 but kept Buy. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable - dominant, moaty exchange franchise.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Derivatives exchange (calendar FY, Dec 31). No inventory - services business, none on balance sheet. EBITDA = operating income + total D&A from cash-flow statement (income-statement D&A ~$135M plus purchased-intangibles amortization; totals 362.6/352.6/336.8/330.9). STRUCTURAL CAVEAT: total assets, current liabilities, and total liabilities are dominated by clearing-house performance bonds & guaranty fund contributions (~$100-160B of member collateral held as matched asset/liability) - EP, RTA, and TSA-DSER parameters are mechanically penalized the same way insurer float penalizes PGR; the operating business itself carries only ~$3.4B LTD vs $4.6B EBITDA (LTD/EBITDA 0.75). PPE is small and gently declining (asset-light). Retained earnings dip 2022-2024 reflects CME's large annual variable dividend policy, not weak earnings; FY2025 RE jumped 54%. Preferred stock value $0 all years per EDGAR; no treasury stock on balance sheet since 2010 and no repurchases tagged since 2011. Q2 2026 earnings July 22 - inside a typical 30-45 DTE window for any near-term play.

Industrials - Engines/Power Systems · price $678.24 · mcap $93.6B · vol 1,196,233 · PE 34.4 · PB 7.4 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 7/17 (41%)
gift at ≥60%

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 8.6% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.16 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.3 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.04 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 8.1%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 41.5% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Bullish/constructive as of Jul 2026. Analyst consensus Buy (13 analysts; ~46% Strong Buy, 38% Buy, 15% Hold; avg PT ~$701). UBS upgraded to Buy (PT $850) on improving truck market; Barclays to Overweight (PT $546) citing EPA 2027 low-NOx rules spurring a heavy-duty pre-buy engine cycle in H2 2026. Additional tailwind: data-center backup-power demand for Power Systems gensets. FY2025 was a NA truck downcycle year yet Cummins posted record adjusted EBITDA/EPS; raised FY2026 guidance to +8-11% revenue and 17.75-18.50% EBITDA margin. New HELM platform engines (X10, B7.2) align with EPA 2027 and raise content per truck. Overhang: cyclical NA truck exposure and tariff volatility. Assignment of a dominant, well-capitalized engine franchise at a discount would be celebratable; the DCAP miss is structural (heavy-industrial working-capital + goodwill decline), not a quality flag.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Cummins Inc., diesel/natural-gas engines, power systems, components, Accelera (zero-emission), and distribution. FY ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed 2026-02-10, so window = FY2022-FY2025. UNITS: USD millions throughout. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (3713/2785/4815/5130). Deliberately NOT the headline non-GAAP EBITDA (co. reports ~$6.3B FY24 / ~$5.4B FY25) which is inflated by the one-time Atmus Filtration separation gain (~$1.3B in FY2024) and other below-operating-line items; the OI+D&A series is the comparable measure and correctly reflects the FY2023 $2.036B emissions-settlement charge that depressed FY2023 operating income to $1,761M (hence the EBITDA dip that breaks I&NE steady-rise). SHAREHOLDERS_EQUITY is PARENT-ONLY (Cummins Inc. shareholders' equity: 8975/8850/10271/12349), excluding noncontrolling interests (~$1.0-1.1B/yr) - aggregators like stockanalysis.com report total-equity-with-NCI (9967/9904/11308/13408); parent-only is the DCAP-consistent figure. Structural drags expected: EP fails 4/4 (current liabilities $9.6-12.9B far exceed EBITDA ~$3-5B - ordinary heavy-industrial working capital, NOT captive-finance float; Cummins finances via equity-method JVs, so this is a milder version of the PCAR/CAT captive-finance ceiling), RTA avg NOCF/assets ~8.6% (<11%), TSA-DSER avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~1.04 (just over 1.0 tier). RE avg YoY ~8% (FY2023 dip on emissions charge drags it) clears only the 7% tier. GW declines after FY2023 (2343/2499/2370/2224) on Atmus deconsolidation + FX. Positives: LTD very low (LTD/assets ~0.15, LTD/EBITDA 1.3), no preferred, large treasury ($10.7B buyback history), RSH latest EBITDA/SE ~42%, PP&E steady rise. Inventory rises steadily but EBITDA doesn't, so I&NE rise point not earned; R&D line present ($1.4B) so no R&D point. Meritor acquisition closed Aug 2022 (largely inside window at the FY2022 anchor). Cross-checks: retained earnings & treasury & total liabilities & LTD agree between stockanalysis.com and stock-analysis-on.net; EBITDA reconciled against company-reported non-GAAP (difference explained by one-time items).

Sentiment: MAJOR non-celebratable overhang: EA agreed Sept 2025 to be taken private for $55B ($210/share cash) by Saudi PIF, Silver Lake, and Affinity Partners - the largest LBO ever. Shareholders approved Dec 22, 2025; HSR cleared, CFIUS is the last material approval outstanding; outside date extended to Sept 28, 2026 with the merger bond tender settling ~Jul 20, 2026, so a close in H2 2026 is expected. The stock is a merger-arb name pinned near $210 with defined upside and deal-break downside - selling a cash-secured put here is NOT owning a durable business we'd be thrilled to hold; assignment would just leave us holding shares that get cashed out at the deal price. Unwheelable regardless of the financial score. Business fundamentals are fine (steady ~$7.5B revenue, ~$1.9B EBITDA, debt-light, live-services/FC franchise), but the pending buyout dominates any thesis.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends March 31 (FY2025 = year ended 2025-03-31). Software/games publisher: carries NO inventory (0s used deliberately) and NO treasury-stock line - EA retires repurchased shares, so APIC absorbs buybacks and there is no us-gaap:TreasuryStockValue concept in its filings (treasury_stock=absent, abs=0). EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A: OI 1129/1332/1518/1520 (SEC OperatingIncomeLoss, confirmed) + D&A 486/536/404/356 (SEC DepreciationAmortizationAndAccretionNet) = 1615/1868/1922/1876 (matches stockanalysis EBITDA). R&D expense is a large explicit income-statement line ($2.19B/$2.33B/$2.42B/$2.57B), so I&NE R&D half fails. FY2025 LTD drop 1882->1484 = ~$400M senior notes reclassified to current (2026 maturity), not deleveraging. Retained earnings 7607/7357/7582/6470 verified against SEC RetainedEarningsAccumulatedDeficit (exact match); FY2025 RE fell on buybacks exceeding net income, so RE growth is negative on average. Goodwill essentially flat/slightly declining (5387->5376). Preferred authorized but none outstanding.

FIS - Fidelity National Information Services Inc not-a-gift

Finance - bank technology · price $41.25 · mcap $21.3B · vol 6,361,870 · PE 8.1 · PB 1.4 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 7/17 (41%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 5.1% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.25 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 2.5 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.05 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -17.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 26.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Mid-transformation fintech. Apr-2025 FIS announced selling its remaining 45% Worldpay stake to Global Payments while acquiring Global Payments' Issuer Solutions business ($24.25B Worldpay EV / $13.5B Issuer EV, ~$7.7B net cash from FIS); closed H1-2026. Q1-2026 net earnings surged to $2.37B (EPS $4.58) from $77M a year earlier, almost entirely a ~$2.2B pretax gain on the Worldpay sale - not operating strength. Analyst tone mixed-to-cautious: deal is billed adjusted-EPS accretive with >=$600M expense + >=$200M revenue synergies, but adds integration risk and leverage, and headline growth is inorganic (Issuer Solutions consolidation lifts 2026 revenue guide to ~$13.8B from ~$10.7B). Core organic growth remains low-single-digit. Assignment would not be an obvious 'gift': solid but not durable-moat by this scorer - deep legacy accumulated deficit, settlement-float-heavy balance sheet, and a franchise still being reshaped.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fidelity National Information Services (fintech: banking + capital-markets tech; payments/Worldpay now exited). FY ends Dec 31. No inventory (software/services) - inventory set to 0. R&D absent as a separate income-statement line (embedded in cost of revenue) -> flag 'absent'. No preferred outstanding. EBITDA built as operating income + cash-flow D&A: FY22 1176+1878, FY23 1447+1744, FY24 1709+1737, FY25 1741+1883 (matches stockanalysis EBITDA; excludes the ~$17.6B FY2022 Worldpay goodwill impairment, which EBITDA adds back). KEY WINDOW ARTIFACT: Worldpay deconsolidation. FIS sold 55% of Worldpay to GTCR (announced Jul-2023, held-for-sale at YE2023, closed Jan-2024). Result: total assets collapse 63.3B->55B->33.8B->33.5B and current liabilities fall 16-18B->6-8B as merchant-settlement/deposit float leaves the balance sheet. Current-liabilities are structurally huge vs EBITDA (settlement float) in every year -> EP fails 4x, like a payment-processor. Retained earnings are a deep accumulated deficit throughout (RE was +2889 at YE2021, crushed to -14971 by the FY2022 ~$17.6B Worldpay goodwill impairment, then dividends deepen it) -> RE growth 0. GOODWILL BASIS: used continuing-operations goodwill (16.8->17.8B, rising) rather than FY2022 as-originally-reported 34276 (which still consolidated Worldpay's ~$17.5B goodwill); the 34276->17k drop is a reclassification, not franchise erosion, so continuing-ops is the honest series. PP&E also on continuing-ops basis (FY2022 709 restated vs 862 as-originally-reported incl Worldpay). SE = SEC parent StockholdersEquity, positive all 4 years (27218/19093/15698/13899) so TSA-DSER is NOT excused. Treasury stock large and rising (buybacks). Rough hand-calc: I&NE 1 (R&D absent; no inventory rise), EP 0 (curr-liab float), PPE 0 (691<709), RTA 0 (NOCF/assets ~2.6-7.8%), LTD 2 (LTD/assets ~0.25, LTD/EBITDA 2.5), TSA-DSER 0 (avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~1.05, fails >1.0 tier), PS 1, RE 0, TS 1, RSH 1 (EBITDA/SE 26%), GW 1 -> ~7/17 not-a-gift; server is authoritative. TRANSFORMATION FLAG: the FY2022-FY2025 window is unrepresentative of go-forward FIS - in H1-2026 FIS sold its remaining 45% Worldpay stake to Global Payments AND bought Global Payments' Issuer Solutions business (~$7.7B cash out; 2026 revenue guide jumps to ~$13.8B). Rescore after the FY2026 10-K (first clean year: ex-Worldpay, incl Issuer Solutions).

Sentiment: Tone turned bullish in July 2026 despite ugly FY2025 GAAP results: O'Reilly Automotive submitted a $10B+ cash bid for the NAPA auto-parts unit and management is weighing sale vs spinoff with a decision targeted by end of summer (stock +~7-12% on the news); DA Davidson initiated Buy, PT raised to $150 vs ~$134 Street average, calling GPC materially undervalued on the motion spin, NAPA cost cuts, and industrial-cycle leverage. FY2025 loss driven by one-time non-cash pension termination ($742M) and First Brands bankruptcy ($150M), not core erosion. CAUTION for assignment: pending breakup is a major corporate action inside any wheel window - post-split GPC would be a different business and this DCAP would need rescoring; Q2 earnings July 21, 2026.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Genuine Parts Co (NAPA auto parts + Motion industrial distribution), FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (stockanalysis line, validated against EDGAR XBRL components: rev − COGS − SG&A − D&A − restructuring matches within the ~$20-37M doubtful-accounts provision). PPE is the 10-K's net PP&E line from EDGAR XBRL (stockanalysis shows ~2x higher because it folds in operating-lease right-of-use assets). Shareholders_equity is parent-only (EDGAR StockholdersEquity); total_liabilities = assets − total equity incl. ~$15M noncontrolling interests (consistent EDGAR/stockanalysis). No treasury-stock line - GPC retires repurchased shares (buybacks exist but the balance-sheet line is absent). Preferred authorized, none issued (PreferredStockValue = 0 all 4 yrs). No R&D line (distributor; not tagged in XBRL). FY2025 EBITDA/NOCF/retained-earnings declines reflect ~$1.1B pretax one-timers: $742M non-cash pension-termination settlement (below operating line; hit RE) + $150M First Brands bankruptcy charge + $254M restructuring; adjusted FY2025 net income $216M Q4-adj basis per company release.

Sentiment: Dividend King near a 52-week low: ~$24.79 vs $31.86 high, ~4.8% yield, mcap ~$13.6B. FY2025 was a genuine down year - operating income -33% (1068->718.6M), net earnings 805->478M - on commodity cost inflation plus Planters and turkey-business drags. Analyst consensus Hold (6 analysts: ~34% buy/strong-buy, 67% hold, 0% sell) as of Jun 2026. Management reaffirmed FY2026 guidance (sales $12.2-12.5B, adj EPS $1.43-1.51, +4-10% y/y) and posted a 6th consecutive quarter of organic sales growth with Foodservice/International strength; H1 FY2026 still pressured, H2 recovery expected as pork costs ease. Assignment would hand us a discounted dividend king mid-turnaround - celebratable if the recovery holds, but the FY2025 fundamental deterioration is real and staples working-capital/goodwill-heavy structure caps DCAP quality.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Hormel Foods, consumer staples (branded meat/food) - carries substantial inventory. FY ends late October (FY2022=Oct 30 2022 ... FY2025=Oct 26 2025). EBITDA built as operating income + D&A (from cash-flow stmt): FY22 1313+235; FY23 1072+253; FY24 1068+258; FY25 718.6+263.9=982.5. NO treasury stock line: FY2025 equity section reconciles exactly without one (common stock 8.059 + APIC 620.069 + RE 7516.69 + AOCL -243.646 = 7901.17 Hormel shareholders' investment; total incl NCI 7915.815) - Hormel retires repurchased shares rather than holding treasury, so treasury_stock_abs=0 and flag 'absent'. Preferred stock authorized (160M sh, $0.01 par) but ZERO issued -> absent. No separate R&D expense line on the income statement (bundled in SG&A) -> rnd 'absent'. FY2023 current liabilities spiked to 2312 because ~900M note was reclassified to current maturities that year (LTD dropped 3291->2359 same year); as-reported. FY2025 EBITDA/operating-income collapse driven by commodity (pork/turkey) cost inflation, Planters snack-nut recovery and whole-bird turkey business drag - this depresses EP/RTA/RSH and the RE series ticks down in FY2025.

Sentiment: Negative near-term. Feb 2026: Humana guided 2026 adjusted EPS to "at least $9.00" vs ~$12 consensus on a ~$3.5B net Medicare Advantage star-ratings headwind; shares plunged. Only ~20% of MA members in 4+ star plans for 2026 (down from prior years). Company expects >25% MA membership growth (could become largest MA carrier) but analysts (Morningstar) warn the growth further compresses margins. FY25 operating cash flow collapsed to $921M (from $2.97B FY24). Management points to 2027 star recovery, but near-term earnings/margin visibility is poor - not a business assignment we'd currently celebrate.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Humana Inc. - Medicare-Advantage-heavy managed-care insurer; FY ends Dec 31. No inventory (services business) -> inventory series all 0. EBITDA built from SEC XBRL: operating income (OperatingIncomeLoss) + D&A (Depreciation + AmortizationOfIntangibleAssets), not an aggregator EBITDA row (per insurer-EBITDA discipline). shareholders_equity is parent-only StockholdersEquity from SEC (15311/16262/16375/17657); stockanalysis folds in ~$60-80M NCI (15370/16318/16445/17737) - used parent figure. All SE positive -> TSA-DSER applies (not excused). Preferred: 10M shares authorized, none issued -> absent. Structural insurer-float profile expected to floor EP (EBITDA << current liabilities, which hold benefits-payable claims reserves 4x), RTA (NOCF/assets avg ~6.9%, dragged by FY25 $921M OCF collapse), RSH (latest EBITDA/SE 20% <23%). PPE declining -> PPE 0. RE avg YoY ~4.5% (<7%) -> RE 0. Bright spots: LTD/assets ~0.23 + LTD/EBITDA 3.5 (LTD 2/2), goodwill rising (GW 1/1), TS present, no preferred, TSA-DSER borderline avg ~0.996. Cross-checks CI/CME insurer-float pattern.

Sentiment: Broadly positive. ~25 analysts: 19 Strong Buy / 2 Moderate Buy / 4 Hold; mean target ~$87 (~3% upside). Company raised 2026 guidance to 4-5% organic revenue and 8-9% comparable EPS growth on broad-based volume strength (Coca-Cola Zero Sugar +13% volume). Defensive megacap staple; assignment would be an ownable blue-chip, though the DCAP staples floor (EP/RTA/TSA-DSER structural) and RE growth ~4%/yr keep it below the gift bar.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Calendar fiscal year (Dec 31). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (SEC OperatingIncomeLoss + cash-flow D&A): FY22 10909+1260, FY23 11311+1128, FY24 9992+1075, FY25 13762+1050. shareholders_equity = parent line "Equity Attributable to Shareowners of The Coca-Cola Company" per SEC us-gaap:StockholdersEquity (24105/25941/24856/32169), EXCLUDING noncontrolling interests (~1.7-2.1B); total-equity aggregator rows fold NCI in. total_liabilities reconciled as total assets minus total equity. FY24 operating income (9992) and OCF (6805) depressed by a ~$6B IRS tax-litigation deposit (cash) and a large fairlife contingent-consideration fair-value charge; FY25 rebounded (OI 13762, OCF 7408). Goodwill DECLINED FY24->FY25 (18139->15491). Classic consumer-staples profile: current liabilities exceed EBITDA every year (EP fails) and NOCF/assets averages ~9.4% (RTA soft) - the staples working-capital floor. No R&D line; no preferred outstanding; large treasury stock from decades of buybacks.

Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)

Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 138.7% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Constructive/neutral-to-positive. FY2026 (ended Apr 2026) was a record year: revenue $6.93B (+5.4%), net income $1.28B, all-flash revenue $4.2B (+11%), ~1,100 AI/data-prep wins vs ~400 prior year; stock jumped ~25% on results and an upgraded FY2027 outlook ($7.33-7.58B rev). Consensus ~Buy/Hold (median PT ~$119, range $88-137; 9 Buy/11 Hold/1 Sell). Some caution: JPMorgan downgraded to Neutral (Apr 2026, $110 PT) citing slowing All-Flash share gains and uncertain AI product timelines, while Morgan Stanley/Barclays/Goldman raised targets on AI-storage positioning. No accounting or governance red flags; assignment would be a solid enterprise-infrastructure business, though quality-wise NetApp's thin equity, deferred-revenue current liabilities, and share-retirement mechanics are structural DCAP drags.
research notes - methodology & caveats
NetApp (hybrid-cloud data storage; hardware + software). Fiscal year ends last Friday of April; latest is FY2026 (ended 2026-04-24, 10-K filed). Uses last 4 FYs FY2023-FY2026. EBITDA = operating income + total D&A (depreciation+intangible amortization) from cash-flow statement: FY23 1018+249, FY24 1214+255, FY25 1337+243, FY26 1674+200 (matches stockanalysis EBITDA line). Carries real inventory (finished storage systems) so not a services-zero. TREASURY STOCK ABSENT: NetApp retires all repurchased shares (no treasury line since FY2014), which also suppresses RETAINED EARNINGS - buyback overage is charged against RE, driving it 265->208->0->153 (FY25 hit exactly $0). This makes the RE series volatile/artifactual rather than an operating signal, and zeroes the TS point. EP is structurally weak: EBITDA sits far below current liabilities every year because current liabilities are dominated by deferred revenue (support/subscription), not operating debt. Thin buyback-driven equity (SE ~$1.0-1.4B vs ~$9.4-9.8B total liabilities) kills TSA-DSER but makes RSH (EBITDA/SE) trivially large. No preferred. R&D expense line present (~$1.0B/yr).

Sentiment: Constructive-but-cooling. O is 'The Monthly Dividend Company' - 671 consecutive monthly dividends, 135th increase, Dividend Aristocrat, ~5.4% yield. Q1-2026 strong: 99% occupancy, 103% re-leasing recapture, $2.8B invested at ~7% initial cash yield; raised 2026 AFFO guide to $4.41-4.44 and investment guide to $9.5B. But analyst consensus slipped to 'Hold' (from Moderate Buy) - 5 Strong Buy / 17 Hold / 1 Strong Sell - with PTs clustered low-$60s (Barclays $68, 'Goldilocks for net-lease'). Assignment would be owning a best-in-class net-lease REIT, but the DCAP rubric structurally floors REITs (low cash-on-assets, distribution-driven accumulated deficit, no buybacks).
research notes - methodology & caveats
Realty Income (O) is a net-lease (triple-net) equity REIT, Dec fiscal year. INVENTORY: genuinely none - holds real estate for investment, not inventory (0s; the small 'inventory' figures some data vendors show are spurious). PP&E = net real estate held for investment (SEC RealEstateInvestmentPropertyNet). LONG_TERM_DEBT = senior notes payable + term loans + mortgages payable, net (excludes short-term commercial paper); reconciled to SEC components (NotesPayable+LoansPayable+SecuredDebt) and matches stockanalysis exactly each year. RETAINED_EARNINGS = SEC AccumulatedDistributionsInExcessOfNetIncome - a large, growing accumulated deficit is structural for a REIT that distributes >100% of GAAP net income (depreciation-driven); kept negative. SE = parent stockholders' equity (SEC StockholdersEquity), positive all 4 yrs, so TSA-DSER applies. TREASURY_STOCK: none - no treasury-stock line in O's XBRL (an earlier web search claiming ~$1.05B treasury was a misread of accounts payable; SEC is authoritative). PREFERRED: none outstanding - all Series A preferred redeemed Sep 2024. EBITDA = SEC operating income (Revenues − CostsAndExpenses) + D&A, monotonic rising; cross-checks macrotrends (2435/2765/3256). CURRENT_LIABILITIES: O files an UNCLASSIFIED balance sheet (no current/noncurrent split), so this is a data-vendor (stockanalysis) aggregation of near-term obligations (commercial paper, current debt maturities, distributions payable, AP/accrued) - noted as a proxy; EP fails FY22 regardless. Goodwill step-up FY24 = Spirit Realty merger (closed Jan 2024); VEREIT merger drove the FY21→ base. Expect a REIT-floor score (EP/RTA/RE/RSH/TS structural) like AMT/IRM.

DCAP 7/17 (41%)
gift at ≥60%

Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 11.5%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)

Sentiment: Consensus Buy (15 analysts: ~27% Strong Buy, 47% Buy, 27% Hold), price target ~$425. High-quality serial-acquirer medtech; revenue forecast ~7.9%/yr and EPS ~16%/yr over next 3 yrs. Watch items: slowing organic growth, gross-margin miss vs consensus, and integration/dis-synergy risk from the Inari vascular acquisition (new physician specialties outside core ortho). No solvency/impairment red flags; assignment would be owning a durable-franchise compounder, but DCAP floors the balance-sheet quality like other acquisitive medtech.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Stryker Corp, medtech (Orthopaedics, MedSurg & Neurotechnology, Vascular). Dec-31 FY end; used FY2022-FY2025 (FY2025 10-K filed 2026-02-11). EBITDA = operating income + D&A, reconciles cleanly each year (e.g. FY25 4889+1193=6082). R&D expense line present (~$1.45-1.62B/yr) -> forfeits I&NE no-R&D point. Balance sheet carries NO treasury stock line -> equity section is common stock/APIC/retained earnings/AOCI; Stryker retires repurchased shares rather than holding treasury, so treasury_stock=absent and treasury_stock_abs=0 (this zeros the TS point and gives no |treasury| cushion to TSA-DSER). No preferred. FY25 goodwill +21.7% (15855->19291) and LTD step-up (12188->14859) reflect the ~$4.9B Inari Medical acquisition (vascular) closed 2025; GW still monotonically increasing (GW point ok), PPE no >60% spike. Expect a medtech floor: EP fails (EBITDA < current liabilities all 4 yrs), RTA avg NOCF/assets ~9.2% (<11%), TSA-DSER TL/SE ~1.15 avg (>1.0, no treasury cushion), I&NE broken by FY24 EBITDA dip (4916->4739, 96.4% of prior). Structurally similar to MDT/BDX medtech floors in the bench.

Sentiment: Trading near its all-time high (~$339 vs $343.45 52-wk high) at ~10x earnings; up ~20% YTD and ~32% over 1 year. Analyst tone is mixed-to-cautious on valuation: consensus Hold/Moderate Buy with an average target near $308 (below spot), and analysts model a ~25% YoY EPS decline off 2025's exceptional underwriting year; Simply Wall St pegs it ~9% overvalued while a DCF-based Yahoo piece argues 47% undervalued. TravelersLLM proprietary AI initiative is drawing positive attention on efficiency/underwriting quality. Fundamentals over the window are strong: operating cash flow up 64% (FY22→FY25), retained earnings +26%, equity +53%, debt modest at ~28% of equity. Q2 2026 earnings call is Friday 2026-07-17 - inside the 30-45 DTE window for any near-term CSP ladder. Assignment at a sub-spot strike on this franchise (Dow 30 P&C leader, 1.3% dividend, relentless buybacks) would be celebratable; the main caveat is entry near record highs with consensus targets below the current price.
research notes - methodology & caveats
P&C insurer (calendar fiscal year) with an UNCLASSIFIED balance sheet: no current-liabilities subtotal and no separately disclosed net PP&E in the 10-K or XBRL (PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet untagged; PP&E sits inside 'Other assets') - both series recorded null, not zero; expect EP and PPE parameters to no-score structurally, same pattern as PGR/CME memory. Inventory is genuinely 0 (insurance carries none). long_term_debt is TRV's single balance-sheet 'Debt' line (DebtLongtermAndShorttermCombinedAmount - essentially all long-term senior/junior notes, includes a small commercial-paper sliver; no LTD/current split is published). EBITDA = operating income + D&A per stockanalysis, verified to the exact million via SEC XBRL pretax income + interest expense + D&A (FY2022: 3,354+351+826=4,531 ... FY2025: 7,796+425+680=8,901). Retained earnings and treasury stock match stockanalysis vs SEC XBRL exactly. No preferred outstanding (no PreferredStockValue facts in any 10-K; equity section is common/retained/AOCI/treasury only). No R&D expense line. Heavy continuous buybacks held as treasury stock (grew $40.1B → $45.4B).

gift at ≥60%

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)2/2avg cash return on assets 36.8% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)0/2avg debt/assets 2.76 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 4.6 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negativeN/Aexcused: shareholders' equity was negative or incomplete in the window, so this test is off the scale (max drops to 15)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 2.4%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1equity is negative (heavy buybacks can do this) but 4 straight years of positive EBITDA back it - DANGER-rule pass
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Consensus Buy (23-24 analysts) but softening: avg PT cut from ~$464 to ~$404-434 on more cautious revenue/margin assumptions. Q1 2026 U.S. same-store sales +0.9% missed the 3% target; early-year SSS soft on weather/timing. Structural headwinds cited: high gas prices, inflation, GLP-1 weight-loss drugs, and a mid-2027 refinancing of ~$1.3B debt expected to cut EPS by 25-30 cents at current rates. Healthy, dominant QSR franchise but a decelerating-comps + rising-rate story; the negative-equity leveraged-recap structure is by design, not distress.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Domino's fiscal year ends the Sunday nearest Dec 31 (FY2025 ended Dec 28, 2025; 10-K filed Feb 2026). Leveraged-recap franchise: negative stockholders' equity all 4 years (SEC: -3,901.1M FY2025, -3,962.3M FY2024) from debt-funded buybacks, so TSA-DSER should be excused (max drops to 15). EBITDA = operating income + D&A, cross-checks with stockanalysis EBITDA line (848/900/967/1043). Retained earnings is a persistent accumulated DEFICIT (never flips sign), shrinking only ~2-3%/yr -> RE growth likely 0/3 (deficit-shrink pattern like CHTR/BKNG). Company REPURCHASES AND RETIRES shares (785,280 retired in 2025, $354.7M program) -> no treasury-stock line carried, so treasury_stock_abs=0 and TS flag 'absent' (buyback-retirement, an operator-call quirk like CMG/BJ). FY2024 current-liabilities spike to $1,612M is the $1,150M current portion of long-term debt ahead of a 2025 refinancing (LTD back up to $4,811M FY2025) -> EP fails only on FY2024 for this refi reason, not operations. Asset-light: tiny total assets vs ~$4.6B avg LTD means LTD/assets ~2.75 and LTD/EBITDA ~4.6 -> LTD likely 0/2. Strong cash economics: NOCF/assets avg ~37% (RTA 2/2) and latest EBITDA/|SE| positive with 4 yrs positive EBITDA (RSH DANGER rule should pass). Inventory not a steady rise (FY2024 destock) and goodwill declining (11.76->10.73). No R&D line; no preferred. Expect a leveraged-franchise floor well under the 60% gift bar.

gift at ≥60%

Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 2.5% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.06 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.8 years of EBITDA (cap 4)

Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)1/3retained earnings grew 7.2%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 16.2% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Stock at 52-week high ($120.46 on 2026-07-06, +9.7% YTD). Analyst tone neutral-to-cautious: consensus PT ~$112 (below price), 3 buy / 3 sell, flat revenue outlook with margin compression projected; trades at premium P/E vs insurance peers. June 2025 cyber incident exposed data of 22.6M people (SSNs, medical) - operations unaffected, no ransomware, 2-yr credit monitoring offered, class actions pending; brand intact. 43 consecutive years of dividend increases; buyback machine. Q2 earnings Aug 6-7, 2026 - lands INSIDE a 30-45 DTE window opened from early July; flag for chain analysis. Assignment at a sensible strike would be ownable (fortress franchise), but DCAP insurer pattern (EP/RTA/TSA-DSER structural fails) expected to cap the score below gift, consistent with PGR/TRV/WRB.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Insurer (supplemental health/life, Japan+US). Inventory 0s - insurance carries none. Current liabilities null x4 - insurance balance sheet is unclassified (no current/non-current split); EP unscorable, structural for insurers. EBITDA constructed = pretax income + interest expense on debt + D&A (no operating-income line on an insurer income statement; aggregator 'EBITDA' rows unreliable - stockanalysis shows $12.7B FY2025 which is a mislabeled aggregate). Components all from 10-K XBRL via EDGAR: pretax 4869/5262/6417/4533, interest (InterestExpenseDebt) 217/190/194/217, D&A 45/39/40/36; pretax series cross-checked exact-match vs stockanalysis. LTD = notes payable and lease obligations (DebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations tag) - Aflac's only debt line, essentially all long-term. FY = calendar year (ends Dec 31). FY2025 EBITDA dip driven by net investment losses (incl. yen remeasurement), not core underwriting. Treasury stock large and growing ($29.8B) - decades of buybacks; no preferred issued (no preferred line on balance sheet, no PreferredStockValue XBRL tag). Goodwill flat/slightly declining (265->260, FX on Japan goodwill). Balance-sheet series cross-checked EDGAR vs stockanalysis: exact match on all eight overlapping series.

Sentiment: Constructive, not euphoric. Q1 2026 revenue +6% with six growth drivers outpacing patent losses; MariTide (monthly obesity injectable) advancing into multiple Phase 3 MARITIME studies incl. a SWITCH trial from weekly GLP-1s, plus olpasiran in cardio - analysts (Mizuho PT $303, Neutral, 6/16/26) say the market may underprice the pipeline. Jul 2: stock +3% after federal judge blocked Colorado's Enbrel price cap. Watch items: Tavneos data-integrity questions, Corlanor recall, Enbrel erosion/IRA pricing, and still-heavy Horizon debt load. Assignment at a sane strike would be acceptable: durable biotech franchise with a real obesity option - though the leveraged, buyback-hollowed balance sheet is the structural DCAP weakness, not the business.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY = calendar year (Dec 31). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (both from EDGAR XBRL; matches stockanalysis EBITDA to the dollar). Horizon Therapeutics $27.8B acquisition closed Oct 2023: merger-year distortion drives the FY2023 jumps (total assets 65B->97B, LTD 37B->63B, inventory step-up 4.9B->9.5B); company has deleveraged since (LTD 63B->50B). Retained earnings is an accumulated DEFICIT caused by share repurchases + dividends charged against it - Amgen RETIRES repurchased shares, so treasury_stock flag is 'absent' with no treasury line (same retired-buyback pattern flagged on MSI/ADI: heavy buyback history exists but the TS point can't be awarded literally - operator call). R&D expense present and rising ($4.4B FY2022 -> $7.3B FY2025). Shareholders' equity positive all 4 years, so TSA-DSER applies, but equity is razor-thin vs $82B liabilities. Next earnings ~early Aug 2026 (likely inside 30-45 DTE windows).

Sentiment: Mixed-to-cautious. BD is now a pure-play medtech after divesting Biosciences/Diagnostics to Waters (closed Feb 2026); the strategic focus is welcomed but execution risk is high. Company CUT FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance sharply post-spin (from $14.75-15.05 to $12.35-12.65), triggering price-target cuts: TD Cowen to $163 (Hold), BofA to $170 (Neutral). Consensus still leans Buy/Outperform (~$190-210 targets) but with reduced conviction. Overhangs: voluntary recall of certain skin-prep products (contamination), integration/dis-synergy from the separation. Not a clean 'delighted to own' story right now - the business being assigned in 2026 differs from the 4-year filings, and guidance is falling. Recommend operator review before any play given the post-spin transformation.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Becton Dickinson (BDX), medical-device maker; fiscal year ends Sept 30, so window is FY2022-FY2025 as filed in 10-Ks. Carries real inventory (medical products/consumables). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (D&A ~$2.2-2.5B/yr). R&D expense line present (~$1.19-1.27B/yr). Preferred stock: none outstanding at FY2025 (mandatory-convertible preferred from 2020 already converted); a new class was created only in Feb 2026 as a spin-off mechanic, post-period. Treasury stock large and growing (active buyback). MAJOR EVENT: on 2026-02-09 BD completed the separation of its Biosciences & Diagnostic Solutions unit to Waters Corp ($18.8B; BD got $4B cash + 39.2% Waters stake) - this occurred AFTER FY2025, so these four years reflect the pre-separation whole company; go-forward BD is a smaller pure-play medtech and the FY2026 10-K will materially reshape every series. Structural DCAP drags expected: EP (EBITDA < current liabilities every year), RE growth is slow (~3%/yr avg - dividend aristocrat pays out most earnings), and EBITDA dipped FY22->FY23 so I&NE steady-rise fails. Flag for operator: the spin-off means the entity we would be assigned in 2026+ is not the same business these financials describe.

Sentiment: Hold consensus (2 Buy / 21 Hold / 7 Sell as of mid-2026); avg PT ~$99, Goldman Sell $83, Morgan Stanley Equal-Weight $97. FY2026 guidance CUT hard: EPS to $4.78-4.98 from $5.60-5.95 (-24% to -27% y/y), gross margin down 250-300bps, net sales ~-6% / organic ~-9% - largely the reversal of ~2 weeks of ERP-transition pre-shipments booked in FY2025 (~$0.90 EPS drag). Ex-ERP noise, underlying organic +3-5%. Durable brand franchise (Clorox, Kingsford, Glad, Brita, Burt's Bees) but near-term earnings are in a self-inflicted air-pocket; assignment is not especially celebratable in the current window.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Clorox; fiscal year ends June 30 (FY2025 = yr ended 6/30/2025, 10-K filed 8/8/2025). All figures from SEC 10-K consolidated statements. PPE = core us-gaap PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (1334/1345/1315/1267); aggregators (stockanalysis) inflate this to ~1600 by folding in the separate operating-lease ROU asset line (~$333M) - I used the core line per the BJ ROU-trap lesson. EBITDA = operating income + D&A, reconciled to the 10-K: FY22 750+224, FY23 408+236, FY24 512+235, FY25 1080+219. Clorox reports no explicit 'operating income' subtotal; op income derived = gross profit less S&A/advertising/R&D/divestiture-loss/pension/impairment, matches stockanalysis. NOTE the window is noisy: FY2023 carries a $445M goodwill/trademark/asset impairment (VMS) and FY2024 was the cyberattack + $240M divestiture-loss + $171M pension-settlement year (EBITDA trough 644->747), FY2025 the recovery (1299, boosted by ~2 weeks of ERP pre-shipment inventory that reverses in FY2026). shareholders_equity = reported 'Total stockholders' equity' incl. small NCI (~$161-173M); Total Clorox-only SE = 556/220/328/321. Either basis leaves all 4 years positive (TSA-DSER applies, not excused) and RSH passing hugely, so choice is non-decisive. Retained earnings positive but volatile (dividends + FY23/24 charges); treasury $1,404M/8.05M sh - buybacks + $0.05M-par thin equity structure. Preferred: 5,000,000 auth, none issued/outstanding.

Sentiment: Healthy, well-regarded ag franchise: analysts ~75% Buy/Strong-Buy, 0% Sell; FY2026 guidance reaffirmed at $4.0-4.2B operating EBITDA (+7% midpoint), Q1'26 Seed +11.7% / Crop Protection +10.1%. BUT major corporate action: Corteva announced a Q4 2026 separation into "New Corteva" (standalone crop-protection company) and a separate seed business "Vylor" - an assigned position today would end up in a different, split entity, so this needs an operator call and a rescore after the split. Competitive pressure from Bayer and softer crop-protection margins are the main fundamental overhangs. Structural DCAP ceiling is the ag working-capital story (EP/RTA/RE), not a quality problem.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Corteva (CTVA): agricultural seed + crop protection, FY ends Dec 31; spun off from DowDuPont 2019. EBITDA = operating income + total cash-flow D&A (verified against SEC XBRL: FY2022 1565+1223=2788, FY2023 1774+1211=2985, FY2024 1808+1227=3035, FY2025 2438+1203=3641 - matches stockanalysis exactly). Carries real inventory (seed/crop-chem), so I&NE uses actual balances (note inventory DROPS FY2024 on destocking, so it is not a steady rise). R&D expense line present (~$1.2-1.5B/yr). Huge seasonal current liabilities (deferred revenue/prepay + payables, $10-12B) dwarf EBITDA -> EP fails structurally, same working-capital pattern as ADM/BG/CMI ag names. Retained earnings oscillate near zero by design (250/-41/55/-67) -> capital returned via dividends + share retirement keeps RE ~0; RE growth scores 0. No treasury-stock line (constructive retirement of buybacks -> TS flag absent, treasury_stock_abs=0). No preferred stock (only common $7M). PP&E is XBRL PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet (net, not ROU-inflated). SE is total stockholders' equity, all 4 years positive. Long-term debt modest (LTD/EBITDA latest 1686/3641=0.46). All figures USD millions, oldest->newest FY2022->FY2025.

DLTR - Dollar Tree not-a-gift

Retail Trade · price $121.46 · mcap $23.3B · vol 3,603,106 · PE 19.4 · PB 6.8 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 6/17 (35%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)1/2avg cash return on assets 11.3% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)2/2avg debt/assets 0.15 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 1.1 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 2.48 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -20.2%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 61.3% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Hold consensus (19 analysts, ~$117-120 PT, Jun 2026). Standalone post-Family-Dollar Dollar Tree is executing: Q1 FY2026 (May 2026) adj EPS $1.74 beat ~$1.55 est, revenue +7.2% to $4.98B, raised FY2026 guidance to adj EPS $6.70-7.10 and net sales $20.5-20.7B, comps +3-4%. The business is a cleaner, less-levered discount retailer post-divestiture; the un-celebratable part is purely the messy 4-year window (two segments -> impairment losses -> standalone), not current operations. No takeover/breakup overhang remaining.
research notes - methodology & caveats
DIVESTITURE-DISTORTED WINDOW - operator call. DLTR fiscal years end late Jan/early Feb: FY2022=Jan-28-2023, FY2023=Feb-3-2024 (53-wk), FY2024=Feb-1-2025, FY2025=Jan-31-2026 (latest 10-K filed 2026-03-16). NOTE: stockanalysis.com mislabels DLTR period-end dates by ~1 year (53-wk-year quirk) - discarded it, all figures from SEC XBRL / as-filed 10-K R-files. FAMILY DOLLAR SALE: In Q4 FY2024 DLTR classified Family Dollar as held-for-sale/discontinued ops; sold to Brigade+Macellum for ~$1.0B (net ~$0.8B), closed July 2025 (FY2025). The go-forward assigned entity is standalone Dollar Tree only. This makes a clean 4-yr series impossible. MIXED BASIS (unavoidable, documented): FLOW items (ebitda, net_operating_cash_flow) use CONTINUING-OPERATIONS basis for all 4 yrs - the only basis presented consistently across the window and the go-forward business. EBITDA = continuing operating income + continuing D&A: FY22 2099.3+365.3; FY23 1774.5+400.5; FY24 1462.0+526.9; FY25 1653.1+648.1. NOCF = us-gaap NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations. BALANCE-SHEET items use AS-REPORTED FACE values from each 10-K: FY22-FY23 consolidated (incl Family Dollar); FY24 segregates Family Dollar into held-for-sale (current_liabilities 8585.9 includes ~$5B current liab of disc ops; total_assets 18644 includes 5008.9 current assets of disc ops); FY25 standalone. This mix inflates FY24 current_liabilities/leverage and, conversely, flatters FY25 RTA/RSH (continuing NOCF/EBITDA over shrunken standalone assets/equity) - treat borderline RTA (~11%) and RSH as artifacts. NET LOSSES: FY23 net (998.4) and FY24 net (3030.1) driven by Family Dollar discontinued-ops charges (-2264.2, -4072.6); retained_earnings falls 8123->7125->3942->3804 across the window (RE growth negative -> 0/3). Goodwill 1983->914->421->423: FY22->FY23 drop is Family Dollar goodwill impairment; Dollar Tree segment goodwill ~$423M flat throughout (GW not increasing -> 0/1). Flags: no R&D line (retailer); no preferred outstanding (equity = common $0.01 par + APIC + AOCI + RE, no preferred line); NO treasury stock line - DLTR retires repurchased shares (treasury_stock_abs=0, TS flag absent, like MSI/CMG/COST retirement pattern). Debt materially reduced post-divestiture (LTD 3426->2431) -> LTD 2/2 strong. Structural retailer working capital + held-for-sale inflation fail EP and TSA-DSER. Expect ~6/17 not-a-gift; the standalone go-forward Dollar Tree is healthier than this window shows - re-score after FY2026 10-K (~Mar 2027) when 2 clean standalone years exist.

Line item (as researched)FY2022FY2023FY2024FY2025
Inventory5,4495,1132,6722,495
EBITDA2,4652,1751,9892,301
Current liabilities4,2254,6978,5863,229

ELV - Elevance Health not-a-gift

DCAP 6/17 (35%)
gift at ≥60%

Sentiment: 2025 was the managed-care sector's trough: elevated Medicaid/ACA medical cost trend reset ELV's adjusted EPS base from ~$33 to the mid-$20s. Tone has since turned constructive: Q1 2026 beat (revenue +1.5% to $49.5B, adj EPS +5.1% to $12.58, benefit expense ratio improved to 86.8%), FY2026 adj EPS guidance raised to ≥$26.75, and management targets ≥12% adj EPS growth in 2027; stock rebounded from a $273 52-wk low to ~$408. Watch items: $935M CMS risk-adjustment accrual (management says no impact to FY outlook), elevated Medicaid cost trend vs slightly below-trend rate updates, ACA subsidy policy risk. Moat intact (Blue Cross licenses = switching costs); assignment at a sensible strike would be palatable, but note ~$40k capital per contract at current price.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Elevance Health (managed care; Blue Cross Blue Shield licensee). All figures $ millions, FY2022→FY2025, fiscal year ends Dec 31; FY2025 10-K filed 2026-02-06 (accn 0001156039-26-000013). Inventory: no inventory line on the balance sheet - services business (health benefits + Carelon) - recorded as 0s. EBITDA = operating income (XBRL operating gain less amortization of other intangible assets: 7,516/7,614/7,282/6,571) + total cash-flow D&A net of accretion (1,675/1,745/1,393/1,546), all from EDGAR 10-K XBRL as filed; FY2022 reconciles exactly to stockanalysis.com's published EBITDA (9,191). Note: stockanalysis.com's cash-flow D&A column shuffles FY2023-FY2025 vs the as-filed 10-K values, so its EBITDA for those years (9,007/8,828/8,316) differs modestly - EDGAR as-filed used. Shareholders' equity = parent "Total shareholders' equity" (XBRL StockholdersEquity); total equity incl. noncontrolling interests is slightly higher (36,330/39,405/41,426/44,026). Preferred stock: authorized, none issued (PreferredStockValue = 0 all years). Treasury stock: no treasury line - ELV repurchases and retires shares, so heavy buyback history earns no TS point under the letter of the rule (flag for operators: ~$2-3B/yr retired buybacks exist but the balance-sheet line is absent). No R&D expense line. NOCF declined sharply FY2023→FY2025 (8,061→5,808→4,290) on Medicaid redetermination acuity and ACA cost trend - this is the trough-year story.

Sentiment: Semiconductor-materials name leveraged to WFE/advanced-node capex and AI-driven memory/logic buildout; consumables model gives more stability than equipment. Multi-year story is de-levering after the ~$6B CMC Materials deal - debt down from $5.6B to $3.7B, margins pressured by intangible amortization and a soft-but-recovering 2023 semi cycle. Analyst tone constructive-to-neutral on the deleveraging + AI leverage, but leverage remains the overhang and China export-control exposure is a recurring risk. Nothing scandalous; assignment risk is cyclical, not existential.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Entegris (ENTG) - semiconductor materials/consumables (fluid handling, deposition materials, CMP slurries/pads). FY ends Dec 31; window = FY2022-FY2025, latest 10-K FY2025 filed Feb 2026. The window is dominated by the July-2022 CMC Materials acquisition (levered assets to $10.1B, LTD to $5.6B, goodwill to $4.4B) followed by 2023-2025 divestitures (electronic chemicals, PIM, etc.) used to de-lever - so total assets SHRINK 10.1B->8.4B and goodwill DECLINES (4408->3947) across the window, an M&A-then-divest artifact. IMPORTANT DATA NOTE: stockanalysis.com mislabels FY2023 operating income as $129.6M and thus reports FY2023 EBITDA ~$517M - this is WRONG. Primary GAAP FY2023 operating income is $499.158M (Q4-2023 release); EBITDA here = GAAP operating income + full D&A (depreciation + amortization of intangibles) from the earnings releases: FY22 480.0+279.3=759.3; FY23 499.2+387.2=886.3; FY24 533.9+378.2=912.1; FY25 455.9+389.7=845.6. NOCF also taken from primary releases (FY22 352.3, FY23 629.6, FY24 631.7, FY25 695.4). Inventory NOT a steady rise (post-CMC destock 812->607). EP fails only in FY2022 where EBITDA 759.3 sits just under current liabilities 761.93 (merger year). LTD/EBITDA latest = 4.37 (>4). Latest EBITDA/SE = 21.4% (just under 23% RSH bar). Heavy CMC intangible amortization depresses EBITDA-to-asset ratios (RTA ~6.6%). Big R&D line (present). Treasury stock tiny ($7.1M) but present. No preferred. SE positive all 4 years so TSA-DSER applies (not excused).

Sentiment: Strongly bullish operator/analyst tone in 2026: stock near all-time highs (~$115, 52wk high $118), up ~44% off the July-2025 low. Consensus Buy/Outperform; targets $118-$135 (Scotiabank $129, UBS $135). Thesis = Gulf-South data-center + industrial load growth (~3.5 GW contracted 2025, 7-12 GW pipeline, 20-yr Meta/Evest deal, ~10% industrial / ~5% retail sales growth in 2026). Funding the capex via forward equity sales. Nothing that makes assignment un-celebratable - it's a healthy, in-favor utility - but the DCAP structural floor is the constraint, not sentiment.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Entergy Corp - regulated electric utility (AR/LA/MS/TX), FY ends Dec. EBITDA built as operating income + cash-flow-statement D&A, which reconciles to stockanalysis EBITDA every year (2051+2190=4241 ... 3202+2537=5739). PP&E is genuine regulated utility rate base (not an ROU-lease artifact), so no aggregator inflation trap. Preferred flagged PRESENT: consolidated balance sheet carries subsidiaries' preferred stock without sinking fund (part of Entergy's defined capital structure). Treasury stock present (~$4.8B, slowly declining). Inventory = fuel/materials & supplies. No R&D line (utility). Expect the classic regulated-utility floor: EP fails (current liabilities > EBITDA every year on short-term debt/current maturities), RTA low (~6.4% avg, capital intensity), TSA-DSER fails (leverage, avg TL/(SE+treasury) ~2.4), GW declining, and RE avg growth ~6.7% just under the 7% first tier (FY24 +0.6% flat year drags it). Sits with ATO (7/17) and DUK (8/17) as a structural utility non-gift. See [[ato-utility-floor]], [[duk-utility-floor-re-lowbase]].
