Sentiment: Analyst tone neutral-to-cautious: most recent rating a Hold, ~$75 PT (UBS). The Worldpay/Issuer Solutions transformation closed Jan 12, 2026, creating a pure-play commerce provider; management guides FY2026 to ~5% constant-currency adjusted net revenue growth, adjusted EPS $13.80-14.00 (13-15% growth), >90% FCF conversion, and >$2B of capital returns (incl. a $500M ASR). But Q1 2026 booked a GAAP loss (deal/integration charges) even as adjusted EPS grew, and the balance sheet now carries deal financing. A payments name mid-integration of a transformative acquisition is a 'watch, don't celebrate yet' assignment - execution/leverage risk until the combined entity's financials settle.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Global Payments Inc., merchant/issuer payments processor; FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (cash-flow D&A): FY22 640.2+1756, FY23 1316+1895, FY24 1975+1994, FY25 1755+1543 (identity matches stockanalysis EBITDA row exactly). shareholders_equity is PARENT-only 'Total Global Payments shareholders' equity' per SEC XBRL (excludes noncontrolling interests of 237/280/575/690M) to avoid the NCI trap; all four years positive so TSA-DSER applies (no negative-equity excusal, max 17). NO inventory line (payments processor). NO treasury-stock line: GPN repurchases and RETIRES shares (paid-in capital fell 18,119->17,079M FY24->FY25), so treasury_stock 'absent' / abs=0 is a buyback-retirement artifact, not lack of buybacks. NO preferred (5M authorized, none issued). NO R&D expense line (SG&A only). PP&E is real 'Property and equipment, net' from the 10-K, NOT ROU-inflated (operating-lease ROU 360/346/248/250M sits in Other noncurrent assets). Current liabilities are inflated by settlement/merchant float (classic processor EP/RTA drag). Goodwill drops FY23->FY24 (26,744->17,028M) = Issuer Solutions reclass/impairment ahead of divestiture. HARD STRUCTURAL FLAG: the Worldpay acquisition (from FIS/GTCR) + Issuer Solutions divestiture (to FIS) CLOSED Jan 12, 2026 - so FY22-FY25 is the OLD entity; from FY2026 GPN is a different pure-play commerce company with Worldpay and materially more debt (LTD already ramped to 19.5B). This score has limited forward relevance; rescore after the FY2026 10-K. Cross-check: retained earnings, parent SE, goodwill, LTD, current liab, total liab, total assets, PP&E all verified directly against SEC XBRL / the FY2025 & FY2023 10-K balance sheets (primary), and match the stockanalysis aggregator rows.

Sentiment: Constructive/strong. FY2025 record earnings (pretax $4,760M, net income ~$3,836M; revenue $28.1B). Analyst consensus Moderate Buy (11 Strong Buy / 14 Hold of 26), avg PT ~$148 (~7% upside). Watch item: P&C pricing cycle cooling in property/reinsurance through early 2026 plus Q1-2026 winter-storm/convective cat losses and softer small-business/middle-market pricing -> some EPS-growth deceleration into FY2026. No governance/solvency red flags; well-run diversified P&C + group benfits insurer. Assignment would be ownership of a high-quality insurer, but DCAP rubric structurally penalizes the insurance float model.
research notes - methodology & caveats
The Hartford (rebranded "Hartford Insurance Group" in 2025), calendar FY (Dec 31). P&C insurer -> NO inventory (0s genuine). Insurers file an UNCLASSIFIED balance sheet with no current/non-current split; current_liabilities here = insurance float proxy = claims/loss reserves + unearned premiums (FY22 41,745+7,815; FY23 42,802+8,599; FY24 45,058+9,408; FY25 46,712+10,053). EBITDA built OI+D&A and cross-checked pretax+interest+D&A: FY25 pretax $4,760M + int ~$230M + D&A $396M = ~$5,386M vs $5,355M here (<0.6% diff); cash-flow D&A (625/510/356/396) reconciles OI to EBITDA each year. PP&E is net PP&E (small for an insurer, ~$0.9B). Preferred present: $334M 6.00% Series G still paying dividends in 2025 (PS point should be 0). Treasury present and growing (1,773->4,589) = active buybacks. Goodwill flat $1,911M all 4 yrs (no increase -> GW point 0). Expect classic insurer float floor: EP fails 4x (EBITDA ~$3-5B << float ~$50-57B), RTA low (NOCF/assets ~5-7%). Do not re-run on window roll unless structure changes. See [[dcap-rubric-penalizes-insurers]].

KHC - Kraft Heinz not-a-gift

Consumer Non-Durables · price $24.82 · mcap $29.4B · vol 14,045,152 · PE -5.1 · PB 0.7 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 6/17 (35%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence

Sentiment: Negative/"show me". KHC trades ~$25 near 52-wk lows (52wk 21.0-29.2), P/E negative on the FY2025 loss, dividend yield ~6.4% (payout under scrutiny), P/B ~0.7. Sept 2025 the company announced a plan to split into two companies (Global Taste Elevation + North American Grocery); in early 2026 new CEO Steve Cahillane CANCELLED the breakup after review (~$300M cost, diverted focus), redirecting >$600M into marketing/R&D/innovation. Berkshire (28% stake) opposed the split. Analysts cautious ("many years of investment to overcome"); a $9.3B FY2025 impairment underlines eroding brand equity. Assignment here would NOT be celebrated: a value-trap staples turnaround with impairing goodwill and a stressed payout.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Kraft Heinz Co, packaged-food staples, FY ends last Saturday of Dec (FY2025 = Dec 27, 2025). EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A (from cash-flow statement): FY22 3634+933=4567, FY23 4572+961=5533, FY24 1683+948=2631, FY25 -4669+968=-3701; internally reconciles to stockanalysis EBITDA row and cross-checked vs stocktitan. FY2025 operating income and EBITDA swung deeply negative on a ~$9.3B non-cash goodwill/intangible impairment (goodwill fell 28,673->22,179); operating cash flow stayed strongly positive (~$4.46B), so this is an accounting reset, not a cash collapse. Inventory carried (physical-goods maker) but DECLINING over the window. No separate R&D expense line on the income statement (typical for packaged food; R&D folded into SG&A) -> flag absent. No preferred stock outstanding. Treasury stock present and rising (847->2636). Retained earnings built modestly 489->2171 then swung to a -4,629 accumulated deficit in FY2025 on the impairment (sign flip). Shareholders' equity positive all 4 years (TSA-DSER applicable). Legacy 2015-merger goodwill/intangible base is enormous and impairing repeatedly (2018-19 and again 2025), which structurally suppresses RTA/RSH/EP and now RE/GW.

KKR - KKR &amp; Co Inc not-a-gift

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets -0.1% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)1/2avg debt/assets 0.14 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 90.6 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 11.56 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)

Sentiment: Constructive Street tone: ~Buy consensus (21-26 analysts, ~64% Buy / 21% Strong Buy, 0% Sell), 12-mo targets ~$125-130 implying ~30-40% upside; Global Atlantic full ownership framed as a 'closed-loop capital flywheel' boosting fee-paying AUM and durable earnings. Next earnings 7/29/2026 (cons. EPS ~$1.40). Sentiment is bullish on the franchise/compounding story - the DCAP floor is a rubric-vs-structure artifact (fund + insurance consolidation), not a business-quality red flag.
research notes - methodology & caveats
KKR & Co. Inc. (FY=Dec). ALTERNATIVE ASSET MANAGER + consolidated insurer (Global Atlantic, fully owned from Jan 2024) + consolidated fund/CFE vehicles - balance sheet is unclassified and dominated by insurance/fund assets & NCI, so most ratios read like insurer-float names (ARES/APO/BX/Apollo/Carlyle tier). Key modeling choices: (1) shareholders_equity = equity ATTRIBUTABLE TO KKR & Co. Inc. common (parent), per SEC XBRL us-gaap:StockholdersEquity: 18,808/22,859/23,652/30,903M - positive all 4 yrs, so TSA-DSER is NOT excused (max stays 17). Total equity incl. NCI is far larger (~55-82B) but that is not KKR's equity. (2) inventory=0 - a financial firm carries none. (3) goodwill & PP&E are NOT on the face of the balance sheet (folded into 'Other assets'); taken from the 10-K Other-Assets notes: Goodwill 594.3/558.3/509.6/519.6M (declining then flat → GW likely 0); Fixed Assets net (ex-ROU) 857.9/863.1/902.9/975.5M (steady rise, no spike). ROU lease assets excluded from PP&E. (4) treasury_stock_abs=0 - KKR RETIRES repurchased shares/equity awards (no treasury line on balance sheet), so TS scores 0 like other retire-not-hold names. (5) preferred_stock=present - 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred issued Mar-2025 (~$2.25B) plus the sole Series I governance preferred share → PS scores 0. (6) EBITDA is aggregator GAAP operating-income+D&A (stockanalysis): 158/2383/1045/584M - highly distorted by fund/insurance consolidation and volatile; note GAAP consolidated PRETAX income diverges massively (~$7.1B FY23 / $6.8B FY24, Yahoo) because it includes fund/policyholder investment income attributable to NCI. Either EBITDA measure fails EP (current liabilities 11.5-18.1B >> any EBITDA), RTA (NOCF/assets ~0%), LTD/EBITDA (>>4), and RSH (<23%), so the exact EBITDA does not move any parameter. NOCF is likewise consolidation-distorted (negative FY22-23 from GA/fund flows). RE is the one clean bright spot: parent retained earnings 6,701→13,884M, genuine positive growth ~28%/yr avg (46.5%/25.1%/13.0%) → RE 3/3, NOT a sign-flip artifact. Cross-checks: total_assets/total_liabilities/retained_earnings/parent-equity all tie to SEC XBRL companyfacts exactly; RE also matches stockanalysis. Expect ~6/17 - an alt-manager floor, NOT a gift; would not celebrate assignment on balance-sheet quality grounds (the franchise is excellent, the rubric just can't see it through fund/insurance consolidation). No lever to flip.

Sentiment: Bullish franchise/backdrop: US defense budget projected >$1T, strong tailwinds from Golden Dome (GD4A) missile-defense and Trident II D5 work; Q1'26 analyst mix ~15 Buy / 6 Hold / 0 Sell, median PT ~$390 (range $283-$443). 2026 guide $23-23.5B revenue, EPS $11.40-11.60. Assignment would be celebratable on business quality; the low DCAP score is structural (merger goodwill + contract-liability working-capital floor + acquisition debt), not a franchise-deterioration signal.
research notes - methodology & caveats
L3Harris Technologies, defense prime (comms, space/sensors, missile-defense; Golden Dome/Trident). Fiscal year ends Fri nearest Dec 31 (FY2022=12/30/22, FY2023=12/29/23, FY2024=1/3/25, FY2025=1/2/26). Carries real inventory (defense manufacturing) but inventory declines FY23->FY25, so I&NE inventory-rise leg misses. EBITDA built as operating income + D&A (SEC: OI 1127/1426/1918/2110; D&A 938/1166/1289/1224). Goodwill ~$20B from L3+Harris 2019 merger and Aerojet Rocketdyne 2023 acquisition floors RTA (NOCF/assets ~6%) and RSH (EBITDA/SE ~17%). EP fails all 4 years: large contract liabilities/customer advances put current liabilities > EBITDA (defense working-capital floor, same pattern as industrial primes). Aerojet+refi debt (LTD jumped $6.2B->$11.2B in FY23) pushes avg TL/(SE) ~1.06, so TSA-DSER 0/2. shareholders_equity is total equity (incl small NCI, = TA-TL); SEC parent equity is ~$100M lower and positive every year, so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). treasury_stock_abs=0: LHX retires repurchased shares (no treasury-stock line and no XBRL TreasuryStockValue tag despite ~$1.2B FY25 buybacks) -> TS point structurally 0, retirement pattern. Income statement carries NO separate R&D expense line (company-funded R&D embedded in cost of sales) -> rnd flag absent. Preferred: 0 shares issued/outstanding (SEC).

Sentiment: FY2026 was Medtronic's strongest year in a decade: revenue $36.4B (+8.4% reported, +5.8% organic), highest annual revenue growth in 10 years, driven by Diabetes (planned MiniMed separation), cardiac ablation (Affera/PulseSelect), and Hugo soft-tissue robot US approval. Analyst tone constructive-to-neutral; leverage and goodwill-heavy balance sheet are the fundamental drags, not the franchise. Nothing that would make assignment un-celebratable; a durable, diversified medtech leader.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Medtronic plc (medtech), FY ends late April; FY2026 ended Apr 24, 2026 (10-K filed 2026). EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A (FY23 5485+2697; FY24 5144+2647; FY25 5955+2861; FY26 6467+2958). Inventory and EBITDA both DIP in FY24 (destock/margin trough), so I&NE steady-rise fails; R&D expense line present (~$2.7-2.9B/yr). Current liabilities (~$9-13B) exceed EBITDA in FY23 (8182 vs 9051) and FY24, so EP fails. Goodwill-heavy (~$42B from Covidien) drags RTA (~7.4% avg NOCF/assets) and RSH (18.8% EBITDA/SE). RE grows only ~2.4%/yr avg (mature medtech). Irish plc REDEEMS/cancels repurchased shares -> no treasury stock line (treasury_stock_abs=0, TS point unavailable), same structure as an Irish-domiciled buyback program. SE positive all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). Equity figures include noncontrolling interest (~$600M) to balance the sheet; RE attributable confirmed $32,638M in FY26 10-K. Structural medtech floor akin to [[bdx-not-a-gift-spinoff]].

Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 22.7% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Neutral-to-mildly-positive. Q2 FY2026 (reported late June 2026) beat: net sales ~$1.94B vs $1.91B est., adj. EPS $0.80 vs $0.70 consensus; FY2026 outlook reaffirmed (adj. EPS $3.05-$3.13), CEO touting margin strength in a tough cost environment. Concerns: volumes soft (Q2 organic +1.7% almost all pricing; volume/mix -0.5%), value-seeking consumers trading toward private label, tariff/cost pressure. Analyst consensus neutral: 6 buy/9 hold/0 sell, median PT $82.50. Dividend paid ($0.48/qtr, ex-date 2026-07-06); decades-long dividend-growth record. Assignment would be owning the dominant global spice/flavor franchise - celebratable as a business, though the balance sheet carries heavy acquisition goodwill.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Nov 30 (FY2025 = period ended 2025-11-30, 10-K filed Jan 2026). EBITDA = operating income + D&A, both from SEC XBRL (OI 863.6/963.0/1060.3/1070.8; D&A 200.6/199.3/208.8/231.3), matching stockanalysis's stated EBITDA. Shareholders' equity is SEC StockholdersEquity (parent, excludes NCI); stockanalysis shows total equity incl. NCI (~$20-30M higher/yr). R&D flag: R&D is filed in XBRL (ResearchAndDevelopmentExpense $87.5M/$94.9M/$102.9M/$106.1M, ~1.5% of sales) though shown in notes rather than as a separate face-of-income-statement line - marked present (conservative read; CHD-style judgment call, flagging for operators). Treasury stock: no treasury-stock concept filed - MKC retires repurchased shares, so buyback history exists but no treasury line (absent, per the data). No preferred stock filed. Inventory dipped FY2022→FY2023 (destocking), goodwill dipped FY2024 (FX on Cholula/FONA-heavy goodwill base).

gift at ≥60%

Sentiment: Constructive-to-bullish. Stock +36% over the past year near 52-week highs on the AI data-center power theme: FPL data-center request pipeline ~21 GW, NEER contracted backlog ~33 GW and a data-center hub >60 GW, plus two Japan-backed gas projects (~10 GW) for PA/TX data centers. Reaffirmed 2026 adj EPS $3.92-4.02 with 8%+ CAGR through 2032 and ~10%/yr dividend growth. Analyst consensus target ~$99 (range ~$99-115), overweight-tilted. WATCH: reported all-stock ~$67B acquisition of Dominion Energy -- a transformational merger that, if it closes, materially changes the balance sheet and would warrant a re-score. This is a business one would be content to own, but the DCAP scorer floors it on regulated-utility capital intensity + leverage; not a gift on the numbers.
research notes - methodology & caveats
NextEra Energy (NEE): regulated electric utility (Florida Power & Light) + NextEra Energy Resources (renewables/storage). FY ends Dec; FY2022-FY2025 all filed. shareholders_equity is parent-attributable common equity (SEC us-gaap:StockholdersEquity = 39229/47468/50101/54608), which excludes the large noncontrolling interests (tax-equity + NextEra Energy Partners); using it keeps TSA-DSER honest. EBITDA reconstructed as SEC OperatingIncomeLoss + cash-flow D&A and cross-confirms stockanalysis exactly: [8871,16388,13240,15221] -- highly volatile because NEER carries mark-to-market gains/losses on energy derivatives (FY2022 MTM losses depress it, FY2023 MTM gains spike it), so the EBITDA-dependent params (I&NE steady-rise, EP) read weak for a genuinely durable franchise. Utility structure: PPE steady CAPEX ramp; current liabilities exceed EBITDA every year (classic reg-utility working capital) so EP fails; NOCF/assets ~5-7% (rate-base capital intensity) so RTA floors; TL/parent-equity ~2.6x avg so TSA-DSER floors -- the standard regulated-utility DCAP floor (cf. DUK 8/17, ATO 7/17, ETR 6/17). Goodwill essentially flat/declining (4854->4849) so GW 0. No treasury stock (utility issues shares to fund CAPEX, doesn't buy back). No preferred on the parent balance sheet ($0; the NEE.PRx listings are junior subordinated debentures/equity units, not balance-sheet preferred). No R&D expense line.

Sentiment: Mixed-to-cautious. Buy consensus but price targets cut into the $620-628 range (from ~$700) on below-average organic growth, B-21 margin dilution, and rising capex (~4.5% of sales, +14%/yr). Record backlog ~$95.6B; B-21 moved from loss to operating-income contribution after renegotiated agreements; Sentinel ICBM and space/missile-defense are long-cycle drivers. Several firms at Hold: full valuation, high customer (US govt) concentration, and rotation away from defense limit near-term upside. FY2026 guide sales $43.5-44.0B, adj EPS $27.40-27.90. Nothing that would make assignment un-celebratable operationally, but this is a structural DCAP floor name, not a gift.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Northrop Grumman, defense prime (FY ends Dec 31). Classic defense-prime DCAP floor (cf. LMT 8/17, LHX 6/17): (1) EBITDA well below current liabilities in all 4 yrs - contract/advance-payment liabilities balloon current liabilities, so EP fails 0x4. (2) Goodwill-heavy asset base (~$17.5B, ~1/3 of assets) floors RTA (avg NOCF/assets ~8.3%, <11%). (3) NOC RETIRES repurchased shares - no treasury-stock line exists in the XBRL/balance sheet, so treasury_stock_abs=0 and TS param scores 0 despite heavy buybacks. (4) RE dipped FY2022->FY2023 (15,312->14,773) on the ~$1.56B pre-tax B-21 loss provision recognized in 2023, then recovered; avg YoY ~3% so RE 0/3. Goodwill flat-to-declining (17,516->17,437) -> GW 0/1. EBITDA computed as operating income + D&A and matches stockanalysis EBITDA row (OI+D&A: 3601+1342=4943; 2537+1338=3875; 4370+1370=5740; 4511+1472=5983). No R&D expense line (company-funded R&D sits in cost of sales) -> I&NE gets the no-R&D point but inventory/EBITDA not steady (FY23 EBITDA + FY25 inventory dips). No preferred stock. Positive SE all 4 yrs so TSA-DSER applies (max 17); leverage avg TL/SE ~2.1 fails both tiers. Strong points: LTD/assets ~0.29 and LTD/EBITDA ~2.5 (LTD 2/2), RSH latest EBITDA/SE ~35.9%.

gift at ≥60%

Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years1/1EBITDA covered current liabilities in every one of the 4 years
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)1/1rose steadily with no acquisition-sized spikes
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 5.2% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)1/2avg debt/assets 0.32 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 5.8 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative2/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 0.62 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding0/1preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -35.0%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 10.5% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Consensus Buy (16 analysts; ~38% Strong Buy), avg PT ~$154 (mid-single-digit upside). June 2026: BMO upgraded to Outperform ($162 PT), RBC/Truist raised targets - optimism centers on PLD's push into DATA CENTER development as an AI-infrastructure revenue pillar (highlighted in Q1 2026 results). Offset: lagging rent growth and softening industrial/warehouse demand remain risks despite falling vacancy. Blue-chip logistics landlord; sentiment constructive but not euphoric.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Prologis (PLD) - large industrial/logistics REIT (Dec-31 FY). REIT structure drives several structural zeros. NO INVENTORY (owns real estate, not goods) -> inventory 0s. PPE = "Net investments in real estate properties" (SEC RealEstateInvestmentPropertyNet); within the FY22-FY25 window it rises steadily 72.6->80.4B with no spike (the >60% FY21->FY22 jump from the $26B Duke Realty merger is OUTSIDE the window). BALANCE SHEET IS UNCLASSIFIED (no current/non-current split, typical REIT): "current_liabilities" = Accounts payable & accrued expenses (SEC AccountsPayableAndAccruedLiabilitiesCurrentAndNoncurrent) as the current-liability proxy; debt is essentially all long-term. shareholders_equity = TOTAL equity INCLUDING noncontrolling interests (SEC StockholdersEquityIncludingPortionAttributableToNoncontrollingInterest) so the sheet balances (Assets 98,724 = Liab 40,970 + Equity 57,754 for FY25); parent-only stockholders' equity is ~53,193M FY25 and does not change TSA-DSER/RSH outcomes. RETAINED EARNINGS = "Distributions in excess of net earnings" (accumulated deficit) - modest negative (~-458 to -902M) because large realized real-estate gains offset distributions; series is choppy and negative throughout. GOODWILL: PLD reports no goodwill line since 2010 ($32.8M then); real-estate acquisitions are asset acquisitions, not goodwill -> 0s. TREASURY STOCK: none (no XBRL tag) -> absent/0. PREFERRED: $63.9M series-Q preferred outstanding all 4 yrs -> present. R&D: no R&D expense line -> absent. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (op income from stockanalysis + D&A from cash flow; independently reconstructs to the same 4,093/5,569/5,679/6,040). Note an alternative gains-inclusive EBITDA (macrotrends) runs higher (~5,036/5,654/6,431/6,146) but EP-pass, LTD/EBITDA>4, and RSH<23% hold under either definition. Expect a REIT floor: RTA (NOCF/assets ~5%), RE (negative deficit), RSH (~10-11%), PS (preferred), TS (no treasury), GW (no goodwill) all structural zeros; TSA-DSER should score 2/2 (equity-heavy, avg TL/equity ~0.62) like O net-lease.

Sentiment: Constructive/positive as of Jul 2026. Q1 2026 beat (EPS $1.37 vs $0.99 y/y, revenue +16%, NI +34%). 18-analyst Buy consensus (~33% Strong Buy, 50% Buy), avg PT ~$112-115. FY26 EPS est ~$4.86 (called conservative), rev forecast +7.4%/yr, EPS +38% over 3yr. Balance-sheet flexibility improving post-2023 deposit-flight scare; clearer path to capital returns. No red flags to make assignment un-celebratable, but this is a deposit-funded bank/broker whose rubric score is capped by structural float, not business quality.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Charles Schwab = bank/broker-dealer holding company (FY ends Dec 31). No inventory (financial services) -> zeros. 'current_liabilities' is the stockanalysis current-liabilities figure, which for a bank lumps in bank deposits + payables to brokerage clients (settlement/deposit float ~$400B+), so EP compares $12.8B EBITDA vs ~$419B -> expect EP 0/4 and TSA-DSER floored by the deposit-funded balance sheet. Preferred stock outstanding every year ($6.8-9.7B). Treasury stock present (buyback history, FY25 step to $18.4B). Total shareholders' equity includes preferred; kept as reported total equity. Classic financial-float floor case (like IBKR/AXP/banks in the bench) - RTA also weak (NOCF/assets ~2-4%, volatile from bank cash-flow classification: FY23 OCF $19.6B is an outlier). Expect a structural not-a-gift; recording for the audit trail.

Sentiment: FY2025 was a record year: revenue $6.36B, domestic NOI +4.4% / portfolio NOI +4.7%, strong leasing demand and shopper traffic; net income boosted by remeasurement/disposal gains from stepping up to full ownership of Taubman Realty Group (bought remaining 12%) plus Brickell City Centre (~$2B of acquisitions total). Analyst tone on the highest-quality US mall/outlet operator is constructive; dividend well-covered by FFO. Nothing scandalous in the window. The un-celebratable angle is structural, not headline: this is a levered REIT (TL/equity ~4-5x), so under the DCAP rubric assignment is not a 'gift' - the balance-sheet leverage and near-full dividend payout (accumulated deficit) are the point, consistent with the REIT-floor bench.
research notes - methodology & caveats
SPG = Simon Property Group, retail (mall/outlet) REIT, Dec fiscal year-end; FY2025 10-K filed Feb 2026. STRUCTURE: consolidates Simon Property Group L.P. (operating partnership) with large noncontrolling/limited-partner interest; shareholders_equity here is the LITERAL 'Total stockholders' equity' PARENT line per SEC us-gaap:StockholdersEquity (FY22 3138.5 / FY23 3022.8 / FY24 2941.9 / FY25 5208.3); total equity incl NCI runs ~0.5-1.3B higher but does not change any DCAP outcome. SE positive all 4 years -> TSA-DSER NOT excused, max stays 17. INVENTORY: REIT carries no inventory -> 0s (genuine). CURRENT_LIABILITIES: SPG files an UNCLASSIFIED (REIT) balance sheet and does NOT report a 'total current liabilities' subtotal; SEC has no LiabilitiesCurrent tag. Figures shown are stockanalysis.com's reconstruction (accounts payable/accrued + other current) - flagged because EP hinges on it, though EBITDA comfortably exceeds it every year regardless. PPE = SEC us-gaap:RealEstateInvestmentPropertyNet (net investment properties); FY25 jump 21.2B->30.2B (+42.7%, under the 60% spike bar) is the Taubman full-consolidation, not organic capex. LTD = balance-sheet 'mortgages and unsecured indebtedness, net of deferred financing costs' (essentially all debt is long-term/single line); SEC gross DebtInstrumentCarryingAmount ~75-165M higher - LTD scores 0/2 either way (LTD/assets ~0.74, LTD/EBITDA ~6.0). GOODWILL flat at exactly $20.098M all 4 years (SEC us-gaap:Goodwill; stockanalysis mislabels as 'not disclosed'). RE = accumulated deficit, SEC-exact, deepens FY22->FY24 then shrinks FY25 on Taubman remeasurement gains. Classic REIT profile: passes EP/PPE/TS/RSH, floors on LTD/TSA-DSER/preferred/RE/goodwill - expect a non-gift REIT-floor score around mid-30s%.

Sentiment: Sentiment is speculative/high-beta, not celebrate-the-assignment quality. Entire thesis rides on GTA VI, now dated Nov 19 2026 at a premium $80 base price (announced Jun 2026); pre-orders mid-May 2026. FY2027 net-bookings guide $8.0-8.2B; bulls (BofA Buy, PT $320) model $8.8B rev / $1.1B earnings by 2028 - i.e. the current price already discounts a flawless launch. Balance sheet is post-Zynga-impairment scar tissue: -$7.36B accumulated deficit, EBITDA negative in 2 of last 4 years, goodwill written down ~85% from peak. Assignment here means owning a pre-launch, unprofitable, execution-and-delay-risk story, not a durable-moat compounder.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Fiscal year ends March 31; used latest 4 (FY2023-FY2026, FY2026 10-K filed ~May 2026). Video-game publisher (GTA, NBA 2K, Zynga) - carries no inventory line (digital/services); 0s reflect genuine absence, not missing data. PP&E is SEC XBRL PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet ($402.8/411.1/443.8/445.4M), which EXCLUDES operating-lease ROU assets - stockanalysis inflates PP&E to ~$685-770M by bundling ROU; used SEC ex-ROU per DCAP convention. Balance sheet dominated by the May-2022 Zynga acquisition (~$12.7B): goodwill spiked to $6.8B (FY23) then was impaired down to ~$1.06B (FY24 -$2,342M and FY25 -$3,545M goodwill-impairment charges per SEC), gutting EBITDA and driving retained earnings from +$1.16B to a -$7.36B accumulated deficit. EBITDA = operating income + full D&A (incl. intangible amortization) per stockanalysis; deeply negative FY24/FY25 because the goodwill-impairment charges sit inside operating loss and are NOT added back in standard EBITDA. FY2026 recovered (OCF +$624M, EBITDA +$1.2B) ahead of the GTA VI cycle. Shareholders' equity positive all 4 years, so TSA-DSER applies (max 17). HUMAN CALL: this is a merger-wreck + impairment window (cf. BG/Viterra, OMC/IPG, STZ) - normalized/adjusted EBITDA adding back goodwill impairment would be ~+$0.6B in FY24/FY25, but even normalized the RE deficit, EP, RTA and GW-collapse still fail; not a gift on any reading.

Sentiment: Bullish operating momentum, weak balance-sheet structure. Q1-2026 strong: data-center revenue +47% to $255M, 2026 guidance raised to ~$7.9B revenue / ~$2.95B adj-EBITDA (~14% growth); analysts lifting targets (JPMorgan $138 OW, Truist $140 Buy, Jefferies Buy $120). 4th straight year of dividend growth (+10% Jan-2026). But this is a highly-levered, negative-equity REIT priced for growth - assignment hands you a levered data-center/storage REIT at a rich multiple, not a Buffett-style fortress balance sheet. DCAP penalizes the structure regardless of the growth story.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Iron Mountain = records-management/storage + fast-growing data-center REIT. (1) INVENTORY genuinely 0 - services business, no inventory line on the balance sheet. (2) EBITDA = operating income + income-statement D&A, all from SEC 10-Ks: FY22 1049.9+727.6; FY23 921.8+776.2; FY24 1009.5+900.9; FY25 1163.8+1024.4. FY23 dips below FY22 so the series is NOT a steady rise. (3) PP&E is NET PP&E straight from SEC 10-K balance sheets; aggregators (stockanalysis) inflate it by ~2-3B via operating-lease ROU assets - do NOT use those (SEC net PP&E FY22 5115, FY23 6315, FY24 7632, FY25 9546 vs aggregator 7699/9011/10122/12012). Rising is real data-center buildout capex. (4) SHAREHOLDERS_EQUITY = parent 'Total Iron Mountain Incorporated Stockholders (Deficit) Equity'; total equity incl NCI would be -304.7 (FY24)/-709.3 (FY25). Equity turned NEGATIVE FY24-FY25 as a REIT that pays distributions in excess of earnings - TSA-DSER should be excused (max 15). (5) RETAINED_EARNINGS = 'Distributions in excess of earnings' - deeply negative and worsening every year (structural REIT payout), so RE growth is 0/3. (6) NO treasury-stock line and NO preferred in the equity section (common stock + APIC + distributions-in-excess + AOCI only). (7) LTD/assets ~65-77% and LTD/EBITDA ~7.4x - heavily levered. Anticipate REIT-floor score in the 5/15 range (EP fails FY22, RTA ~6%, LTD 0/2, RE 0/3, TS 0), akin to the AMT REIT floor.

Sentiment: Tone has turned constructive after a rough 18 months: activist-driven CEO change (Menezes, early 2025), then large write-offs exiting speculative green-hydrogen projects (FY2025 impairments; $2.9B Louisiana Clean Energy Complex exit in 2026 - stock jumped 8% on the news). Core industrial-gas business is solid: FQ2-2026 EPS $3.20, 9% revenue growth, 200bps margin expansion, FY26 adj-EPS guidance raised to +8-10%, ~2.5% dividend yield (60+ yr dividend-growth record). Shares +13.5% in the past month; some analysts flag ~34% overvaluation. Watch FQ3 earnings late July 2026 for final LCEC charge size / further exits. Assignment celebratable: dominant top-3 industrial-gas oligopoly player with long-term take-or-pay contracts - but GAAP FY2024-25 numbers are distorted by one-off gains/charges, which will depress the scored EBITDA-based parameters.
research notes - methodology & caveats
$ millions; fiscal years FY2022→FY2025, FYE Sept 30. All figures from SEC EDGAR XBRL (10-K tagged facts). EBITDA = GAAP operating income + D&A: FY2024 includes ~$1.6B gain on LNG business sale to Honeywell; FY2025 operating income is -$877.0M due to ~$3.1B of impairment/project-exit charges (green-hydrogen retrenchment under new CEO Eduardo Menezes), so FY2025 EBITDA of $687.2M is charge-depressed, not an operating collapse (FY2025 NOCF was $3,256.8M). long_term_debt = noncurrent LTD incl. finance leases and related-party (Jazan JV) debt = LongTermDebtAndCapitalLeaseObligationsIncludingCurrentMaturities minus current portion (recent 10-Ks split the balance-sheet line, so noncurrent was derived; matches stockanalysis.com exactly). shareholders_equity is parent stockholders' equity excluding noncontrolling interests (total equity incl. NCI: 13,702/15,660/18,674/17,350). NOCF is continuing operations (the only operating-cash-flow line APD tags). PPE is the 10-K net PP&E line (excludes lease ROU assets). R&D expense line present ($96-106M/yr); no preferred stock outstanding; treasury stock ~$2.0B all four years. Retained earnings dip in FY2025 reflects the charges + dividends.

Sentiment: Consensus Buy (7 analysts, ~29% Strong Buy), PT nudged to ~$150; stock ~$111, up ~55% off its $71.60 July-2025 low. FY2025 GAAP net income $816M (down from $1,137M) and adj EPS $7.57 vs $9.19, but Viterra synergies drove adj segment EBIT up to $2,459M and management raised FY2026 adj-EPS guidance to $9.00-$9.50. Headwinds: global soybean oversupply compressing crush margins, geopolitical/regulatory risk, and integration execution risk (a special 2026-2028 exec incentive is tied to Viterra cost-synergy targets). Assignment thesis is cyclical-commodity, not durable-moat: celebratable only as a discounted-entry cyclical, not a Buffett-grade compounder.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Bunge Global SA (agribusiness: grain/oilseed origination, processing, refined oils, milling). Dec FY-end. EBITDA constructed = GAAP operating income + D&A each year (FY22 2313+408; FY23 3130+451; FY24 1617+468; FY25 1296+703), matching stockanalysis EBITDA. MAJOR CORPORATE ACTION IN WINDOW: the ~$34B Viterra merger closed mid-2025, so FY2025 is a step-change year - total assets, inventory, PP&E all ~2x, goodwill jumps $453M->$3,141M from acquisition. This mechanically breaks the "steady rise / no spike" tests on inventory, PP&E and goodwill even though the underlying business grew. FY2022 net operating cash flow was deeply negative (-$5,549M) on commodity-price/working-capital swings (margin calls), which sinks the RTA average. Classic low-margin agri-commodity working-capital structure (like ADM/ADM cyclical): EBITDA sits well below current liabilities every year, so EP fails structurally. Carries meaningful inventory (commodity stocks), so 0s would be wrong. No R&D expense line on the income statement. Preferred stock: none outstanding (all preference shares auto-converted to common in March 2022). Treasury stock present every year (buyback history; $1.0B latest). Expect a low, structural, not-a-gift score similar to other ag-commodity names; re-check after the FY2026 10-K when the Viterra step-change is inside the base and synergies mature.

Sentiment: Constructive/bullish (mid-2026). HSBC upgraded to Buy on 2026-07-06 on improved HIV outlook; consensus "Buy", avg 12mo PT ~$158 (~20% upside), Morgan Stanley $175 / Truist $155. Momentum drivers: Yeztugo/lenacapavir HIV-prevention franchise (once-weekly oral sSD accepted, Feb-2027 PDUFA) and Trodelvy first-line TNBC approvals (FDA 2026-06-25, EU). Q1-2026 beat: rev $7.0B (+4%), EPS $2.03 vs $1.61 est. 2026 guide ~$30B revenue, EPS ~$8.65. Assignment would be celebrated on business quality - but this is not a rubric drag driver; the FY2024 IPR&D-driven earnings collapse + rebate-heavy current liabilities + flat goodwill + net leverage keep the DCAP score structurally low.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Gilead Sciences, biopharma, FY ends Dec 31. All balance-sheet items from SEC 10-K consolidated balance sheets (FY2025 10-K covers FY2025+FY2024; FY2023 10-K covers FY2023+FY2022) - exact. EBITDA built as operating income + cash-flow D&A: OI 7330/7605/1662/10022 + D&A 2103/2693/2767/2760 = 9433/10298/4429/12782 (matches stockanalysis EBITDA). FY2024 EBITDA collapse is real: operating income cratered to $1,662M on ~$3.9B CymaBay acquired-IPR&D charge + Trodelvy IPR&D impairment (R&D line spiked to $14,750M) - wrecks I&NE and EP. shareholders_equity = parent-only 'Total Gilead stockholders equity' (NCI is -84, negligible; SE positive all 4 yrs). NO treasury stock line in the 10-K equity section - Gilead retires repurchased shares (buybacks fund via additional paid-in capital), so treasury_stock_abs=0 and TS flag 'absent' (aggregators mislabel a nominal ~$8M line). Preferred: authorized but none outstanding → absent. Goodwill flat $8,314M all 4 yrs (no increase → GW 0). Inventories real (drug product). Total liabilities = assets - total equity(incl NCI); ties to stockanalysis exactly (41962/39376/39749/36405).

Sentiment: Consensus Hold. FY2025 hammered by ~$7.6B EV special charges (pullback on all-electric) plus China share erosion vs domestic EV makers; operating income fell to $2.9B. Into 2026 GM raised adjusted-earnings guidance ($13.5-15.5B) as EV losses shrink and after a Supreme Court tariff-rebate decision, but still guides $2.5-3.5B gross tariff cost and faces intense China pressure. Muted near-term upside; not obviously a business we would be delighted to be assigned into on fundamentals, and the score reflects that.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Automaker with a large captive-finance arm (GM Financial) that inflates the balance sheet, the classic PCAR/CAT-style floor. TRAPS AVOIDED: (1) PP&E uses core 'Property, net' from SEC XBRL (PropertyPlantAndEquipmentExcludingLessorAssetUnderOperatingLease...) = 45,248/50,321/51,904/51,683; aggregators (stockanalysis) fold in GM Financial's 'Equipment on operating leases, net' (~33B, itself a finance-arm earning asset) and report PP&E ~78-85B. (2) shareholders_equity is PARENT-only (SEC us-gaap:StockholdersEquity) = 67,792/64,286/63,072/61,119; aggregators report TOTAL equity incl. noncontrolling interest (NCI 4,135/3,903/2,518/2,049) as 71,927/68,189/65,590/63,168 (NCI trap). (3) goodwill is true us-gaap:Goodwill = flat 1,900 all 4 yrs; aggregators bundle intangibles and show ~4,900 declining. Either way GW does not increase. EBITDA = operating income + total D&A (SEC OI 10,315/9,298/12,784/2,909 + cash-flow D&A incl. leased-equipment); cross-checks macrotrends & stockanalysis exactly. FY2025 EBITDA/operating-income collapse is EV special charges (~$7.6B FY2025 + $1.1B). long_term_debt = non-current 'Long-term debt' line (stockanalysis); SEC total debt incl. current maturities is 116,217/123,494/131,758/131,574. No treasury stock line and no preferred outstanding (SEC has no TreasuryStockValue / PreferredStockValue) - GM retires repurchased shares against APIC/RE rather than holding treasury. No R&D expense line broken out on the income statement. FY ends December.

Sentiment: Near 52-week high - $240 (high $243.57 set 2026-07-02), up from $174.76 Oct-2025 low. Strong operating momentum: Q1 2026 adjusted EPS +23.7% y/y to $4.18, NII $1.8B (+3.4%); Q2 2026 consensus ~$4.67 EPS. BUT stock now trades ABOVE analyst targets: consensus Hold (16-26 analysts), avg 12-mo PT ~$222-231 vs $240 spot; Baird cut MTB to Neutral (PT $240) on 2026-07-06 on valuation. Solid dividend (2.5% yield, $1.50/qtr), well-capitalized, but revenue-growth/cost-pressure and competitive-deposit concerns cited. Assignment ~$240 would be buying a fairly-to-richly-valued regional bank at multi-year highs - not obviously celebratable, and DCAP score (bank floor) says it's not a gift.
research notes - methodology & caveats
M&T Bank Corp - regional bank holding company (Buffalo NY), calendar fiscal year. BANK-SPECIFIC RESEARCH JUDGMENTS: (1) inventory=0 all years - a bank carries no inventory. (2) EBITDA is not disclosed for banks; constructed as pretax income + D&A (I do NOT add back interest - net interest income is a bank's core operating revenue, so adding back interest expense would be nonsensical). Pretax income (stockanalysis): 2612/3619/3310/3692; D&A (cash-flow stmt): 435/497/508/498 → EBITDA 3047/4116/3818/4190. (3) current_liabilities = total year-end DEPOSITS (a bank's balance sheet is unclassified with no current/non-current split; deposits are the demand/short-term obligation analog). Deposits: FY22 $163.5B, FY23 $163.274B, FY24 $165.4B, FY25 $166.9B (from 10-Ks/press releases). EP structurally fails 4x - no bank can cover its deposit base from a year of earnings. (4) long_term_debt = borrowings (grew FY23-24 on FHLB/senior notes, partially repaid FY25). (5) shareholders_equity is TOTAL equity incl. ~$2.0-2.8B preferred. Expected floor pattern like lender/insurer-float names: EP/RTA/TSA-DSER/RSH fail on deposit leverage + low NOCF/assets (~1.4-2.3%); PS present; goodwill flat at $8.465B (People's United, FY22 tiny true-up) so GW not increasing. Genuine positives: LTD/assets ~0.04 and LTD/EBITDA ~2.6 (2/2), RE growth ~9.8%/yr (1/3), treasury stock present. Rescore after FY2026 10-K.

Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 5.7% on equity - needs ≥23%

Sentiment: Analysts bullish post-merger: consensus Buy, avg 12-mo PT ~$103 (~30-40% upside), upgrades Apr 2026. IPG integration delivering - Q1'26 adjusted EBITDA margin 14.8% (+240bps), $900M cost synergies targeted 2026 rising to $1.5B by mid-2028, plus $2.5B non-strategic asset sales; combined Omnicom Media now world's largest media network ($75.6B billings, 15.8% share). BUT GAAP FY2025 was wrecked by merger + repositioning charges and the balance sheet is materially more leveraged post-deal. Assignment today would put us in a leveraged, mid-integration ad-holdco whose durable-moat metrics are structurally weak (EP/RTA/TSA-DSER/RSH all floor) - not celebratable yet; the thesis rides entirely on synergy execution.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Advertising/marketing-services holding co (BBDO/DDB/TBWA + IPG agencies), FY ends Dec 31; window = last 4 completed FYs. CRITICAL merger-year distortion: FY2025 consolidates the Interpublic Group (IPG) all-stock merger that closed Nov 26, 2025 - total assets $29.6B->$54.4B, goodwill $10.7B->$18.6B, current & total liabilities balloon, and FY2025 EBITDA collapsed to $721M (op income $444.7M, down $1.8B YoY) on IPG acquisition + repositioning/integration costs. That one merger year zeros I&NE (EBITDA not rising), drags RTA/RSH, and flips RE negative (-6.2% FY25). 'Inventory' = billable work-in-process (media/production costs), NOT product inventory - ad-services carries no true inventory; FY2025 step-up is IPG consolidation (I&NE point 1 fails on EBITDA regardless). Total liabilities computed from SEC identity (Assets − total equity incl NCI − redeemable NCI 382.9/414.6/429.0/363.2 per SEC XBRL); stockanalysis's total-liab column was corrupted (its FY24 17,105 is below current-liab+LTD, impossible). Structural ad-services floor even pre-merger: media-vendor payables floor EP (EBITDA << current liabilities all 4 yrs), asset-light low NOCF floors RTA (~5%), TSA-DSER ~2.2 (payable+debt leverage) fails both tiers, RSH 5.7%. LTD FY2022 dip (900) = maturing notes reclassed to current maturities. Pre-merger ceiling (FY2021-24) ~7-8/17 (RE would earn 1/3 at ~8.5%); merger costs ~3 pts. Re-score after clean post-merger years (FY2026-2027) once $900M/2026 -> $1.5B/2028 synergies normalize EBITDA. SE positive all years -> TSA-DSER applies, max 17.

Sentiment: Mixed-to-cautiously-positive. Consensus 'Moderate Buy' (7 Strong Buy / 12 Hold of 19 analysts), 12-mo target ~$126 (+6%). FY2025 was the trough (EPS $5.01 adj); FY2026 EPS seen +11.6% to ~$5.59 as US/Canada Class 8 market inflects positive (230-270k units guided) on higher freight rates. 87 consecutive profitable years; Kenworth/Peterbilt/DAF franchise quality is not in question - the drag is cyclical truck demand + the captive-finance balance sheet, not competitive erosion. Assignment would be owning a dominant truck OEM near a cycle bottom - tolerable, but the DCAP structural score is what it is. Near-term watch: Q2 earnings July 28 (EPS est $1.32, -3.7% y/y).
research notes - methodology & caveats
Calendar FY (Dec 31). PACCAR runs a captive-finance arm (PACCAR Financial Services, $22.8B assets) - balance sheet is only partially classified: current_liabilities is stockanalysis's derivation (Truck current liabilities + FS payables + FS commercial paper/bank loans; reconciles exactly to 10-K components for FY24/FY25). LTD = FS term notes (Truck segment carries essentially no LT debt). PPE = the balance-sheet 'Property, plant and equipment, net' line (Truck) per 10-K/XBRL; stockanalysis shows ~$6.3B because it folds in FS equipment-on-operating-leases - DCAP uses the filing line. EBITDA = stockanalysis figures (operating income + D&A); my independent EDGAR build (pretax income + total cash-flow D&A: 4,639/6,642/6,318/3,851) agrees within ~8%, definitional only - no DCAP parameter flips between the two series. FY2025 is a cyclical trough: truck deliveries down ~33% in N. America, tariff headwinds, plus a $264.5M after-tax EU civil-litigation charge; net income $2,375.8M vs $4,162.0M (FY24). R&D: present ($445.5M FY25) - genuine engine/tech R&D, spirit-of-Buffett reading same as flag. Treasury stock: flag 'absent' but MSI-pattern caveat - PACCAR repurchased and RETIRED 0.4M shares in 2025 ($128.4M cumulative under the 2018 $500M authorization), nil 2023-24; buyback history exists but no treasury line is carried, so TS point reads 0. Preferred: 1.0M shares authorized, NONE issued. Expect the captive-finance structure to drag TSA-DSER/EP/RTA the same way insurer float does (cf. PGR, CME). Q2 earnings July 28, 2026.

Sentiment: Staples name wrecked by the Nov-2023 $5.6B Hostess acquisition: two straight GAAP net losses (FY2025 -$1.23B, FY2026 -$138.7M) driven by $2.41B cumulative goodwill impairments plus large trademark impairments on the Sweet Baked Snacks / Hostess unit, which continues to contract and lose share. Retained income flipped to an accumulated deficit at FY2026. Shareholder-investigation notices (e.g., Hagens Berman) over the Hostess writedowns. Despite this, Street tone is constructive: ~21 analysts average 'Buy', ~$122 12-mo target (~6% upside), on an FY2026 beat and FY2027 guide for higher EPS/profitability (coffee deflation lowers net sales) with debt-reduction focus; dividend yield ~3.9%. Assignment here would mean owning a serial-impairment turnaround story, not a celebration.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends April 30; latest 4 FYs are FY2023-FY2026 (FY2026 10-K filed June 2026). All figures from SEC EDGAR XBRL R-pages (FY2026 10-K for FY2024-26, FY2024 10-K for FY2023). EBITDA = operating income + depreciation + amortization, both D&A components taken from the cash-flow statement (income-statement 'Amortization' line is intangibles-only; full D&A = Dep+Amort): FY23 157.5+224.1+206.9=588.5; FY24 1305.8+239.7+191.1=1736.6; FY25 -673.9+283.2+219.3=-171.4 (negative on $1.66B goodwill+intangible impairments); FY26 360.2+346.4+210.6=917.2 (still burdened by $507.5M goodwill + $454.2M intangible impairments). PP&E is net PP&E EXCLUDING operating-lease ROU assets (reported separately: 103.0/174.6/115.4/148.8), so no ROU trap. Treasury: Smucker nets 43.8M treasury shares against common at stated value with NO separate treasury-stock dollar line, so treasury_stock_abs=0, but flag=present (active buybacks each year: $367.5M/$372.8M/$3.3M/$5.6M). Preferred authorized 6M shares, none outstanding. No R&D expense line on the income statement. Retained income turns to an accumulated deficit (-101.3) at FY2026. LTD = noncurrent long-term debt only (excludes current portion 0/999.3/0/150.0). stockanalysis.com PP&E (3195/3247) is inflated vs SEC net PP&E; SEC used throughout.

DCAP 5/17 (29%)
gift at ≥60%

Sentiment: Business momentum is very strong and Street sentiment is bullish (avg rating Buy/Strong Buy, ~$234 PT, +23% upside): landmark media-rights wins (WWE Raw to Netflix ~$5B; UFC to Paramount+/CBS, ~2x prior value; Zuffa Boxing), 2026 revenue guide $5.68-5.78B, high-margin monetization. That momentum is about the OPERATING business, not the DCAP balance-sheet fortress test. For our 'assignment is a gift' lens the quality signal is weak/structurally hard to celebrate: Up-C with a deepening accumulated deficit, two merger step-changes inside the 4-yr window (WWE 2023, IMG/On Location/PBR common-control recast 2025), goodwill-heavy asset base ($8.4B GW), no buyback-to-treasury, and net debt rising (LTD $2.7B->$3.7B in FY25). Not a balance-sheet compounder; the appeal here is growth/media-rights, which the DCAP rubric deliberately does not reward.
research notes - methodology & caveats
TKO Group Holdings (NYSE:TKO) = UFC + WWE, formed 2023-09-12. Up-C structure: parent (Class A) equity is only ~$3.7-4.1B; the shareholders_equity series uses TOTAL equity INCLUDING the large Endeavor noncontrolling interest (the balance-sheet 'Total equity' line: FY22 $568.9M / FY23 $8,839M / FY24 $8,697M / FY25 $9,216M). Parent-only equity would be far thinner (FY24 $4,091M / FY25 $3,737M), making leverage ratios worse. NO INVENTORY (live events + media rights/licensing = services) -> inventory 0s. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (rubric method). BASIS WARNING (two step-changes bracket the window): (1) FY2022 is UFC/Zuffa PREDECESSOR-only (WWE merged in Sept 2023, UFC the accounting acquirer) -> the FY22->FY23 jump in assets ($3.6B->$12.7B), PP&E (+247%, a >60% spike), goodwill ($2.6B->$7.7B), total equity and current liab is the WWE merger, not organic growth. FY2022 retained_earnings = null: UFC predecessor was an LLC (members' equity, no retained-earnings line). (2) FY2022-FY2024 use AS-ORIGINALLY-REPORTED figures (TKO standalone); FY2025 is on the RECAST basis because the Jan/Feb-2025 acquisition of IMG, On Location & PBR from Endeavor was a COMMON-CONTROL transaction recast retrospectively -> the FY2025 10-K restated FY2024 UP (assets $12.70B->$15.11B, curr liab $671M->$1,414M, goodwill $7.66B->$8.44B, EBITDA down to ~$489M). I did NOT use those restated FY24 numbers, to keep FY22-24 on one clean as-filed basis; consequently the FY24->FY25 deltas conflate organic growth + the IMG/OL/PBR add-back. stockanalysis.com mixes the two bases (FY23 original + FY24 restated) and should not be trusted for a consistent series. Deepening accumulated deficit (-$135M->-$292M->-$797M) is real (Up-C tax-receivable + buyback + distributions), locking RE 0/3. No treasury stock / no buyback-to-treasury history and no preferred. EP: FY25 current liabilities ($1,847M) exceed EBITDA ($1,320M) so EP fails on the latest year regardless of basis.

Sentiment: Strongly positive and improving: Amvuttra (vutrisiran) ATTR-CM approval (Mar 2025) drove TTR franchise +150% YoY in Q1 2026; FY2025 revenue $3.71B (+65%) with first full-year GAAP profit; 2026 TTR revenue guidance raised to $4.4-4.7B; beat-and-raise twice in 2025. Pipeline catalysts H2 2026 (bleeding disorders, Huntington's, obesity). Assignment would be owning the RNAi platform leader with a dominant, patent-protected franchise - operationally celebratable, but the trailing 4-year financials still carry the pre-inflection loss years.
research notes - methodology & caveats
FY ends Dec 31. EBITDA = operating income + D&A (both from SEC XBRL: OperatingIncomeLoss −785.07/−282.18/−176.89/+501.58; DepreciationAmortizationAndAccretionNet 44.47/54.05/56.67/55.66). Shareholders' equity NEGATIVE in FY2022 and FY2023 (−158.2, −220.6) - expect TSA-DSER excusal (15-pt max). Retained earnings is an accumulated deficit (approx −$6.7B latest); deficit deepened through FY2024 then shrank in FY2025 (first profitable year). Long-term debt is the carrying value of the 1% convertible senior notes due 2027 (~$1.0B); total liabilities additionally include a Blackstone sale-of-future-royalties liability and deferred items, so LTD understates total leverage vs total liabilities. No goodwill line on the balance sheet (no us-gaap Goodwill concept in EDGAR XBRL) - genuinely zero, not missing. No treasury stock line (ALNY has issued equity historically, never bought back). Preferred stock authorized but none outstanding. R&D expense very much present (~$1.32B FY2025) - pure-play RNAi biotech. DCAP window straddles the loss-to-profit inflection: FY2022-FY2024 losses drive most zeros; a rescore after the FY2026 10-K (early 2027) drops the worst year (FY2022) and should look materially better.

DCAP 4/17 (24%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 7.5% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)0/2avg debt/assets 0.53 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 4.9 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 4.41 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -36.6%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%1/1latest EBITDA returned 66.5% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Positive/constructive Street tone (Jul 2026): Buy consensus across 25 analysts, ~$217 avg 12-mo target (~30% above price); Truist ($208) and Raymond James ($240, Strong Buy) raised targets after Q1 beat. CoreSite data-center unit +17% cash revenue on AI/hybrid-cloud demand is the bull narrative; AMT raised 2026 guidance (~4% organic tenant billings, double-digit data-center growth) and hiked the dividend 5% to $1.79/qtr in Mar 2026. 2026 seen as a transition year with positive AFFO/share inflection expected 2027+. Nothing here makes assignment un-celebratable as a business; the DCAP miss is purely balance-sheet structure (leverage + REIT payout), not operational distress.
research notes - methodology & caveats
American Tower (AMT) is an equity REIT owning ~150k communication sites + CoreSite data centers; FY ends Dec 31; latest is FY2025 10-K (filed Feb 2026). No inventory line (real-property REIT) - modeled as 0 across all years, so I&NE 'both series rise' cannot award (EBITDA rises cleanly 6094->6887 but inventory is flat 0). EBITDA = operating income + D&A (GAAP-derived, consistent all 4 years); this differs from AMT's non-GAAP 'Adjusted EBITDA' (~$7,130M FY2025) which is higher - used the consistent GAAP-derived figure. Retained earnings is a GROWING accumulated deficit (-2102 -> -5086), structural for a REIT that distributes ~all taxable income as dividends; RE-growth (3 pts) and EP will fail on this + heavy leverage. SE positive all 4 years so TSA-DSER applies (17-pt max), but TL/(SE+treasury) ~4.4x avg and LTD/EBITDA ~4.9x, so LTD and TSA-DSER both structural zeros - classic REIT capital structure. Preferred redeemed years ago (none outstanding). Expect a low, structural REIT score, not a gift; consistent with prior REIT-ceiling findings.

Consumer Non-Durables · price $36.12 · mcap $19.3B · vol 11,543,945 · PE -190.7 · PB 2.7 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 4/17 (24%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab

Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 8.8% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)1/2avg debt/assets 0.37 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 8.6 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 1.13 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding1/1no preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew 1.3%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present1/1buyback history on the books (treasury stock present)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 19.5% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years0/1goodwill not increasing (or unknown)
Sentiment: Weak. FY2026 (reported 2026-07-01) was a down year: net sales -5% to $18.4B, organic -2%, operating profit -73% to $885.8M, a diluted LOSS of $(0.16)/sh driven by $1.75B non-cash Pet (Blue Buffalo) goodwill/intangible impairment + $1.0B Brazil divestiture valuation loss. Analysts at Hold consensus, avg PT ~$41; FY2027 guidance implies further profit/EPS declines (lapping a 53rd week and divestitures) even with a targeted $750M+ cost-savings program. Portfolio in heavy reshaping (nearly a third turned over since FY2018 via M&A/divestitures). Assignment would NOT be celebratable right now: shrinking, impaired, levered staples turnaround, not a durable-advantage compounder.
research notes - methodology & caveats
General Mills; FY ends last Sunday of May (FY2026 = 5/31/2026). Window is the latest 4 10-Ks; the FY2026 10-K was filed 2026-07-01, so I used FY2023-FY2026 (not FY2022-FY2025). All figures from SEC XBRL / 10-Ks in USD millions. EBITDA = operating income + cash-flow D&A (textbook, unadjusted). MATERIAL FY2026 CAVEAT: operating profit collapsed to 885.8 (from 3304.8) on $1.75B non-cash goodwill+intangible impairment of the North America Pet business (Blue Buffalo) plus a $1.0B Brazil divestiture valuation loss; this drags FY2026 EBITDA to 1441 and pushes retained earnings down (21917.8 -> 20514.9). Adding the impairment back (adjusted EBITDA ~3191) would not change the verdict; the score is robustly not-a-gift either way (staples working-capital + leverage floor, same as MKC/CL/CLX/HSY). shareholders_equity = TOTAL stockholders' equity INCLUDING NCI (NCI is small, ~12-250M; parent-only SE would be trivially higher and does not flip RSH/TSA-DSER). SE positive all 4 years -> TSA-DSER not excused, max 17. LTD = long-term debt incl. finance leases (us-gaap:LongTermDebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations), matches aggregator LTD. No R&D line on the income statement (R&D embedded in SG&A) -> rnd absent gives the I&NE no-R&D point. No preferred outstanding. Treasury stock large and growing (buyback history). Structural not-a-gift: EP fails 4x (current liabilities > EBITDA every year), RTA ~8.8% avg (<11%), TSA-DSER ~1.13 avg leverage (>1.0), RE avg growth ~1.3% wrecked by FY2026 decline, goodwill declines in FY2026 (impairment), RSH ~19.5% (<23%). Balance sheets reconcile (TL + SE = Assets each year).

ARES - Ares Management Corp not-a-gift

Finance - alternative asset management · price $121.80 · mcap $40.2B · vol 2,600,176 · PE 54.1 · PB 10.3 · ✅ stage-1
DCAP 2/17 (12%)
gift at ≥60%
ParameterPointsEvidence
Inventory & net earningsI&NE · both on a steady 4-yr rise (+1); no R&D expense line (+1)1/2inventory & EBITDA did not both rise steadily; no R&D expense line - the moat isn't bought in the lab
Earning powerEP · EBITDA exceeds current liabilities in each of the 4 years0/1EBITDA did not cover current liabilities every year (or the data is incomplete)
Plant & equipmentPPE · steady rise with no acquisition-sized spikes (>60% YoY)0/1not a steady, spike-free rise (a >60% YoY jump, a decline, or missing data)
Return on total assetsRTA · avg operating cash flow ÷ assets: ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)0/2avg cash return on assets 4.6% - thresholds ≥11% (+1), ≥17% (+2)
Long-term debtLTD · avg debt ÷ assets ≤0.5 (+1); latest debt ≤4× EBITDA (+1)0/2avg debt/assets 0.55 (cap 0.50); latest debt ≈ 11.9 years of EBITDA (cap 4)
Adjusted debt-to-equityTSA-DSER · avg liabilities ÷ (equity + |treasury|): ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+1); excused if equity went negative0/2avg treasury-adjusted debt-to-equity 3.28 - thresholds ≤1.0 (+1), ≤0.8 (+2)
Preferred stockPS · none outstanding0/1preferred stock outstanding
Retained-earnings growthRE · avg growth ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)0/3retained earnings grew -58.9%/yr on average - thresholds ≥7% (+1), ≥13.5% (+2), ≥17% (+3)
Treasury stockTS · buyback history present0/1no treasury stock found (or unknown)
Return on equityRSH · latest EBITDA ÷ equity ≥23%0/1latest EBITDA returned 13.1% on equity - needs ≥23%
GoodwillGW · increasing across the 4 years1/1goodwill increased across the window (steady acquirer of moats)
Sentiment: Constructive franchise, volatile sector sentiment. Ares ended 2025 with record $623B AUM (+29% YoY), FPAUM +32%, raised $100B+ in 2025, and management fees topped $1B/quarter for the first time in Q1 2026 (+22%). Analyst tone positive: ~4 buys / 0 sells, median PT ~$155. Overhang: the private-credit industry is under negative publicity from high-profile defaults/bankruptcies, and ARES shares have periodically slid on "private-credit jitters" alongside peers. RBC (Bart Dziarski): results "highlight the strength of its franchise" despite negative sentiment. Assignment thesis is mixed - durable fee franchise but leveraged, structurally complex balance sheet and a sector under a credit-cycle cloud.
research notes - methodology & caveats
Ares Management Corp (ARES) - alternative asset manager, FY ends Dec. No inventory (asset manager) - inventory series set to 0s. STRUCTURAL CAVEATS for scorer/operator: (1) Balance sheet is inflated by Consolidated Funds (VIEs) and CLO/fund obligations that Ares consolidates but that are non-recourse - total assets $28.6B and total liabilities $19.9B overstate the operating balance sheet, distorting EP/RTA/LTD/TSA-DSER just like insurer float and CME collateral. (2) NOCF is deeply negative FY2022 (-734) and FY2023 (-233) because consolidated-fund investment activity is classified as operating cash flow; it swung strongly positive FY2024 (+2791) / FY2025 (+3267) - so RTA on NOCF/assets is meaningless here and averages low. (3) Retained earnings is a GROWING accumulated deficit (-369 -> -1452) driven by the Up-C structure and distributions exceeding GAAP net income attributable to ARES - RE parameter (3 pts) will correctly score 0; not a sign of operating weakness. (4) shareholders_equity uses TOTAL equity as reported by the aggregator, which INCLUDES large non-controlling interests (Ares Operating Group units + consolidated funds); controlling-interest Class A equity is materially smaller - flag for operator before trusting TSA-DSER/RSH. (5) Preferred stock (Series B mandatory convertible, ~$1,459M) present FY2024-25 -> PS scores 0. (6) No treasury stock line -> TS scores 0 (Ares does not carry treasury shares). (7) Goodwill jump FY2025 (1163 -> 3454) is the GCP International acquisition (closed Mar 2025). EBIT cross-checked vs macrotrends (FY2024 946, FY2025 893); EBITDA = EBIT + D&A per stockanalysis; retained earnings cross-checked against SEC XBRL (exact match). Expect a low DCAP consistent with the asset-manager/financial-float pattern (cf. CME 7/17, insurers) - likely NOT a gift.

*IVR is a realized-vol-rank proxy (broker exposes no IV history) - see SCREENS.md.

Paper wheel book

The track record under construction - every decision the gate accepted, with the Stage-4 exit rule that closed it.
#SymbolKindStrikeExpiryPrem/shCapital $StatusP&L $Exit reason
No paper positions yet - by operator directive, the book opens after Mike & Jonny review this dashboard together.

What the numbers mean

DCAP

Durable Competitive Advantage Points - the canonical 11-parameter scorer (Jimenez & Vuolo Technical Plan) over 4 fiscal years of filings: inventory & earnings trend, earning power, PP&E, return on assets, debt load, adjusted debt-to-equity, preferred stock, retained-earnings growth, buybacks, return on equity, goodwill. 17 points max (15 when equity history excuses one test).

Gift

A name scoring ≥60% of its applicable DCAP max. The thesis: sell puts only where being forced to buy 100 shares at the strike is a purchase we'd celebrate - then assignment is not a loss.

Cash-secured put (CSP)

Sell a put and hold the full strike×100 in cash. Outcomes: the put expires worthless (keep the premium) or we're assigned the shares at an effective discount (strike − premium) and the wheel continues with covered calls.

Efficiency

(premium ÷ strike×100) ÷ days to expiration - daily yield per dollar of capital at risk, shown annualized (×365). Normalizing by capital AND time makes every candidate comparable; faster capital recycling compounds harder than bigger single premiums.

Delta (Δ)

The put's price sensitivity; ~rough odds of finishing in the money. We cap |Δ| at 0.30 so we're paid to wait, not coin-flipping into assignment.

IV Rank (IVR*)

Where today's volatility sits vs the stock's own trailing year, 0-100. Higher = richer premium for the same risk. *v1 uses a realized-volatility rank proxy - the broker exposes no implied-volatility history.

Premium yield

Premium ÷ (strike × 100). The screen requires >1.5% so the trade pays enough to be worth securing the capital.

Fails

Which screen rules that specific play violates, with the actual number vs the threshold. Dimmed rows are kept deliberately - near-misses show where each ladder is richest.

The 50% exit

Stage 4: buy back any short put at 50% of max profit - the first half of the premium decays fastest with the least risk. Secondary exits: underlying −8% vs entry, or 21 DTE remaining. Never hold to expiration chasing the last drop.

Paper book

Every wheel decision is tracked as a paper position first - real orders exist only behind a time-boxed operator arm switch that agents cannot touch.

Built by the dca-wheel-research system - agents research with cited sources; engine/ computes every verdict (DCAP scorer per the Jimenez & Vuolo Technical Plan, screens contract in prompts/SCREENS.md). Paper research only; nothing here is investment advice. Regenerate: python -m orchestrator.main publish.
